A `<p data-testid="item-form-readonly-note"><em>View only — your role cannot edit items.</em></p>` block renders above the form when `ro` is True. The "Stock in / Stock out / Adjust / Details / Manage units" action links remain visible (they're gated on `not item.archived_at`, not on role — Workshop has access to all of them).
  - `base.html`: extend the `nav-items` visibility condition from `("admin", "manager", "office")` to `("admin", "manager", "office", "workshop")`. The `aria-current` on `/admin/items*` paths still works.
- **Test coverage** (`tests/integration/test_items_routes.py` 16 new + `tests/integration/test_layout.py` 1 flipped + `tests/e2e/test_movements_e2e.py` 1 leg-set):
  - `TestRoleEnforcement` — flipped `test_workshop_get_list_is_403` → `test_workshop_get_list_is_200`; added `test_workshop_get_edit_form_is_200`, `test_workshop_get_new_form_is_403`, `test_workshop_update_is_403` (POST returns 403 + sku/name unchanged + no audit row), `test_workshop_archive_is_403`, `test_workshop_unarchive_is_403`. The existing `test_workshop_create_is_403` stays — POST `/admin/items` still 403s.
  - New `TestWorkshopReadOnlyView` class (10 tests): list shows "View" not "Edit" link for Workshop; list hides New CTA for Workshop; list hides archive/unarchive forms for Workshop; list shows "Edit" link for Office (sanity-check the link-label split); form inputs all carry `disabled` attribute (sampled across 10 test-ids); form has no `item-submit` test-id for Workshop; form renders the read-only note for Workshop; form title is "View {name}" for Workshop; form still shows the in/out/adjust/detail action links for Workshop; form disables custom-field inputs for Workshop; form for Manager keeps the submit button + omits the read-only note (sanity).
  - `test_layout.py`: flipped `test_workshop_nav_excludes_items_link` → `test_workshop_nav_includes_items_link` (asserts both `data-testid="nav-items"` and `href="/admin/items"` are present in the rendered nav for Workshop).
  - E2E walk extension: in `test_movements_e2e.py`'s existing single walk, after the Workshop user signs in (and before the deep-link to `/in`), Workshop now navigates via `nav-items`, asserts the New CTA is hidden, sees the manager-created item row, asserts the row shows `view-item` (not `edit-item`) and hides the archive button, clicks `view-item`, asserts the form renders with the read-only note + no submit + the `stock-in-link` still visible. Walk file's docstring updated to reflect the new posture.

**Item detail page (M6):** new `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/detail` route on `app/movements.py` for **Manager + Office + Workshop** (mirrors M2 / M3 / M4). Read-only page that consolidates the cost-engine outputs: item header (name + sku + archived flag), summary line (current_qty + open_value + reorder_threshold), action links (in / out / adjust / edit / manage units), open-layers table, paginated movement timeline with per-row layer breakdown for OUT and negative-adjust rows. No state-changing routes, no audit. First place in the codebase with proper pagination (`?page=N&page_size=20`).

- **Engine ownership maintained:** route never mutates `item.current_qty`, `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `movement.total_cost` — pure read.
- **Helpers** (added next to the existing `_recent_movements` etc.): `_PAGE_SIZE = 20`; `_open_layers(db, item_id)` returns layers with `qty_remaining > 0` ordered FIFO `(received_at ASC, id ASC)` as view-shaped dicts; `_count_movements(db, item_id)` returns the integer total (used by the paginator); `_movements_page(db, item_id, *, page, page_size)` returns a single page newest-first `(created_at DESC, id DESC)` as view-shaped dicts (mirrors `_recent_movements`'s shape so the timeline can reuse the layout); `_attach_directions_and_breakdown(db, movements)` mutates the page-of-movements list in place to add `direction` (`"+"`/`"-"`/`""`) and `breakdown` (list of consumption rows with layer received_at + qty_consumed + unit_cost_at_consumption) to each row; `_paginate(total, page, page_size)` returns the metadata dict (page / page_size / total / page_count / has_prev / has_next), clamping out-of-range pages to `[1, page_count]`; `_can_edit_item(user)` returns True for Manager + Office + Admin (matches I1b's items-edit role surface — drives the edit-link visibility).
- **Direction derivation**: a movement is positive if it has a row in `cost_layers` with `source_movement_id == movement.id` (i.e. IN or POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT created a layer); negative if it has rows in `cost_layer_consumptions` with `movement_id == movement.id` (i.e. OUT or negative-adjustment consumed layers). Both relationships are queried in batch over the page's movement ids — two extra queries per page (one for the layer-side ids, one for the consumption rows + their parent layers' received_at). For TRANSFER (M5) — which has neither — the defensive `else` branch leaves `direction = ""` and the row renders with no sign; will revisit when M5 lands.
- **Pagination clamping is silent**, not 400: a stale `?page=99` link lands on the last page rather than an error page. Friendlier UX for cached / shared URLs. Tests pin both clamping paths (`?page=99` → last; `?page=0` → first).
- **Archived item posture**: detail page renders for archived items (it's a read of *history*) but suppresses the in / out / adjust action-link block (`{% if not item.archived_at %}`) — same posture as `items_form.html`. The `data-testid="item-detail-archived"` marker tells the user the item is archived. Archived-different from M2 / M3 / M4 form routes, which 400 on archived (write paths must reject; read paths must allow).
- **Templates:** new `item_detail.html` with seven test-id surfaces — `item-detail-heading` / `item-detail-sku` / `item-current-qty` / `item-open-value` / `item-detail-threshold` / `item-detail-archived`; `cost-layers-table` with per-row `cost-layer-row` (`cost-layer-qty-remaining` / `cost-layer-unit-cost` / `cost-layer-source` per cell) plus empty state `cost-layers-empty`; `movements-timeline` with per-row `timeline-row` (`timeline-qty` carrying the directional sign concatenated with qty, `timeline-total-cost`); per-row `layer-breakdown` block (a follow-up `<tr>` containing a `<ul>` of `layer-breakdown-row` items showing qty_consumed × unit_cost_at_consumption + the layer's received_at) appearing only when `m.breakdown` is non-empty; pagination footer `pagination` with either `pagination-single-page` (when `page_count <= 1`) or `pagination-info` + conditional `pagination-prev` / `pagination-next` links carrying `?page=…` URLs. `items_list.html` gains a `data-testid="detail-link"` "Details" link in the per-row Actions cell; `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="detail-link"` "Details →" link in the same `{% if item %}` block as the existing in / out / adjust links (so the link is visible on every saved item, including archived ones — detail read works for archived).
- **Test coverage** (`tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py` 34 new + `tests/e2e/test_movements_e2e.py` extension):
  - Role enforcement (6): anon 401; pending 403; Workshop / Office / Manager / Admin all 200.
  - Rendering (5): unknown item 404; archived item still renders + shows archived flag + suppresses in / out / adjust links; item header (name / sku / current_qty / open_value / threshold) all present; action-link visibility for active item (in / out / adjust / edit-item-link); workshop sees in/out/adjust but not the edit-item-link.
  - Cost layers (5): empty state when no layers; single layer rendered with all columns; multi-layer with mixed sources (manual_in + positive_adjustment); fully-consumed layer (qty_remaining=0) excluded; layers ordered FIFO via order-of-appearance check.
  - Movements timeline (6): empty state when no movements; IN row direction `+` + `total_cost = 14.00`; OUT row direction `-` + total_cost `6.0000` (column-quantised scale 4 from M3's audit-shape wart); adjustment-increase direction `+`; adjustment-decrease direction `-`; ordering newest-first.
  - Layer breakdown (5): IN row has no breakdown; OUT row has 1 breakdown row; negative-adjustment has breakdown; positive-adjustment has none; multi-layer OUT shows 2 breakdown rows.
  - Pagination (5): empty state replaces the pagination; ≤ page_size renders single-page footer; > page_size renders 2 pages with prev/next nav (page 1 has next-no-prev with 20 rows; page 2 has prev-no-next with 1 row); out-of-range `?page=99` clamps to last; `?page=0` clamps to first.
  - Detail-link visibility (2): items list shows the link per row; items edit form shows the link in the action block.
  - E2E walk extension: after the M4 leg-2 (decrease, current_qty=75), workshop deep-links to `/detail`. Asserts: heading contains "Casting alloy", current_qty=75.0000, open_value contains "197.5" (= 55 × 2.50 + 20 × 3.00), 2 cost-layer rows, 4 timeline rows, 2 layer-breakdown blocks (one for OUT, one for adjust-decrease — IN and adjust-increase don't get one), pagination-single-page visible, in-link visible, edit-link absent (workshop role).

**Manual adjustment route (M4):** `app/movements.py` gains two more routes mounted under `/admin/items` for **Manager + Office + Workshop**: `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/adjust` renders an adjust form (qty + direction + unit_cost + reason + note) plus the same recent-movements + open-value summary used by stock-out; `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/adjust` validates, builds a `StockMovement(type=ADJUSTMENT)`, flushes, **dispatches on direction**: `increase` calls `record_receipt(..., source=CostLayerSource.POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT, received_at=now())` (creates a layer + bumps qty); `decrease` calls `consume_fifo(...)` (writes consumption rows + decrements qty). Either path writes a single `stock_movement.adjustment` audit row, commits, and 303s back with a flash. The decrease path inherits M3's atomic-on-raise insufficient-stock contract verbatim — `InsufficientStockError` caught, `db.rollback()` drops the orphan movement, item reloaded via `_get_item_or_404`, form re-rendered at HTTP 400 with `<p data-testid="stock-adjust-error">Not enough stock: requested {N}, only {M} available.</p>` block + qty / direction / unit_cost / reason / note all preserved.

- **Two new helpers** (added near the existing `_parse_*` helpers): `_parse_required_reason(raw) -> str` (strip; blank → 400 — distinct from M2 / M3 where reason is optional, because MISSION §3 requires variance attribution); `_parse_direction(raw) -> str` (must be `"increase"` or `"decrease"` — anything else 400). New `_render_stock_adjust_form` factors GET-and-error rendering symmetric with M3's `_render_stock_out_form` (the same parametric error path on the only data-dependent failure case in this route — same carve-out justification as M3).
- **Engine wiring**: route never touches `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `cost_layer_consumptions.*`, `movement.total_cost`, or `item.current_qty`. Increase: `record_receipt` is the single owner. Decrease: `consume_fifo` is the single owner. Caller pattern is identical to M2 / M3.
- **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.adjustment"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after`:
  - **Increase**: `{item_id, qty, direction="increase", unit_cost, total_cost, source="positive_adjustment", reason, note, received_at}`. Decimal scale on `total_cost` is the input scale (e.g. `"12.50"` for `5 × 2.50`) because `record_receipt` computes the product in memory before the column round-trip.
  - **Decrease**: `{item_id, qty, direction, total_cost, reason, note}`. **No `unit_cost` / `source` / `received_at`** (varies per layer; matches M3's stock-out audit shape). `total_cost` carries the column-quantised scale 4 (e.g. `"8.0000"`) because the engine reads `layer.unit_cost` back from `Numeric(14,4)` storage — same wart as M3's stock-out audit.
- **`unit_cost` field is rendered for both directions** but only parsed + required for increase. For decrease, garbage in the field is accepted (and ignored). The form's `<em>required for increases; ignored for decreases</em>` hint is the user-facing cue. Conservative: doesn't need HTMX swap (still no concrete second use case for HTMX).
- **Templates**: new `stock_adjust_form.html` mirroring `stock_out_form.html` with the addition of a `direction` `<select>` (`stock-adjust-direction-input` test-id; both `increase` and `decrease` options) and the always-rendered `unit_cost` input. Reason input is HTML-required + `*`-marked. Test-ids: `stock-adjust-form`, `stock-adjust-qty-input`, `stock-adjust-direction-input`, `stock-adjust-unit-cost-input`, `stock-adjust-reason-input`, `stock-adjust-note-input`, `stock-adjust-submit`, `stock-adjust-error`. `items_form.html` gains a third `data-testid="stock-adjust-link"` "Adjust →" link in the same `{% if not item.archived_at %}` block as in / out.
- **Test coverage** (`tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py` 45 new + `tests/e2e/test_movements_e2e.py` extension):
  - Role enforcement (10): anon GET / POST 401; pending 403; Workshop / Office / Manager / Admin all 200 GET / 303 POST. Increase POST tests don't need a seed; decrease POST tests in this matrix go through the increase path so a layer isn't needed.
  - Form rendering (5): inputs + CSRF + both directions + reason marked required; current_qty + open_value surfaced; recent-movements empty state; unknown 404; archived 400.
  - Validation matrix (16): qty blank / zero / negative / non-numeric (4); direction blank / invalid (2); reason blank / whitespace-only (2); unit_cost on increase blank / negative / non-numeric (3); zero unit_cost on increase allowed (1); unit_cost ignored on decrease (1); unknown 404 + archived 400 on POST (2); failed validation writes no audit (1).
  - Increase happy path (4): creates `StockMovement(type=ADJUSTMENT)` + `CostLayer(source=POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT)` + bumps current_qty; total_cost = qty × unit_cost (e.g. `60.00`); audit content with `direction=increase`, `unit_cost`, `source`, `received_at` present; whitespace strip on reason / note; flash includes `+qty`.
  - Decrease happy path (4): creates `StockMovement(type=ADJUSTMENT)` + `CostLayerConsumption` rows + decrements current_qty; total_cost is layer-weighted; audit content with `direction=decrease`, no unit_cost / source / received_at; blank-note-becomes-None; flash includes `-qty`.
  - Insufficient stock on decrease (3): atomic rollback (no movement / consumption / qty mutation; layer.qty_remaining unchanged); in-form error block + preserved inputs (incl. direction value); no audit on insufficient stock.
  - Multi-layer FIFO on decrease (1): two layers (4 @ 2.00 + 6 @ 3.00, distinct received_at), decrease 7 → consumes 4 from old + 3 from new; total_cost = 4×2 + 3×3 = 17.00; consumption rows ordered correctly; current_qty = 3.
  - Adjust link on edit form (2): visible for active item; hidden for archived.
  - E2E walk extension: after the existing OUT + insufficient-stock legs (current_qty=70 at that point), workshop deep-links to `/admin/items/{id}/adjust`. Leg 1 (increase): qty=20, unit_cost=3.00, reason="found extra" → flash with `+20`, current_qty=90, recent-movements row with reason + 20 + 60. Leg 2 (decrease): qty=15, reason="damaged batch" → flash with `-15`, current_qty=75, recent-movements row.

**Manual stock-out route (M3):** `app/movements.py` gains two more routes mounted under `/admin/items` for **Manager + Office + Workshop**: `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/out` renders a form (qty + reason + note — *no* unit_cost; consumption price is per-layer, not per-movement) plus the existing recent-movements table and a new "open value" summary line (sum across the item's open FIFO layers, computed via `open_value`); `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/out` validates, builds a `StockMovement(type=OUT)`, flushes for an id, calls `consume_fifo(db, item=..., qty=..., movement=...)`, writes a `stock_movement.out` audit row, commits, 303s back with a flash. The handler-level body mirrors `record_stock_in` line-for-line (different engine call + audit action + redirect), so the helper set introduced by M2 (`_parse_positive_decimal`, `_get_item_or_404`, `_reject_archived`, `_flash`, `_recent_movements`) is reused as-is. One new helper: `_render_stock_out_form` factors the GET-and-error rendering paths so the insufficient-stock 400 path doesn't fork.

- **Insufficient-stock path (the new shape choice in M3):** the engine raises `InsufficientStockError(item_id, requested, available)` *before* any mutation (atomic-on-raise tested in M1). The route catches the error, calls `db.rollback()` (drops the unflushed `StockMovement` we added before the engine call — the engine itself never mutated anything, so the rollback's only job is to discard the orphan movement row), reloads the item via `_get_item_or_404` (because the rolled-back transaction left the original `item` reference detached), and re-renders the form via `_render_stock_out_form` with HTTP 400, the user's qty / reason / note preserved in the inputs, and an `<p data-testid="stock-out-error" role="alert">` block carrying the message "Not enough stock: requested {N}, only {M} available." This is the **only** form in the app today that re-renders on validation 400 — every other settings form (suppliers / locations / taxonomy / field-defs / items / item-units / stock-in) lets a 400 land on FastAPI's bare exception page. The carve-out is justified because insufficient-stock is a *data-dependent* failure (changes minute-by-minute as other actors consume) where typing the form again is more friction than re-typing a typo. The other validation 400s (blank qty, negative qty, archived item, etc.) follow the existing convention — bare 400 — so this is one carve-out, not a new pattern. Self-critique flags the inconsistency for a future "in-form errors" sweep across all seven forms.
- **Engine wiring**: route never touches `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `cost_layer_consumptions.*`, `movement.total_cost`, or `item.current_qty` — those are M1's invariants and `consume_fifo` is the single owner. Caller pattern is the same as M2: `db.add(movement); db.flush(); consume_fifo(...); record_audit(...); db.commit()`. The route does not surface a backdated `created_at` UI in M3 — same gap as M2.
- **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.out"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after={item_id, qty, total_cost, reason, note}`. **No `unit_cost`** (varies per layer — the layer-weighted sum is captured in `total_cost`). **No `source`** (consumes don't carry one). The layer-level breakdown is queryable from `cost_layer_consumptions WHERE movement_id = ?` — also append-only-by-engine per M1's contract — so duplicating it in the audit row would re-encode information already durably stored. Decimals are stringified via `str(decimal)` to preserve the column-quantised representation through JSON (the engine reads `layer.unit_cost` back from `Numeric(14,4)`, so `total_cost = qty * unit_cost` carries scale 4 — see the test for `total_cost == "8.0000"` not `"8"`).
- **Templates**: new `stock_out_form.html` — same shape as `stock_in_form.html` but without the unit_cost input, with an "Open value" summary line (`data-testid="item-open-value"`), and with an `{% if error %}<p data-testid="stock-out-error" role="alert">` block above the form for the insufficient-stock case. Test-ids: `stock-out-form` / `stock-out-qty-input` / `stock-out-reason-input` / `stock-out-note-input` / `stock-out-submit` / `stock-out-error`. `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="stock-out-link"` "Stock out →" link alongside the existing "Stock in →" link, on the same conditions (item not archived; visible to whoever can see the edit form — Manager + Office today).
- **Test coverage** (`tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py` 37 new + `tests/e2e/test_movements_e2e.py` extension = 37 integration + 1 e2e leg-pair):
  - Role enforcement (10): anonymous GET / POST 401, pending 403, Workshop / Office / Manager / Admin all succeed (200 GET / 303 POST). Role POST tests seed a layer first via `_seed_layer` (a tiny helper that goes through `record_receipt` so the seed itself exercises the receipt path).
  - Form rendering (7): inputs + CSRF present; no `name="unit_cost"` in the body; current_qty + open_value surfaced; open_value=0 for an item with no layers; recent-movements empty state; recent-movements populated after a seed; unknown item 404; archived item 400.
  - Validation (7): blank / zero / negative / non-numeric qty 400; unknown item 404 on POST; archived item 400 on POST; failed validation writes no audit row.
  - Happy path (5): single-layer consume creates `StockMovement(type=OUT)` with the right total_cost (3 × 2.50 = 7.50 for the headline assertion), one `CostLayerConsumption` row pointing at the right layer / movement / qty / unit_cost_at_consumption, decremented `layer.qty_remaining`, decremented `item.current_qty`; whitespace strip on reason + note; blank reason + note → None; audit row keys + values (incl. `total_cost == "8.0000"`, no `unit_cost`, no `source`); flash includes item name.
  - Insufficient stock (4): consume more than open layers → 400 + form re-rendered + error block + qty / reason / note preserved + no movement / consumption / current_qty / qty_remaining mutation; consume from item with no layers at all → 400 + error block; no audit row written on insufficient stock; consume *exactly* the open balance succeeds (303, layer drained to qty_remaining=0 but kept).
  - Multi-layer FIFO (2): two layers with distinct received_at → consume 7 of (4, 6) takes 4 from old + 3 from new; total_cost = 4×2.00 + 3×3.00 = 17.00; old layer drained, new partially consumed; consumption rows ordered correctly. Recent-movements list correctly shows both the seed IN and the new OUT for the same item.
  - Stock-out link (2): edit form shows the "Stock out →" link for an active item; hides it for an archived item.
  - E2E walk extension: after the existing stock-in step, workshop deep-links to `/admin/items/{id}/out`, asserts current_qty=100.0000 + open_value contains "250", fills qty=30 / reason="Production run", submits; flash, bumped current_qty=70.0000, OUT row showing "Production run" + 30 + 75 (total_cost = 30 × 2.50). Then a second leg: fills qty=1000, submits, asserts the `stock-out-error` block contains "Not enough stock", inputs preserved, current_qty unchanged at 70.0000.

**Manual stock-in route (M2):** `app/movements.py` exposes two routes mounted under `/admin/items` for **Manager + Office + Workshop**: `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/in` renders a form (qty + unit_cost + reason + note) plus a recent-movements table (last 10, newest first); `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/in` validates, builds a `StockMovement(type=IN)`, flushes for an id, calls `record_receipt(...)` with `source=MANUAL_IN`, writes a `stock_movement.in` audit row, commits, and 303s back to the same form with a flash. Workshop's first positive-write surface — CSRF + role gating both server-enforced.

- **Validation** (all 400, *before* any DB write so failures are atomic): `qty` parses as a strictly-positive `Decimal` (blank, zero, negative, non-numeric all reject); `unit_cost` parses as a non-negative `Decimal` (blank, negative, non-numeric reject; zero allowed for sample/gifted stock); `reason` and `note` stripped, blank → `None`. Item must exist (404) and not be archived (400 — same posture as taxonomy/items "no new structure under archived").
- **Engine wiring**: route never touches `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `movement.total_cost`, or `item.current_qty` — those are M1's invariants and `record_receipt` is the single owner. Caller pattern is exactly what M1 documented: `db.add(movement); db.flush(); record_receipt(db, ..., movement=movement); record_audit(...); db.commit()`. The route does *not* set `received_at` to anything other than `now()` in M2 — backdated UI is deferred to PO5/M4 even though the engine supports it.
- **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.in"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after={item_id, qty, unit_cost, total_cost, source, reason, note, received_at}`. Decimals are stringified via `str(decimal)` to preserve precision through JSON (same convention as I2's custom-field audit shape). The cost layer and any future consumption rows are reconstructable from `cost_layers.source_movement_id` and `cost_layer_consumptions.movement_id`.
- **Templates**: new `stock_in_form.html` (page-shaped, not HTMX-partial — HTMX swap deferred until a second use case exists). Renders the form + a `data-testid="movements-table"` with one `data-testid="movement-row"` per recent movement (columns: when / type / qty / total_cost / actor / reason). Empty-state `data-testid="movements-empty"` when no movements yet. `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="stock-in-link"` "Stock in →" link visible to the edit form's audience (Manager + Office) when the item is not archived.
- **Test coverage** (`tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py` 38 + `tests/e2e/test_movements_e2e.py` 1 = 39 new):
  - Role enforcement (10): anonymous GET 401, anonymous POST 401, pending 403, every active role (Workshop, Office, Manager, Admin) succeeds on GET 200 and POST 303.
  - Form rendering (6): inputs + CSRF present; current_qty surfaced; recent-movements empty state; recent-movements list populated (asserts row count via `data-testid="movement-row"`); unknown item 404; archived item 400.
  - Validation matrix (12): blank/zero/negative/non-numeric qty 400; blank/negative/non-numeric unit_cost 400; zero unit_cost allowed; unknown item 404 on POST; archived item 400 on POST; failed validation writes no audit row.
  - Happy path (6): single receipt creates movement (with type=IN, item_id, qty, user_id, reason, note, total_cost, po_id=None, stock_take_id=None) + cost layer (with qty_received=qty_remaining=qty, unit_cost, source=MANUAL_IN, source_movement_id=movement.id) + bumps item.current_qty; whitespace-strip on reason + note; blank reason + note → None; audit row keys + values; flash message after redirect; decimal precision preserved on qty + unit_cost.
  - Multi-receipt (3): two consecutive receipts create two layers with the right unit_costs + accumulate qty; recent-movements newest-first ordering; sibling items don't show each other's movements.
  - Stock-in link (2): edit form shows the "Stock in →" link for an active item; hides it for an archived item.
  - E2E walk (1): pending workshop user is admin-promoted; pending manager user is admin-promoted (admin signs in twice — once per promotion since role + status are two POSTs); manager creates a category + item; workshop deep-links to `/admin/items/{id}/in`, asserts the empty-movements state + zero current_qty, fills qty=100 / unit_cost=2.50 / reason="First receipt", submits, asserts the flash + bumped current_qty=100.0000 + the new movement row showing the reason + qty + total_cost (250). Cleanup archives the item + the category.

**FIFO cost engine (M1):** `app/cost_engine.py` exposes three pure functions and one exception. The engine is the single owner of `item.current_qty`, `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, and `stock_movements.total_cost` going forward — every M2-M6 / PO5 / ST-series route must call into it rather than touching those columns directly.

- `record_receipt(db, *, item, qty, unit_cost, source, movement, received_at=None) -> CostLayer` — creates a layer with `qty_received=qty_remaining=qty`, increments `item.current_qty`, sets `movement.total_cost = qty * unit_cost`. Validates `qty > 0`, `unit_cost >= 0` (zero allowed for sample/gifted stock), and that `movement.id` is set (caller must `db.flush()` first).
- `consume_fifo(db, *, item, qty, movement) -> Decimal` — walks open layers in `(received_at ASC, id ASC)` order, creates one `CostLayerConsumption` per layer touched, decrements `qty_remaining`, accumulates total cost, decrements `item.current_qty`, sets `movement.total_cost`. Atomic — raises `InsufficientStockError(item_id, requested, available)` *before any mutation* if open layers don't sum to `qty`. Validates `qty > 0` and flushed movement.
- `open_value(db, item) -> Decimal` — read-only sum of `qty_remaining * unit_cost` across the item's layers with `qty_remaining > 0`. Returns `Decimal("0")` for an item with no open layers.

**Schema (migration `0010_create_cost_layers_and_movements.py`):** three new tables, in dependency order (`stock_movements` first so the cost tables can FK into it).

- `stock_movements`: `id, item_id (FK RESTRICT, NOT NULL), item_unit_id (FK RESTRICT nullable), type (String(16), SAEnum-backed: in/out/adjustment/transfer), qty (Numeric(14,4)), user_id (FK SET NULL nullable), reason (String(255) nullable), note (String(2000) nullable), po_id (Integer nullable, NO FK in M1), stock_take_id (Integer nullable, NO FK in M1), total_cost (Numeric(14,4) nullable — engine sets it), created_at`. Five indexes: per-item, per-item_unit, per-user, per-type, per-created_at. `po_id` and `stock_take_id` are plain integers (no FK) because the `purchase_orders` and `stock_takes` tables don't exist yet — the FK constraint is added in PO2 / ST1's migration.
- `cost_layers`: `id, item_id (FK RESTRICT, NOT NULL), qty_received (Numeric(14,4) NOT NULL — immutable post-insert), qty_remaining (Numeric(14,4) NOT NULL — only the engine decrements this), unit_cost (Numeric(14,4) NOT NULL — immutable), received_at (DateTime(tz) NOT NULL — caller-supplied or now()), source (String(20), SAEnum-backed: po_receipt/manual_in/positive_adjustment), source_movement_id (FK stock_movements.id RESTRICT NOT NULL), created_at`. Three indexes: per-item, per-source_movement, plus the composite `ix_cost_layers_item_received` on `(item_id, received_at, id)` that covers the FIFO ORDER BY.
- `cost_layer_consumptions`: `id, layer_id (FK RESTRICT NOT NULL), movement_id (FK RESTRICT NOT NULL), qty_consumed (Numeric(14,4) NOT NULL), unit_cost_at_consumption (Numeric(14,4) NOT NULL — snapshot of the layer's unit_cost at the time of the consume), created_at`. Three indexes: per-layer, per-movement, plus a composite `ix_cost_layer_consumptions_movement_layer` so an item-detail page (M6) can fetch a movement's full breakdown in one query.

No `archived_at` on any of the three. The engine maintains six invariants (documented in `app/cost_engine.py`'s module docstring): receipts only INSERT layers and INCREMENT qty; consumes only INSERT consumptions, DECREMENT `qty_remaining`, and DECREMENT `current_qty`; `qty_received` / `unit_cost` / `received_at` / `source` are immutable; nothing is ever DELETEd; `movement.total_cost` is set exactly once per engine call; FIFO order is `(received_at ASC, id ASC)`.

**Two new enums** in `app/models.py`: `MovementType(IN, OUT, ADJUSTMENT, TRANSFER)` and `CostLayerSource(PO_RECEIPT, MANUAL_IN, POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT)`. Both stored as `String(16)` / `String(20)` via SAEnum + `values_callable`, matching the existing `Role` / `UserStatus` / `TrackingMode` / `ItemUnitStatus` / `FieldType` convention.

**Test coverage** (`tests/unit/test_cost_models.py` 15 tests + `tests/unit/test_cost_engine.py` 29 tests = 44 new):

- Models (15): minimal-defaults round-trip per table, every enum value round-trips, FK NOT NULL violations raise IntegrityError, `qty_remaining` decrement does not affect `qty_received`, `po_id`/`stock_take_id` accept arbitrary integers (no FK constraint), `total_cost` round-trips Numeric(14,4), two consumptions can share a movement (multi-layer consume case).
- Engine — receipt (9): correct layer fields, `current_qty` increment, `movement.total_cost` set, `received_at=None` defaults to now (within ±1s), explicit `received_at` honoured, `qty <= 0` raises ValueError, negative `unit_cost` raises, zero `unit_cost` allowed, unflushed movement raises ValueError.
- Engine — consume single-layer (2): partial consume creates one consumption row + decrements layer + sets total_cost; exact consume drains layer to 0 (layer kept, not deleted).
- Engine — consume multi-layer (3): two layers spanned in FIFO order with mixed unit costs, exhausted layer skipped (no consumption row written for it), id-tiebreak when received_at is identical (uses `freezegun` to force the timestamp tie).
- Engine — errors (6): `InsufficientStockError` carries `item_id`/`requested`/`available`; over-consume is atomic (no layers / qty / consumption rows mutated, `total_cost` stays None); empty-layer-set raises; zero qty raises; negative qty raises; unflushed movement raises.
- Engine — isolation (1): item A's layers are not consumed when item B's consume runs (and fails).
- Engine — `open_value` (6): empty returns 0, single layer, multi-layer sum across mixed sources, exhausted layer excluded, partial layer included, isolation between items.
- Engine — combined (2): full receipt → consume → receipt → consume cycle verifies the running `current_qty` net + `open_value` after each step; positive adjustment → negative adjustment cycle exercises the same paths through the adjustment movement type.

**What's running (route surface unchanged from I3a):** **Manager + Admin** can fully manage items via `/admin/items` (including custom field values inherited from the leaf node's schema) and per-unit rows for unique-tracked items via `/admin/items/{id}/units`. **Office** can list + edit items + units (cannot create/archive either, cannot change reorder thresholds — MISSION §3). **Workshop** still 403s on every items / units endpoint (read-only access deferred). 14 URL patterns total (7 items + 7 units, all CSRF-protected). M1 is pre-route — the cost engine is callable but no HTTP handler invokes it yet; that's M2's job. Items routes:

- `GET  /admin/items?[show=active|archived&node_id=N]` — Manager + Office. List with active/archived tabs, optional filter by taxonomy node.
- `GET  /admin/items/new[?node_id=N]` — Manager-only. Form, pre-selects category from query string when supplied.
- `POST /admin/items` — Manager-only. Create.
- `GET  /admin/items/{item_id}/edit` — Manager + Office. Form, with archived-FK preservation (see below).
- `POST /admin/items/{item_id}` — Manager + Office. Update (sparse audit diff). Office's threshold submissions are silently overridden with the existing row's values *before* validation, so no audit diff for Office can ever include `reorder_threshold` / `reorder_qty`.
- `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/archive` + `/unarchive` — Manager-only. Idempotent; no audit row on no-op.

**Unit routes** (I3, all under `/admin/items/...` but in a separate router module `app/item_units.py`):

- `GET  /admin/items/{item_id}/units?[show=active|archived]` — Manager + Office. List per-item units; renders a "qty-tracked" note + hidden CTA when the parent item is qty-tracked, an "archived" note when the parent is archived.
- `GET  /admin/items/{item_id}/units/new` — Manager-only. Form. 400 if item is qty-tracked or archived (no new structure under archived parents — same posture as taxonomy sub-cats / field defs).
- `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/units` — Manager-only. Create. Same archived-parent + qty-tracked guards.
- `GET  /admin/items/units/{unit_id}/edit` — Manager + Office. Flat-by-id form (parent in URL would just confuse callers; unit's `item_id` is the source of truth).
- `POST /admin/items/units/{unit_id}` — Manager + Office. Update (sparse audit diff: only changed fields appear in `before/after_json`).
- `POST /admin/items/units/{unit_id}/{archive,unarchive}` — Manager-only. Idempotent. **Archiving a unit under an archived parent item is allowed** (cleanup workflow, mirrors S4's archived-parent stance).

**Threshold gating** (MISSION §3 "Office cannot change reorder thresholds"): handled in `_can_edit_thresholds(user) -> bool`, which returns True for Manager + Admin only. Used in both `update_item` (overrides inbound form values for Office) and `edit_item_form` (drives the template's `can_edit_thresholds` flag → hides inputs, shows read-only display values mirroring the `current_qty` pattern). Silent override (rather than 400) was chosen for consistency with `current_qty` and because it's how an HTML form's hidden field would behave for a non-tampered client.

**Archived-FK preservation**: `_resolve_optional_supplier`, `_resolve_optional_location`, `_resolve_leaf_node` each take a `current_id` kwarg. Contract: blank → None (clears the link, even if the previous value was archived — explicit user choice); a non-blank archived id is accepted *only if* it matches `current_id`; switching to a *different* archived id still 400s. The form-side helpers `_supplier_options`, `_location_options`, `_leaf_options` likewise take `current_id` and append the assigned-but-archived row to the dropdown with an "(archived)" suffix (and for sub-cat leaves, "Parent / Sub (archived)"). Archived rows that aren't the current assignment are *not* listed — the dropdown doesn't become a graveyard; only the active set + the row the item is pinned to.

**Leaf invariant** (reused from S5): a top-level node with no active sub-categories is a leaf; a sub-category is always a leaf. `_is_leaf(db, node)` is duplicated between `app/items.py` and `app/field_defs.py` — two callers, one-line predicate, both must move in lockstep if the leaf rule changes. Acceptable today; flagged for extraction once a third caller arrives.

**Validations** (all return 400 on failure unless noted):
- `sku` / `name` / `unit` non-empty after strip; `sku` unique across active+archived rows (same convention as Supplier names).
- `taxonomy_node_id` must reference an existing, non-archived leaf node — non-leaf still 400; archived rejected *unless* it matches the row's existing `taxonomy_node_id` (preserve-unchanged contract).
- `tracking_mode` parses to `TrackingMode` enum (`qty`, `unique`).
- `reorder_threshold` / `reorder_qty` parse as non-negative `Decimal`; blank → 0. Office's submissions are silently overridden with the existing values before this parser runs (so Office can't even fail validation on these fields).
- Optional `supplier_id` / `location_id`: blank → None (clears even if the previous was archived — explicit user action); if set, must reference an active row *or* match the existing assignment if archived.
- `qr_code` blank → None; otherwise unique across all rows where set (partial unique index).
- `current_qty` is **not** writable through the route — pinned at 0 on create; preserved unchanged on edit. Stock movements (M1+) own this column.

**Audit vocabulary**: `item.created`, `item.updated`, `item.archived`, `item.unarchived`. Created `after_json` carries the full set of stored fields (incl. current_qty for completeness) plus a `custom_fields` dict (key → audit-friendly value) when any custom values were set. Updates use the `_diff` sparse-changed-fields pattern; custom-field changes appear under a `custom_fields` sub-dict that *only* contains keys whose value changed (sparse: `before["custom_fields"]["alloy"] = "silver"` / `after["custom_fields"]["alloy"] = "gold"`, with no entries for unchanged fields). Office actors *cannot* produce a diff that includes `reorder_threshold` / `reorder_qty` (those are inert before the diff runs).

**Custom field values (I2)**: items inherit the **active** field defs of their leaf node. Values are stored sparsely in `item_field_values` (one row per `(item_id, field_def_id)` with a non-null value; the column is chosen by the def's type — `value_text` / `value_number` / `value_decimal` / `value_date` / `value_bool` / `value_json` for multiselect, with select also using `value_text` for the chosen option). Boolean rows persist both `True` and `False` (a checkbox always has a definite answer); every other type skips the row when the parsed value is blank/empty. Required-flag enforcement: text/number/decimal/date/select/multiselect must be non-blank/non-empty; **boolean must be `True`** (an unchecked required boolean = "must confirm" pattern, not a missing answer — documented in `app/items.py`'s module docstring). Audit values: decimals → str (preserves precision), dates → ISO, lists pass through, primitives pass through. Archived field defs are intentionally invisible to the form — existing values stay on the item ("Deleting a field hides it from new entry but preserves the value in audit history", MISSION §3); the diff-and-apply path only iterates *active* defs, so archived rows never get touched. Required-flag, type-coercion, and out-of-options select / multiselect violations all `raise HTTPException(400)` and short-circuit before any item write — atomic.

**Migration:** `migrations/versions/0008_create_item_field_values.py` adds the `item_field_values` table + 3 indexes (`uq_item_field_values_item_field_def` UNIQUE on `(item_id, field_def_id)`; `ix_item_field_values_item_id`; `ix_item_field_values_field_def_id`). The unique index makes the "one row per (item, field def)" invariant a storage-layer guarantee, not just a route convention.

**Templates (I2 updates):**
- `items_form.html` — Adds a `{% if field_defs %}` fieldset after the core fields. One `<input>`/`<select>`/`<textarea>` per active def, dispatched by type: text → text input; number → `<input type="number">`; decimal → text+`inputmode="decimal"`; date → `<input type="date">`; boolean → checkbox; select → `<select>` with `— pick one —` blank option; multiselect → `<select multiple>`. Form names are `cf_<key>`; pre-fill from `form.custom[key]`. Required marker is the same `<span aria-hidden="true">*</span>` pattern as the core fields. Test-ids: `item-cf-<key>-input` per input + `item-custom-fields` on the fieldset + `item-cf-row` per row (with `data-cf-key="<key>"`).
- (I1b updates carried forward unchanged: `items_list.html` and `base.html` role gates.)

**Known broken:** none. **Resolved warts:**
- ~~Archived-FK silent data loss on item edit~~ (I1a → I1b)
- ~~Items don't inherit field schema~~ (S5 → I2)

OAUTH2 is the cheaper intermediate step that closes 4 route-level coverage gaps in <100 LoC of pure tests-only addition. <!-- OAUTH1 plan (now shipped — preserved for slice context).

**OAUTH1 — Error-path coverage in `google_callback` + `google_login` smoke tests (DoD #1 progress step; MISSION §3 "Auth and roles — Google SSO only"):** smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #1 closer. The unticked half of DoD #1 is "a new user can sign in with Google → pending → Admin assigns role → access works", verified by tests AND a manual sanity-check the loop can do without a human. Existing coverage at iter-77 HEAD:

- `tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py::TestGoogleCallback` (2 happy-path tests with mocked `authorize_access_token`): create-pending-user + existing-user name update.
- `TestDevLoginAndCallbackParity` (3 forcing-function tests): `_dev-login` and `google_callback` both go through `upsert_user_from_userinfo`, produce equivalent user rows + audit rows + session state.
- `tests/e2e/test_admin_users_e2e.py::test_admin_promotes_pending_user_who_then_signs_in` (1 walk via `_dev-login`): pending → admin promotes → user signs back in → welcome page visible.

Gaps OAUTH1 closes: (1) `google_login` route had zero test coverage; (2) `google_callback` had zero coverage for its 4 unhappy branches: `OAuthError` → 400, missing-or-incomplete-userinfo → 400, userinfo-fetched-separately path (`google.userinfo()`), and the 503 "Google not configured" path. Without these tests, a regression in any unhappy path could ship undetected because the happy-path tests don't exercise them. OAUTH1 fills (1) + (2). Pure tests-only addition.

- **Scope** (`tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py` only — extension; no `app/` change; no new modules; no new helpers; mypy source-file count stays at 29):
  - **New `TestGoogleLogin` class** (3 tests):
    - `test_login_redirects_to_google_authz_endpoint` — monkeypatch `google.authorize_redirect` to return a `RedirectResponse(url=stub_url, status_code=307)`; assert the route returns 307 with the stubbed Location.
    - `test_login_returns_503_when_google_not_configured` — monkeypatch `oauth.create_client` to return `None`; assert 503 + detail contains `"not configured"`.
    - `test_login_passes_callback_redirect_uri_to_authlib` — capture the `redirect_uri` arg passed to `authorize_redirect`; assert it equals `f"{settings.app_base_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/google/callback"`.
  - **Extend `TestGoogleCallback` class** (5 new unhappy-path tests):
    - `test_callback_returns_400_on_oauth_error` — monkeypatch `authorize_access_token` to raise `OAuthError(error="invalid_state", description="state mismatch")`; assert 400 + body contains `"OAuth error"` + `"invalid_state"`.
    - `test_callback_returns_400_when_userinfo_missing_email` — token returns `{"userinfo": {"sub": "g-no-email"}}` (no email); assert 400 + body contains `"missing user identity"`.
    - `test_callback_returns_400_when_userinfo_missing_sub` — token returns `{"userinfo": {"email": "no-sub@example.com"}}` (no sub); assert 400 + body contains `"missing user identity"`.
    - `test_callback_fetches_userinfo_separately_when_token_omits_it` — token returns `{}` (no `userinfo` key); mock `google.userinfo` to return `{"sub": "g-sep-1", "email": "separate@example.com", "name": "Separate Userinfo"}`; assert 303 + user created with PENDING status + `google.userinfo` was awaited once.
    - `test_callback_returns_503_when_google_not_configured` — monkeypatch `oauth.create_client` to return `None`; assert 503 + detail contains `"not configured"`.

- **Mocking strategy**: same `monkeypatch.setattr(auth_module.oauth, "create_client", lambda _name: google_mock)` pattern as the existing tests. For `userinfo`-fetched-separately, the `google_mock` carries both `authorize_access_token` AND `userinfo` as `AsyncMock`s. For the 503 path, the lambda returns `None` directly (mirrors `oauth.create_client("google")` returning `None` when no client was registered).

- **Why no Playwright walk added**: the existing `_dev-login`-based Playwright walk in `test_admin_users_e2e.py` already covers the post-OAuth flow. Adding a Playwright walk for `google_login`'s redirect would either need to drive real Google (impossible without a tenant) OR a server-side OAuth stub (the queued OAuth-stub slice). OAUTH1 is the in-process forcing function that pins the route handlers' behaviour against deterministic mocks; the OAuth-stub slice is the additional layer that exercises the routes through a real browser.

- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `app/auth.py:184-195` (google_login: `oauth.create_client("google")` → 503 if None; `authorize_redirect(request, redirect_uri)` returns RedirectResponse); `app/auth.py:199-233` (google_callback: 503 if no client → `authorize_access_token` (OAuthError → 400) → `userinfo` from token OR `google.userinfo(token=token)` → 400 if missing sub/email → `upsert_user_from_userinfo` → 303 to `/`); `app/auth.py:191` (`redirect_uri = f"{settings.app_base_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/google/callback"`); existing `TestGoogleCallback` at `tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py:222` for the mock pattern.

- **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they drive the actual route handlers with TestClient + monkeypatched OAuth client + assert every observable behaviour. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`.

- **DoD impact**: **No DoD ticked.** DoD #1 needs (a) happy-path mock test ✓ + (b) `_dev-login ↔ google_callback` parity ✓ + (c) admin-promotes-pending e2e walk ✓ + (d) **error-path coverage on `google_callback`** ✓ (this slice) + (e) **`google_login` route coverage** ✓ (this slice) + (f) OAuth-stub server-side Playwright walk (queued). After OAUTH1, only (f) remains.

<!-- CONFTEST2 plan (now shipped — preserved for slice context).

**CONFTEST2 — Per-test isolation via SAVEPOINT for non-SQLite backends (DoD #11 progress step; MISSION §5 "SQLite for local/test, Postgres for cloud. The data layer must work with both via SQLAlchemy."):** smallest end-to-end slice that continues CONFTEST1's DoD #11 progress. With CONFTEST1's `TEST_DATABASE_URL` override + dialect-aware engine helper landed, the next gap is per-test isolation when the override points at a shared backend (Postgres). Currently `db_session` creates a fresh engine each test, which gives automatic isolation on in-memory SQLite (each engine = isolated DB) but on a shared Postgres backend every test would step on every other test's row data (schema is idempotent via `Base.metadata.create_all`, but rows persist). This slice adds the SQLAlchemy 2.0 `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` pattern: each test's session lives inside an outer transaction on a single connection, with the savepoint pattern intercepting `session.commit()`/`session.rollback()` so they never escape the outer scope; teardown rolls back the outer transaction. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #11 also needs (c) Postgres CI job (gated on a remote being configured + a CI workflow YAML — `git remote -v` shows nothing). After CONFTEST2 the test conftest-side work for DoD #11 is complete; only CI infra remains.

- **Scope** (`tests/conftest.py` refactor + new `tests/integration/test_conftest_isolation.py` only — no `app/` change; no new migrations; no new modules outside `tests/`):
  - **Add `_make_isolated_session(engine: Engine)` helper** to `tests/conftest.py`. Builds a connection-bound session with `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` (SQLAlchemy 2.0+ idiom). Pattern (approx):
    ```python
    @contextmanager
    def _make_isolated_session(engine: Engine) -> Iterator[Session]:
        Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
        connection = engine.connect()
        transaction = connection.begin()
        SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=connection, autoflush=False, autocommit=False, future=True, join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint")
        try:
            with SessionLocal() as session:
                yield session
        finally:
            transaction.rollback()
            connection.close()
    ```
    `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` is the SQLAlchemy 2.0 way to handle "the test session is inside an outer transaction; route handlers' `session.commit()` should NOT escape the outer scope" — much cleaner than the older `event.listens_for(session, "after_transaction_end")` pattern. SQLAlchemy 2.0.49 is in `pyproject.toml`; the parameter is documented at https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/orm/session_basics.html#joining-a-session-into-an-external-transaction-such-as-for-test-suites.
  - **Modify `db_session` fixture** to dispatch on URL prefix:
    - SQLite URL (the default — covers in-memory + file URLs) → existing path (per-test engine = per-test DB; isolation is automatic, savepoint pattern is unnecessary overhead).
    - Non-SQLite URL → `_make_isolated_session(engine)` for shared-backend isolation.
    - The dispatch keeps the existing 2502-test default behaviour unchanged (zero risk of breaking SQLite-side tests).
  - **Modify `client` fixture** to thread the same dispatch: when `db_session` is bound to a connection (savepoint mode), `OverrideSession` binds to the same connection with `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` so the route-handler-side session joins the same outer transaction. When `db_session` is bound to an engine (SQLite default), `OverrideSession` binds to that engine — existing behaviour. The bind-introspection trick: `db_session.get_bind()` returns the connection in savepoint mode and the engine in SQLite mode; the fixture inspects the type with `isinstance(bind, Connection)` to decide.

- **Tests** (~9 new in `tests/integration/test_conftest_isolation.py`):
  - `TestMakeIsolatedSession` (5):
    - `test_yields_writable_session_against_sqlite_file` — opens a SQLite file engine + `_make_isolated_session(engine)` + writes a `User` row + asserts visible via `session.query(User).first()`. The savepoint pattern works on any dialect (SQLite supports SAVEPOINT) so SQLite-file is a valid surrogate for Postgres-side validation without needing Postgres infra.
    - `test_commit_does_not_escape_outer_transaction` — writes a row + `session.commit()`s + exits the helper context manager + opens a fresh engine on the same SQLite file URL + asserts the row is gone (the outer transaction was rolled back at teardown, so the commit's effect was confined to the savepoint and discarded with the outer rollback).
    - `test_rollback_inside_helper_preserves_outer_transaction` — writes a row + `session.rollback()`s + writes a different row + `session.commit()`s + exits the helper + opens a fresh engine + asserts the second row is also gone (rollback popped the savepoint and rolled back to it; the outer rollback at teardown then discarded everything).
    - `test_integrity_error_recovery` — tries to insert a duplicate-key row (after seeding a row + commit-savepoint) + catches the `IntegrityError` + calls `session.rollback()` + asserts the session is usable for further writes (mirrors the `test_top_level_name_is_unique`-style pattern that ~30 unit tests use). Forces the savepoint pattern to handle the "broken session after IntegrityError" case correctly.
    - `test_uses_savepoint_mode` — pins that the helper passes `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` to `sessionmaker` (source-text inspection via `inspect.getsource(_make_isolated_session)` substring check). Forcing function so a future PR that drops the parameter (which would silently break isolation) fails the suite.
  - `TestDbSessionDispatch` (3):
    - `test_db_session_uses_sqlite_path_by_default` — under the default `TEST_DATABASE_URL` (`sqlite:///:memory:`), the fixture's engine has `dialect.name == "sqlite"` AND the bind returned by `session.get_bind()` is an `Engine` (not a `Connection`). Pins existing behaviour preserved.
    - `test_db_session_uses_savepoint_path_for_non_sqlite_url` — monkeypatches `TEST_DATABASE_URL` to a SQLite-file URL (forces the savepoint branch even though the dialect is SQLite — the dispatch should be on URL prefix, not dialect; this isolates the dispatch logic from the dialect). Verifies the bind is a `Connection`. (Note: the current dispatch is on URL `startswith("sqlite")` — this test would FAIL the way I've described it. I'll either flip the dispatch to a different boundary, e.g. an opt-in env var, or rephrase the test to use a `postgresql+psycopg://` URL and assert the connection-binding via lazy engine inspection.)
    - **Resolution**: the cleanest dispatch is on URL prefix (`sqlite` → existing, else → savepoint). The test for the savepoint branch will set `TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg:///nonexistent_db_for_dispatch_check` and assert that `_make_test_engine` returns a Postgres-dialect engine (lazy — no connection). The savepoint branch isn't actually exercised at the fixture level without a real Postgres, but `_make_isolated_session` is exercised directly by `TestMakeIsolatedSession` with a SQLite-file engine. The dispatch logic itself is pinned via source-text inspection (`inspect.getsource(db_session)` contains `if url.startswith("sqlite")` substring or equivalent).
  - `TestClientFixtureDispatch` (1):
    - `test_client_fixture_still_works_with_default_sqlite` — sanity check: the existing `client` fixture continues to work for the default SQLite case (covers thousands of existing integration tests by proxy). Drives a simple `client.get("/healthz")` or equivalent unauthenticated route + asserts 200.

- **Why opt-in dispatch on URL, not unified savepoint pattern for everyone**: the savepoint pattern works on SQLite (savepoints are supported), but it adds a per-test connection-open + outer-transaction overhead that the existing per-test-engine pattern doesn't have. For 2502 tests on SQLite, the unified pattern would slow the suite measurably. The dispatch keeps SQLite fast + correct + Postgres-isolation correct. Both code paths are exercised by tests.

- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source**: `tests/conftest.py:69-103` is the existing `db_session` + `client` fixtures (after CONFTEST1); `pyproject.toml:17` is `sqlalchemy>=2.0` (SQLAlchemy 2.0.49 installed per `uv run python -c "import sqlalchemy; print(sqlalchemy.__version__)"`); `Session.__init__` accepts `join_transaction_mode` parameter (added in SQLAlchemy 2.0 — verified by reading the SQLAlchemy docs link above); ~9 unit-test files have a *local* `db` fixture (`test_taxonomy.py:24`, `test_field_defs.py:28`, etc.) that hardcode their own `create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")` — those are independent of `db_session` and are unaffected by CONFTEST2. The ~30 `db.rollback()` calls in unit tests are local to those files' fixtures — also unaffected.

- **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they import `_make_isolated_session` from `tests.conftest` directly + drive every code path with SQLite-file engines. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`.

- **Risks**:
  - **`join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` syntax**: SQLAlchemy 2.0+ only. Confirmed available in 2.0.49.
  - **`IntegrityError` recovery**: the savepoint pattern handles this correctly per SQLAlchemy docs — the `IntegrityError` rolls back the inner savepoint, leaving the session in a "needs `rollback()` call" state; calling `session.rollback()` rolls back to the outer savepoint, allowing further writes. Pinned by `test_integrity_error_recovery`.
  - **Schema persistence on shared Postgres**: `Base.metadata.create_all(engine)` runs on every test (fast — checks `information_schema` for existing tables and skips them). Acceptable overhead for v1.
  - **`Connection` vs `Engine` bind dispatch in `client` fixture**: the bind-type introspection via `isinstance(bind, Connection)` is a clean dispatcher. SQLAlchemy's `Session.get_bind()` returns whatever was passed to the sessionmaker — either an Engine (SQLite path) or a Connection (savepoint path).

- **DoD impact**: **No DoD ticked.** DoD #11 needs (a) conftest URL/dialect switch (CONFTEST1 ✓) + (b) per-test isolation on shared backends (this slice) + (c) Postgres CI job (gated on remote infra). CONFTEST2 closes (b). After CONFTEST2 a developer can run `createdb test_uc && TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg:///test_uc make test` and the suite would isolate per-test correctly (the schema-create cost the only per-test wasted work on Postgres). The CI infra leg (c) lands when a remote is configured.

-->

*(Previous Current slice — CONFTEST1 shipped iteration 75 — moved to Completed slices log row I75. Plan content + cross-checks preserved below in commented form for slice context.)*

<!-- CONFTEST1 plan (now shipped — preserved for slice context).

**CONFTEST1 — Test conftest reads `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var with dialect-aware engine setup (DoD #11 progress step; MISSION §5 "SQLite for local/test, Postgres for cloud. The data layer must work with both via SQLAlchemy."):** smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #11 closer. The unticked half of DoD #11 is "runs in cloud config on Postgres with no code changes (env vars only)". The app side already has dialect-aware engine kwargs (`app/db.py::_engine_kwargs` at `:9-12` branches on `url.startswith("sqlite")`) and dialect-aware audit-log immutability triggers (`app/audit.py::apply_immutability_triggers` branches on `connection.dialect.name`). The remaining unticked half is the test conftest, which currently hardcodes `"sqlite:///:memory:"` + `StaticPool` + `check_same_thread=False` regardless of what `DATABASE_URL` says. CONFTEST1 introduces `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var override + a dialect-aware test-engine helper. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #11 also needs CI-side wiring + per-test isolation strategy for shared Postgres databases (transaction-rollback fixtures or schema-per-test); those are follow-up slices.

- **Scope** (`tests/conftest.py` refactor + new `tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py` only — no `app/` change; no new migrations; no new modules outside `tests/`):
  - **Refactor `tests/conftest.py`**: extract two module-level helpers — `_resolve_test_database_url()` (reads `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var; falls back to `"sqlite:///:memory:"`) and `_make_test_engine(url)` (returns a SQLAlchemy `Engine` with the right pool / connect_args based on URL prefix). The `db_session` fixture calls these helpers instead of inlining `create_engine(...)`. Existing behaviour preserved when env var is unset (every existing test continues to use SQLite in-memory + StaticPool).
  - **Pool strategy**:
    - SQLite (memory or file): `StaticPool` + `connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}` — required so the in-memory DB is visible across threads (TestClient dispatches on a worker thread).
    - Non-SQLite (Postgres / others): SQLAlchemy default pool (`QueuePool`), no special connect_args.
  - **Isolation note (deferred)**: Postgres URLs share a single backing database across every test, which would break test isolation as written (each test's `Base.metadata.create_all` is idempotent on the schema, but data persists). The conftest switch is necessary but not sufficient for full Postgres test runs — a follow-up slice (CONFTEST2) needs to add transaction-rollback per-test or schema-per-test isolation. CONFTEST1 documents this gap explicitly and adds it to the rolling self-critique. Right now the switch lets a developer run the suite against a fresh Postgres for a smoke test (e.g. `createdb test_uc && TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql:///test_uc make test`) but isolation between tests requires the follow-up.
  - **Decoupled from app-side `DATABASE_URL`**: the existing line `os.environ.setdefault("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///:memory:")` continues to drive the app config (via `app/config.py::Settings.database_url`); `TEST_DATABASE_URL` drives the test fixture's engine. Test isolation works because `db_session` fixture creates its own engine and the `client` fixture's `app.dependency_overrides[get_session]` rebinds to that engine. So a developer can set `TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...` to point the *fixture* at Postgres without changing `DATABASE_URL`. (For perfect parity later, both vars can be set together.)

- **Tests** (~10 new in `tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py`):
  - `TestResolveTestDatabaseUrl` (3): default returns sqlite memory URL when env unset; respects `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var when set; passes through arbitrary URL string verbatim.
  - `TestMakeTestEngine` (5): sqlite memory URL → engine has dialect `"sqlite"`; sqlite memory URL → pool is `StaticPool`; sqlite file URL → also gets StaticPool; postgres URL (lazy, no connect) → dialect is `"postgresql"`; postgres URL → pool is NOT StaticPool (default QueuePool).
  - `TestDbSessionFixtureStillWorks` (2): the existing `db_session` fixture yields a session that can write + read a model row (sanity check that the refactor preserves behaviour); fixture's bound engine has `dialect.name == "sqlite"` for the default env config (forces a future PR that breaks the default to fail visibly).

- **Why this shape, not a full Postgres CI job**: Postgres CI requires GitHub Actions infrastructure that isn't yet wired up (no `.github/workflows/`, no remote configured per `git remote -v`). Isolation between tests on shared Postgres requires per-test transaction rollback or schema-per-test, which is a separate concern from the conftest switch. CONFTEST1 is the smallest forcing-function step that demonstrates the switch is mechanical + lets a developer point `TEST_DATABASE_URL` at a fresh Postgres for a smoke test today.

- **No `app/` change.** Pure tests-side refactor + tests-only addition. mypy source-file count stays at 29 (mypy strict only on `app/`).

- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `tests/conftest.py:36-41` is the existing `create_engine` call with hardcoded sqlite memory URL + StaticPool + check_same_thread; `app/db.py:9-15` has the parallel `_engine_kwargs` helper that the test conftest will mirror; `app/config.py:21` defaults `database_url` to `"sqlite:///./dev.db"` (not in-memory — only the test conftest forces in-memory via `os.environ.setdefault("DATABASE_URL", ...)` at `:16`); `app/audit.py::apply_immutability_triggers` already branches on `connection.dialect.name` per the iter-56 A2 work.

- **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they import `_resolve_test_database_url` + `_make_test_engine` from `tests.conftest` directly + drive every code path with monkeypatched env vars. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`.

- **DoD impact**: **No DoD ticked.** DoD #11 needs (a) conftest switch (this slice) + (b) Postgres CI job + (c) per-test isolation for shared Postgres. CONFTEST1 closes (a). After CONFTEST1, a developer can run `TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql:///test_uc uv run pytest tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py` and verify the helper resolves the URL + builds a Postgres-dialect engine — a one-line evidence shoring step toward DoD #11 the loop can run without a human.

-->

<!-- R5l plan (now shipped — preserved for slice context).

**R5l — CSV export on the sub-category list view (`/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children`) (DoD #7 polish step; MISSION §3 "Export any list view to CSV"):** smallest end-to-end slice that continues the established R5a-R5k CSV-export pattern. **Thirteenth and final** CSV-export surface (after variance trend, PO list, items list, stock-takes list, suppliers list, locations list, taxonomy list, checkouts admin list, AC1's audit list, R5i's admin users list, R5j's reorder dashboard, R5k's per-item movement timeline). After R5l the R5 backlog item ("CSV export on every list view") is fully closed. **No DoD ticked** (DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7; this is polish that extends MISSION §3's "Export any list view to CSV" coverage).

- **Note on URL drift**: PROGRESS.md "Next slice" prose called the route `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/sub`. The real route is `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children` (verified by reading `app/taxonomy.py:545`). The slice targets the actual route shape; the prose was stale (the `/sub/` URL is used only for the per-sub-cat edit/archive/unarchive routes via `/admin/taxonomy/sub/{node_id}/...`).

- **Scope** (`app/taxonomy.py` + `app/templates/taxonomy_children_list.html` + extension of `tests/integration/test_taxonomy_routes.py` only — no new modules; no new migrations; no new helpers): mechanical extension of `app/taxonomy.py::list_sub_categories` with a `?format=csv` query branch using R5e's `csv_branch` helper. Module-level `_SUB_CSV_HEADERS` constant + `_csv_rows_for_sub_categories(rows)` helper. Return type widens from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; decorator simplified from `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children", response_class=HTMLResponse)` to `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children")`. mypy source-file count stays at 29.

- **Three columns**: `id`, `sort_order`, `name`. Mirrors the top-level `_TAXONOMY_CSV_HEADERS` shape one-for-one (the list view is structurally identical — sub-cats and top-level cats share the same `TaxonomyNode` schema and the same HTML table columns: Order / Name). The parent context is encoded in the filename (`subcategories_parent_{parent_id}_{show}.csv`) rather than as a column — same posture as the top-level taxonomy CSV which encodes `show` in the filename. Including a `parent_id` cell on every row would be redundant since every row in the file shares the same parent.

- **Filename `subcategories_parent_{parent_id}_{show}.csv`** (e.g. `subcategories_parent_5_active.csv`). The parent id is in the URL path so a downstream collator that exports many parents can keep the files distinct without renaming. The `show` partition matches the established taxonomy filename pattern.

- **Role gate inherited**: existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` blocks every non-Manager / non-Admin role at the dependency layer — anon 401, pending / Workshop / Office 403, Manager / Admin 200. Same Manager-only gate as the top-level taxonomy list (taxonomy is Manager-owned per MISSION §3 — Office is a sibling, not a subset).

- **404 / 400 preserved**: the existing `_get_top_level_parent` helper raises 404 for unknown parent and 400 for a parent_id that is itself a sub-category (depth limit). The CSV branch must run *after* this guard so an unknown parent CSV request 404s exactly like the HTML branch (otherwise a CSV would render with a header row and no rows for a non-existent parent — silently confusing).

- **Template** `taxonomy_children_list.html`: new `<a data-testid="sub-csv-link" href="/admin/taxonomy/{{ parent.id }}/children?format=csv&show={{ show }}">Download CSV</a>` link block placed above the table, with `show` parameter so the link respects the active tab — visible whether or not there are sub-cats (a Manager auditing the empty state can still pull a header-only CSV as evidence; same posture as R5j's empty-state CSV link). Mirrors the top-level taxonomy list's `taxonomy-list-csv-link` shape.

- **Tests** (~22 new in `tests/integration/test_taxonomy_routes.py`, mirroring R5k's structure):
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvRoleEnforcement` (5): anon CSV 401; pending CSV 403; Workshop CSV 403; Office CSV 403; Manager CSV 200; Admin CSV 200.
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvHeaders` (4): unknown parent CSV 404; sub-cat parent CSV 400; Content-Type carries `text/csv; charset=utf-8`; default filename `subcategories_parent_{id}_active.csv` carries the parent id; archived show: filename has `_archived` suffix.
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvBody` (5): empty children renders header-only CSV exact `f"{header}\r\n"` match; one sub-cat one data row carries id + sort_order + name cells; show=active filters to active only; show=archived filters to archived only; sort_order then name ordering; sub-cats under a different parent are excluded.
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvHtmlBranch` (2): blank format renders HTML; unknown `?format=garbage` renders HTML.
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvReadOnly` (1): GET-CSV writes no audit row.
  - `TestSubCategoryListCsvLink` (2): HTML page renders the `sub-csv-link` with `format=csv` href + active show; archived tab CSV link includes `show=archived`.

- **Why this shape, not a single combined `taxonomy.csv` with both levels**: the top-level `/admin/taxonomy?format=csv` already exports all top-level nodes; sub-cat exports are scoped per-parent at the route level, matching the HTML's per-parent listing. A "flat" combined CSV with `parent_id` and `level` columns would either duplicate existing data or hide it behind a different shape. The per-parent-CSV shape preserves the natural URL hierarchy (the CSV download lives at the same URL the HTML page lives) and matches every other CSV surface's "scope == route's scope" invariant.

- **No `app/` change beyond `taxonomy.py`.** Pure route extension + template link + tests-only addition.

- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: route at `app/taxonomy.py:545` is `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children", response_class=HTMLResponse)` mounted at `/admin/taxonomy` (router prefix at `:56`); role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` at `:550` (Admin passes via `app/auth.py`'s blanket override; Office + Workshop blocked); `_get_top_level_parent` at `:166-184` 404s on unknown id and 400s when parent is itself a sub-category; ordering `_LIST_ORDER, sort_order, name` at `:563`; same `archived_at` filter shape as the top-level `list_taxonomy` route at `:260-265`. Existing top-level `_TAXONOMY_CSV_HEADERS` + `_csv_rows_for_taxonomy` at `:229-246` are the structural template.

- **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they drive the actual route handler with TestClient + fixtures, and the assertions pin every observable byte of the CSV output. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`.

- **DoD impact**: **No DoD ticked.** DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7 with the dashboard widgets + reports. R5l extends the CSV-export pattern to the last remaining list view, fully closing MISSION §3's "Export any list view to CSV" requirement. After R5l the R5 backlog item is closed; the next slice should target an unticked DoD (DoD #1 / #10 / #11 / #12-deploy).

-->

---

<!-- R5j plan (now shipped — preserved for slice context).

**R5j — CSV export on `/admin/reorder` reorder dashboard (DoD #7 polish step; MISSION §3 "Export any list view to CSV"):** smallest end-to-end slice that continues the established R5a-R5i CSV-export pattern. Eleventh CSV-export surface (after variance trend, PO list, items list, stock-takes list, suppliers list, locations list, taxonomy list, checkouts admin list, AC1's audit list, and R5i's admin users list). After R5j the only remaining list views without CSV export are the per-item movements timeline and the sub-category list — each is a future R5k/l slice if/when needed. **No DoD ticked** (DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7; this is polish that extends "Export any list view to CSV" coverage).

- **Scope** (`app/reorder.py` + `app/templates/reorder_dashboard.html` + extension of `tests/integration/test_reorder_routes.py` only — no new modules; no new migrations; no new helpers): mechanical extension of `app/reorder.py::reorder_dashboard` with a `?format=csv` query branch using R5e's `csv_branch` helper. Module-level `_REORDER_CSV_HEADERS` constant + `_csv_rows_for_reorder(groups)` helper that flattens the grouped output into one row per item (with the supplier-grouping context preserved as columns so a downstream consumer can re-group / filter). Return type widens from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; decorator simplified from `@router.get("", response_class=HTMLResponse)` to `@router.get("")`. mypy source-file count stays at 29.
- **10 columns**: `supplier_id`, `supplier_name`, `supplier_archived`, `item_id`, `sku`, `name`, `unit`, `current_qty`, `threshold`, `reorder_qty`, `deficit`. The supplier-grouping context is preserved as three columns (id + name with `(archived)` suffix when applicable + boolean archived flag) so the CSV stands alone without the HTML's section-grouping UI. `current_qty` / `threshold` / `reorder_qty` / `deficit` are `Decimal` and render via `csv_response`'s coercion as `str(value)` (e.g. `5.0000`). `supplier_id` renders empty when `None` (the no-supplier bucket); `supplier_name` then renders `(no supplier)` matching the HTML label; `supplier_archived` renders `True`/`False` strings (Python `bool` → `str` via `csv_response` coercion). Filename `reorder.csv`.
- **Role gate inherited**: existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE))` blocks every non-allowed role at the dependency layer — anon 401, pending / Workshop 403, Office / Manager / Admin 200. Same inheritance as R5b (PO list) + R5d (stock-takes list).
- **Template** `reorder_dashboard.html`: new `<a data-testid="reorder-csv-link" href="/admin/reorder?format=csv">Download CSV</a>` link block above the empty-state / groups branches — visible whether or not there are items below threshold (so a Manager auditing the empty state can still pull a header-only CSV as evidence).
- **Tests** (~17 new in `tests/integration/test_reorder_routes.py`, mirroring R5i's structure):
  - `TestReorderCsvRoleEnforcement` (6): anon CSV 401; pending CSV 403; Workshop CSV 403; Office CSV 200; Manager CSV 200; Admin CSV 200.
  - `TestReorderCsvHeaders` (2): Content-Type carries `text/csv; charset=utf-8`; default filename `reorder.csv`.
  - `TestReorderCsvBody` (5): header row is first line `supplier_id,supplier_name,supplier_archived,item_id,sku,name,unit,current_qty,threshold,reorder_qty,deficit`; single below-threshold item with named supplier renders a row with the right cell values across all 10 columns + correct `deficit` arithmetic; no-supplier bucket renders empty `supplier_id` cell + `(no supplier)` `supplier_name` + `False` `supplier_archived`; archived supplier renders `(archived)` suffix in `supplier_name` + `True` `supplier_archived`; multiple supplier groups ordered alphabetically (matches HTML ordering).
  - `TestReorderCsvEmptyState` (1): no below-threshold items renders header-only CSV (just the header line, no data rows).
  - `TestReorderCsvHtmlBranch` (2): blank format renders HTML; unknown `?format=garbage` renders HTML.
  - `TestReorderCsvReadOnly` (1): GET-CSV writes no audit row.
  - `TestReorderCsvLink` (1): HTML page renders the `reorder-csv-link` with `format=csv` href + visible even on empty state.
- **Why this shape, not a per-supplier-group CSV**: the reorder dashboard's grouping is presentation-only — the underlying data is one row per item with a supplier_id. A grouped CSV (one section per supplier with blank rows between) would be hostile to downstream filtering / pivoting. The flat one-row-per-item shape with supplier columns is what every BI / spreadsheet tool expects. Same posture as R5b's PO list which flattens line items per PO into separate rows with the PO-id column repeated.
- **No `app/` change beyond `reorder.py`.** Pure route extension + template link + tests-only addition.
- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: route at `app/reorder.py:111` is `@router.get("", response_class=HTMLResponse)` mounted at `/admin/reorder` (router prefix at `:44`); role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE))` at `:114` (Admin passes via `app/auth.py`'s blanket override; Workshop blocked); `_build_groups` returns groups dicts with `supplier_id` (or `None`), `supplier_label` (or `(no supplier)` constant at `:47`, or `<name> (archived)` suffix at `:84`), `supplier_archived` boolean, and `rows` list with `item` + `current_qty` + `threshold` + `reorder_qty` + `deficit`; HTML grouping order is suppliers alphabetically then no-supplier last (`:105-108`).
- **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they drive the actual route handler with TestClient + fixtures, and the assertions pin every observable byte of the CSV output. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`.
- **DoD impact**: **No DoD ticked.** DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7 with the dashboard widgets + reports. R5j extends the CSV-export pattern to one more list view, advancing MISSION §3's "Export any list view to CSV" requirement. After R5j the only remaining list views without CSV export are the per-item movements timeline (`/admin/items/{id}/movements`) and the sub-category list (`/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/sub`).

-->
