Gaps: `GET /auth/google/login` had **zero** test coverage; `GET /auth/google/callback` had zero coverage for its 4 unhappy branches (`OAuthError` → 400, missing-sub or missing-email userinfo → 400, userinfo-fetched-separately path via `google.userinfo()`, 503 when Google not configured). Without these tests, a regression in any unhappy path could ship undetected. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #1 still needs (f) OAuth-stub server-side Playwright walk (~150-200 LoC, technical risks around Authlib `id_token` JWT verification, state echo, `server_metadata_url` discovery bypass; queued in Next slice). After OAUTH1, only (f) remains as the gap. **Scope** (`tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py` only — extension; no `app/` change; no new modules; no new helpers; mypy source-file count stays at 29). **New `TestGoogleLogin` class (3 tests, ~50 LoC)**: `test_login_redirects_to_google_authz_endpoint` monkeypatches `google.authorize_redirect` to return `RedirectResponse(url=stub_url, status_code=307)`, asserts route returns 307 with stubbed Location; `test_login_returns_503_when_google_not_configured` monkeypatches `oauth.create_client` to return `None`, asserts 503 + detail contains `"not configured"`; `test_login_passes_callback_redirect_uri_to_authlib` captures the `redirect_uri` arg via async closure that records the call args, asserts captured URI equals `f"{settings.app_base_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/google/callback"`. **Extended `TestGoogleCallback` class (5 new unhappy-path tests, ~70 LoC)**: `test_callback_returns_400_on_oauth_error` monkeypatches `authorize_access_token` to raise `OAuthError(error="invalid_state", description="state mismatch")`, asserts 400 + body contains `"OAuth error"` + `"invalid_state"`; `test_callback_returns_400_when_userinfo_missing_email` returns `{"userinfo": {"sub": "g-no-email"}}`, asserts 400 + `"missing user identity"`; `test_callback_returns_400_when_userinfo_missing_sub` returns `{"userinfo": {"email": "no-sub@example.com"}}`, asserts 400 + `"missing user identity"`; `test_callback_fetches_userinfo_separately_when_token_omits_it` returns `{}` (no `userinfo` key) + mocks `google.userinfo` to return a complete payload, asserts 303 + user created PENDING + `google.userinfo` was awaited once via `assert_awaited_once`; `test_callback_returns_503_when_google_not_configured` monkeypatches `oauth.create_client` to return `None`, asserts 503 + `"not configured"`. **Mocking strategy**: same `monkeypatch.setattr(auth_module.oauth, "create_client", lambda _name: google_mock)` pattern as the existing tests. For `userinfo`-fetched-separately, the `google_mock` carries both `authorize_access_token` AND `userinfo` as `AsyncMock`s. For the 503 path, the lambda returns `None` directly (mirrors `oauth.create_client("google")` returning `None` when no client was registered at app startup — `app/auth.py:29` only calls `oauth.register("google", ...)` when both `google_client_id` and `google_client_secret` are set). **First-run pass on all 8 tests.** No iteration needed — the cross-checking against source caught every gotcha before they could surface as test failures (e.g. confirmed the `userinfo`-separately path returns the dict directly; confirmed `OAuthError` is exposed at the `authlib.integrations.starlette_client` import; confirmed `google.userinfo()` accepts `token=token` as a keyword arg per Authlib API). **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `app/auth.py:184-195` (google_login: `oauth.create_client("google")` → 503 if None; `authorize_redirect(request, redirect_uri)` returns RedirectResponse via cast); `app/auth.py:199-233` (google_callback: 503 if no client → `authorize_access_token` (OAuthError → 400 with `f"OAuth error: {exc.error}"` detail) → userinfo from token OR `google.userinfo(token=token)` → 400 if missing sub/email → `upsert_user_from_userinfo` → 303 to `/`); `app/auth.py:191` (`redirect_uri = f"{settings.app_base_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/google/callback"`); existing `TestGoogleCallback` at `tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py:222` for the mock pattern. **No `app/` change.** Pure tests-only addition. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Repo health**: ruff clean; mypy clean (29 source files, no change); pytest 2519 passed + 3 skipped (was 2511 + 3 at iter-76 HEAD `5136ba4`, **+8**); Playwright e2e — `test_auth_e2e.py` + `test_admin_users_e2e.py` re-run (3/3 passing); the rest of the e2e suite (16/16 baseline) unaffected by tests-only addition to `tests/integration/`. **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. After OAUTH1 the only gap to ticking DoD #1 is (f) — the OAuth-stub server-side Playwright walk. The next slice should pivot to that substantial slice OR to another unticked DoD (#10 CI gate, #11 CI Postgres job — both gated on remote infra). |
| 76 | CONFTEST2 — Per-test isolation via SAVEPOINT for non-SQLite backends | `5136ba4` | Second DoD #11 progress step (after CONFTEST1 iter-75). The unticked half of DoD #11 ("runs in cloud config on Postgres with no code changes (env vars only)") needs (a) a conftest URL/dialect switch (CONFTEST1 ✓) + (b) per-test isolation on shared Postgres ✓ (this slice) + (c) Postgres CI job (gated on remote infra). With CONFTEST1's `TEST_DATABASE_URL` override in place, each test creates a fresh engine — fine for in-memory SQLite (each engine = isolated DB) but on shared Postgres every test would step on every other test's row data. CONFTEST2 closes (b) by adding the SQLAlchemy 2.0 outer-transaction + SAVEPOINT pattern. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #11 still needs (c) Postgres CI job gated on remote infra. **Scope** (`tests/conftest.py` extension + new `tests/integration/test_conftest_isolation.py` only — no `app/` change; no new migrations; no new modules outside `tests/`): added module-level `_make_isolated_session(engine: Engine) -> Iterator[Session]` context-manager helper that opens a connection, begins an outer transaction, binds a sessionmaker with `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"`, yields a session inside `with SessionLocal() as session:`, and rolls back the outer transaction at teardown via `if transaction.is_active: transaction.rollback(); connection.close()`. Modified `db_session` fixture to dispatch on URL prefix: SQLite default (`url.startswith("sqlite")`) → existing per-engine path (every existing test unaffected); non-SQLite → `_make_isolated_session(engine)` for shared-backend isolation. Modified `client` fixture to handle both bind types: when `db_session.get_bind()` returns a `Connection` (savepoint mode), `OverrideSession` is built with `bind=connection` + `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` so route-handler sessions join the same outer transaction; when it returns an `Engine` (SQLite mode), the existing engine-bound posture is preserved. The bind-type introspection via `isinstance(bind, Connection)` is a clean dispatcher. **`join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"`** is the SQLAlchemy 2.0 idiom for the "test session inside an outer transaction; route-handler `session.commit()` should NOT escape the outer scope" pattern. Documented at https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/orm/session_basics.html#joining-a-session-into-an-external-transaction-such-as-for-test-suites. SQLAlchemy 2.0.49 installed. **First-run failures on 3 of 9 tests** — the `test_commit_does_not_escape_outer_transaction` + `test_rollback_inside_helper_preserves_outer_transaction` + `test_integrity_error_recovery` tests all initially failed with rows persisting after teardown. Root cause: SQLite's pysqlite driver has automatic transaction handling that breaks the SAVEPOINT pattern. Fix per the SQLAlchemy docs (https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/dialects/sqlite.html#serializable-isolation-savepoints-transactional-ddl): install `connect` event listener that sets `dbapi_conn.isolation_level = None` (disables pysqlite's automatic BEGIN/COMMIT) + `begin` event listener that issues explicit `BEGIN`. Postgres has no such quirk so the helper itself stays clean (the events live in the test fixture only). After the fix all 9 tests passed first run. **No `app/` change.** Pure tests-side extension + tests-only addition. mypy source-file count stays at 29 (mypy strict only on `app/`). **Tests** (9 new in `tests/integration/test_conftest_isolation.py`): `TestMakeIsolatedSession` (6): `test_yields_writable_session_against_sqlite_file` (basic round-trip — write a `User` row, flush, read back); `test_commit_does_not_escape_outer_transaction` (write + commit savepoint inside helper, exit helper, open fresh session on same engine, assert row absent — proves savepoint commit doesn't escape outer txn); `test_rollback_inside_helper_preserves_outer_transaction` (write + rollback savepoint, write different row + commit, exit helper, fresh session, assert both absent); `test_integrity_error_recovery` (commit a row, attempt duplicate-email insert, catch `IntegrityError`, call `session.rollback()`, write a different row + commit, assert it's visible inside helper but absent after teardown — pins the savepoint-recovery-after-IntegrityError pattern that ~30 unit tests use); `test_uses_savepoint_mode` (source-text inspection: `'join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"' in inspect.getsource(_make_isolated_session)` — forcing function for a future PR that drops the parameter); `test_session_is_bound_to_a_connection_not_an_engine` (the savepoint pattern requires Connection bind; `isinstance(session.get_bind(), Connection)`). `TestDbSessionDispatch` (2): `test_db_session_uses_sqlite_path_by_default` (default config → bind is Engine, not Connection); `test_db_session_dispatch_source_branches_on_url_prefix` (source-text: `'url.startswith("sqlite")' in inspect.getsource(db_session)` AND `'_make_isolated_session' in source` — forcing function for dispatch-logic changes). `TestSavepointPatternBehaviour` (1): `test_outer_transaction_is_rolled_back_at_teardown` (event listener on `engine` for `rollback` events; verifies the outer-transaction rollback fires during helper teardown). **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `tests/conftest.py:69-103` (pre-edit) was the existing fixtures; SQLAlchemy 2.0.49 installed (verified via `uv run python -c "import sqlalchemy; print(sqlalchemy.__version__)"`); `Session.__init__` accepts `join_transaction_mode` parameter (verified via `help(Session.__init__)`); 9 unit-test files have local `db` fixtures (`test_taxonomy.py:24` etc.) that hardcode their own `create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")` — those are independent of `db_session` and unaffected; 30+ `db.rollback()` calls in unit tests are local to those files' fixtures — unaffected. **The pysqlite-fix events live in the test fixture, not the helper.** Trade-off: the helper assumes the engine is correctly configured for SAVEPOINT use. On Postgres this is automatic. On SQLite, callers must install the `connect`/`begin` events (the SQLAlchemy-recommended boilerplate). This keeps the helper minimal — it's fundamentally a Postgres tool — and lets SQLite-side validation tests use the standard SQLAlchemy-docs incantation. Logged in the rolling self-critique. **Repo health**: ruff clean (one auto-fix needed: removed unused `Base` import); mypy clean (29 source files, no change); pytest 2511 passed + 3 skipped (was 2502 + 3 at iter-75 HEAD `e22ca9f`, **+9 CONFTEST2 tests**); Playwright e2e — full e2e suite re-run 16/16 passing (tests-only refactor doesn't affect the e2e suite which has its own conftest at `tests/e2e/conftest.py`). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. After CONFTEST2 the test conftest-side work for DoD #11 is complete; only CI infra (`.github/workflows/ci.yml` + a configured remote) remains. The next slice should pivot to another unticked DoD: #1 (OAuth stub Playwright walk) or #10 (CI gate, gated on remote being configured). |
| 75 | CONFTEST1 — `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var override + dialect-aware test engine helper | `2f2c2c3` | First DoD #11 progress step. The unticked half of DoD #11 ("runs in cloud config on Postgres with no code changes (env vars only)") needs (a) a conftest switch + (b) a Postgres CI job + (c) a per-test isolation strategy on shared Postgres. CONFTEST1 closes (a). The app side already had dialect-aware engine kwargs (`app/db.py::_engine_kwargs` at `:9-12` branches on `url.startswith("sqlite")`) and dialect-aware audit-log immutability triggers (`app/audit.py::apply_immutability_triggers` branches on `connection.dialect.name`); only the test conftest hardcoded `"sqlite:///:memory:"` + `StaticPool` + `check_same_thread=False`. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #11 also needs (b) Postgres CI job (gated on a remote being configured + a CI workflow YAML — `git remote -v` shows nothing) + (c) per-test isolation strategy on shared Postgres (transaction-rollback fixtures or schema-per-test) which is the queued CONFTEST2 follow-up. **Scope** (`tests/conftest.py` refactor + new `tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py` only — no `app/` change; no new migrations; no new modules outside `tests/`): refactored `tests/conftest.py` to extract two module-level helpers — `_resolve_test_database_url()` reads `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var and falls back to `_DEFAULT_TEST_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///:memory:"`; `_make_test_engine(url)` returns an `Engine` with dialect-aware pool / connect_args (SQLite gets `StaticPool` + `connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}`; non-SQLite returns `create_engine(url, future=True)` with SQLAlchemy's default `QueuePool`). The `db_session` fixture now calls `_make_test_engine(_resolve_test_database_url())` instead of inlining the `create_engine` call. Existing behaviour preserved when env var unset. **Decoupled from app-side `DATABASE_URL`**: the existing `os.environ.setdefault("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///:memory:")` continues to drive the app config (via `app/config.py::Settings.database_url`); `TEST_DATABASE_URL` drives only the test fixture's engine. Test isolation works because `db_session` creates its own engine and `client` fixture's `app.dependency_overrides[get_session]` rebinds to that engine. So a developer can set `TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg:///test_uc` to point only the *fixture* at Postgres without changing `DATABASE_URL`. **psycopg v3 driver scheme** (`postgresql+psycopg://...`): the project's installed Postgres driver per `pyproject.toml:32` is `psycopg[binary]>=3.2`, not psycopg2. Initial test attempts used the bare `postgresql://` scheme which defaults to psycopg2 and failed `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'psycopg2'`; flipped to `postgresql+psycopg://` per SQLAlchemy's URL convention and the engine builds without error (lazy — no actual Postgres connection happens at `create_engine` time). **Isolation note (deferred to CONFTEST2)**: Postgres URLs share a single backing database across every test, which would break test isolation as written (each test's `Base.metadata.create_all` is idempotent on the schema, but row data persists). The conftest switch is necessary but not sufficient for full Postgres test runs — CONFTEST2 needs to add transaction-rollback per-test or schema-per-test isolation. CONFTEST1 documents this gap explicitly in the docstring + the rolling self-critique notes. Right now the switch lets a developer run the suite against a fresh Postgres for a smoke test (e.g. `createdb test_uc && TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg:///test_uc make test`) but isolation between tests requires the follow-up. **No `app/` change.** Pure tests-side refactor + tests-only addition. mypy source-file count stays at 29 (mypy strict only on `app/`). **Tests** (10 new in `tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py`): `TestResolveTestDatabaseUrl` (3): default returns sqlite memory URL when env unset (also pins the `_DEFAULT_TEST_DATABASE_URL` constant against the documented fallback); respects `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var when set; passes through arbitrary URL string verbatim (e.g. SQLite file URL with query params). `TestMakeTestEngine` (5): sqlite memory URL → `engine.dialect.name == "sqlite"`; sqlite memory URL → `engine.pool` is `StaticPool`; sqlite file URL → also gets `StaticPool` (file-backed SQLite still benefits from cross-thread visibility); postgres URL via `postgresql+psycopg://` (lazy — no connection) → `engine.dialect.name == "postgresql"`; postgres URL → `engine.pool` is NOT `StaticPool` (default `QueuePool`). `TestDbSessionFixtureStillWorks` (2): the existing `db_session` fixture yields a writable session that round-trips a `User` row with `UserStatus.PENDING` (sanity check that the refactor preserves behaviour); fixture's bound engine has `dialect.name == "sqlite"` AND `pool` is `StaticPool` for the default env config (forcing-function for a future PR that breaks the SQLite default's cross-thread visibility — e.g. by switching to NullPool — would fail visibly here, since the TestClient → fixture data-sharing contract depends on StaticPool). **First-run failures on 2 of 10**: both `TestMakeTestEngine` Postgres tests failed initially with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'psycopg2'` because I used the bare `postgresql://` URL scheme. Flipped to `postgresql+psycopg://` (psycopg v3) and both passed. The 8 SQLite-side tests passed first run. **Lessons**: (1) When the project ships psycopg v3 (per `pyproject.toml`), the URL scheme must be `postgresql+psycopg://` not the bare `postgresql://`. SQLAlchemy's URL-to-driver mapping defaults to psycopg2 for the bare prefix. Test code that wants to be driver-agnostic should use the explicit `+psycopg` suffix. (2) `create_engine` is lazy — building a `postgresql+psycopg://` engine doesn't require a running Postgres, so dialect + pool inspection is testable without Postgres infra. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `tests/conftest.py:36-41` (pre-edit) was the existing `create_engine` call with hardcoded sqlite memory URL + StaticPool + check_same_thread; `app/db.py:9-15` has the parallel `_engine_kwargs` helper that the test conftest now mirrors; `app/config.py:21` defaults `database_url` to `"sqlite:///./dev.db"` (only the test conftest's `os.environ.setdefault("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///:memory:")` at `:24` forces in-memory for the app config); `app/audit.py::apply_immutability_triggers` already branches on `connection.dialect.name` per the iter-56 A2 work; `pyproject.toml:32` is `"psycopg[binary]>=3.2"`. **Iter-74 count anchor was stale by 16** — re-anchored this iteration. iter-74 PROGRESS.md "Current state" claimed `2508 passed + 3 skipped` but on-disk reality at iter-74 HEAD `f452740` is 2492 + 3 (re-anchored via `git stash && git checkout f452740 -- tests/conftest.py && uv run pytest --collect-only`). Same kind of count drift the iter-67 self-critique flagged about iter-66 + the iter-74 self-critique flagged about iter-73. **Repo health**: ruff clean (auto-fix corrected one `I001` import-sort issue in `tests/conftest.py`); mypy clean (29 source files, no change); pytest 2502 passed + 3 skipped (was 2492 + 3 at on-disk iter-74 HEAD `f452740`, **+10**); Playwright e2e — full e2e suite re-run 16/16 passing (tests-only refactor of `tests/conftest.py` doesn't affect the e2e suite which has its own conftest at `tests/e2e/conftest.py`). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. DoD #11 needs (a) conftest switch (this slice) + (b) Postgres CI job + (c) per-test isolation. CONFTEST1 closes (a). After CONFTEST1, a developer can run `TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg:///test_uc uv run pytest tests/integration/test_conftest_engine.py` and verify the helper resolves the URL + builds a Postgres-dialect engine — a one-line evidence shoring step toward DoD #11. The next slice should pivot to CONFTEST2 (per-test isolation on shared Postgres) or to another unticked DoD (#1 OAuth stub, #10 CI gate). |
| 74 | R5l — CSV export on `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children` sub-category list | `f452740` | Thirteenth and final CSV-export surface (after variance trend, PO list, items list, stock-takes list, suppliers list, locations list, taxonomy list, checkouts admin list, AC1's audit list, R5i's admin users list, R5j's reorder dashboard, and R5k's per-item movement timeline). After R5l the R5 backlog item ("CSV export on every list view") is **fully closed**. Mechanical extension of `app/taxonomy.py::list_sub_categories` (route `GET /admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children` mounted via `router = APIRouter(prefix="/admin/taxonomy")`) with a `?format=csv` query branch using R5e's `csv_branch` helper. **No DoD ticked** (DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7; this is polish that extends MISSION §3's "Export any list view to CSV" coverage to the sub-category list). **Note on URL drift**: PROGRESS.md "Next slice" prose called the route `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/sub`. The real route is `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children` (verified via reading `app/taxonomy.py:545`). The slice targets the actual route shape; the prose was stale (the `/sub/` URL is used only for per-sub-cat edit/archive/unarchive routes via `/admin/taxonomy/sub/{node_id}/...`). **Scope**: route extension on `app/taxonomy.py::list_sub_categories` adds `format: str = ""` query param + walrus-form `csv_branch` call **after** the existing `_get_top_level_parent` guard so unknown parent → 404 and sub-cat parent → 400 happen before the CSV branch runs (CSV would otherwise silently render an empty-row file for invalid parents); module-level `_SUB_CSV_HEADERS` constant + `_csv_rows_for_sub_categories(rows)` helper; return type widened from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; decorator simplified from `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children", response_class=HTMLResponse)` to `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children")`. **Three columns**: `id`, `sort_order`, `name`. Mirrors the top-level `_TAXONOMY_CSV_HEADERS` shape one-for-one (the list view is structurally identical — sub-cats and top-level cats share the same `TaxonomyNode` schema and the same HTML table columns: Order / Name). The parent context is encoded in the filename (`subcategories_parent_{parent_id}_{show}.csv`) rather than as a per-row column — every row in a given file shares the same parent. `sort_order` is rendered as `str(int)` via `csv_response`'s coercion (e.g. `10`); `name` is the raw string. **Filename `subcategories_parent_{parent_id}_{show}.csv`** (e.g. `subcategories_parent_5_active.csv`). Parent id from URL path so a downstream collator that exports many parents can keep files distinct without renaming. The `show` partition matches the established taxonomy filename pattern (`taxonomy_active.csv` / `taxonomy_archived.csv`). **Role gate inherited**: existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` blocks every non-Manager / non-Admin role at the dependency layer — anon 401, pending / Workshop / Office 403, Manager / Admin 200. Same Manager-only gate as the top-level taxonomy list (taxonomy is Manager-owned per MISSION §3 — Office is a sibling, not a subset). **404 / 400 preserved**: `_get_top_level_parent` raises 404 for unknown parent and 400 for parent_id that is itself a sub-category (depth limit). The CSV branch runs *after* this guard so unknown parent CSV requests 404 exactly like the HTML branch. **HTML branch unchanged**: `format=blank` and `format=garbage` paths fall through to HTML render (pinned by `TestSubCategoryListCsvHtmlBranch`). **Audit-quiet**: read-only, no audit row written (pinned by `TestSubCategoryListCsvReadOnly` against `_audit_rows` count of `taxonomy_node` action rows). **Template** `app/templates/taxonomy_children_list.html`: new `<a data-testid="sub-csv-link" href="/admin/taxonomy/{{ parent.id }}/children?format=csv&amp;show={{ show }}">Download CSV</a>` link block placed below the `sub-tabs` nav, with `show` parameter so the link respects the active tab — visible whether or not there are sub-cats (a Manager auditing the empty state can still pull a header-only CSV as evidence; same posture as R5j's empty-state CSV link). The `&amp;` escape (rather than literal `&`) is what Jinja autoescape emits — the rendered HTML decodes back to `&` and the test assertion uses `format=csv` substring which is unaffected. **No new modules**; no new migrations. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Tests** (21 new in `tests/integration/test_taxonomy_routes.py`): `TestSubCategoryListCsvRoleEnforcement` (6: anon 401; pending 403; Workshop 403; Office 403; Manager 200; Admin 200). `TestSubCategoryListCsvHeaders` (5: unknown parent CSV 404; sub-cat parent CSV 400; Content-Type carries `text/csv; charset=utf-8`; default filename `subcategories_parent_{id}_active.csv` carries the parent id; archived show: filename has `_archived` suffix). `TestSubCategoryListCsvBody` (5: empty children renders header-only CSV exact `"id,sort_order,name\\r\\n"` match; one sub-cat one data row carries id + sort_order + name cells; show=active filters to active only; show=archived filters to archived only — pinned by `body.count("Silver") == 1` since "Old Silver" is the only "Silver"-substring row visible; sort_order then name ordering with sort_order=10 ties broken by name; sub-cats under a different parent excluded). `TestSubCategoryListCsvHtmlBranch` (2: blank format renders HTML with `data-testid="sub-tabs"`; unknown `?format=garbage` renders HTML). `TestSubCategoryListCsvReadOnly` (1: GET-CSV writes no `taxonomy_node`-entity-type audit row). `TestSubCategoryListCsvLink` (2: HTML page renders the `sub-csv-link` with `format=csv` href + `show=active`; archived tab CSV link includes `show=archived`). **First-run failures on 7 of 21**: header-shape + body-shape + template-link tests all failed with the expected reason — content-disposition KeyError (no CSV branch existed) and missing `data-testid="sub-csv-link"` (no template link existed). The 14 tests that passed pre-implementation were the role-enforcement matrix (the role gate is inherited by every method on the route, including the CSV variants), the 404/400 guards (same `_get_top_level_parent` helper raises before the CSV branch is reached), the `format=garbage` HTML fallthrough (pre-implementation the route ignored unknown `format` values entirely), and the `csv_writes_no_audit` test (no audit was being written before either). All 21 pass after implementing the CSV branch + template link. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: route at `app/taxonomy.py:545` was `@router.get("/{parent_id}/children", response_class=HTMLResponse)` (pre-edit) mounted at `/admin/taxonomy` (router prefix at `:56`); role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` at `:550` (Admin passes via `app/auth.py`'s blanket override; Office + Workshop blocked); `_get_top_level_parent` at `:166-184` 404s on unknown id and 400s when parent is itself a sub-category; ordering `_LIST_ORDER, sort_order, name` at `:563`; same `archived_at` filter shape as the top-level `list_taxonomy` route at `:260-265`. Existing top-level `_TAXONOMY_CSV_HEADERS` + `_csv_rows_for_taxonomy` at `:229-246` are the structural template. **Iter-73 count anchor was stale by 16** — re-anchored this iteration. iter-73 PROGRESS.md "Current state" claimed `2471 passed + 3 skipped` but on-disk reality at iter-73 HEAD `feae48a` is 2487 + 3 (re-anchored via `git stash && uv run pytest --collect-only`). Same kind of count drift the iter-67 self-critique flagged. **Repo health**: ruff clean; mypy clean (29 source files, no change); pytest 2508 passed + 3 skipped (was 2487 + 3 at on-disk iter-73 HEAD `feae48a`, **+21**); Playwright e2e — `test_taxonomy_e2e.py` re-run (1/1 passing) + full e2e suite re-run (16/16 passing — pure mechanical CSV-export extension; no UI behaviour change beyond the new download link). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. After R5l **the R5 backlog item is fully closed** — every list view in the app has a CSV export. The next slice should target an unticked DoD: #1 (Google SSO + role assignment), #10 (zero-failure full suite), #11 (SQLite/Postgres parity), or #12-deploy. |
| 73 | R5k — CSV export on `/admin/items/{item_id}/detail` per-item movement timeline | `6e60470` | Twelfth CSV-export surface (after variance trend, PO list, items list, stock-takes list, suppliers list, locations list, taxonomy list, checkouts admin list, AC1's audit list, R5i's admin users list, and R5j's reorder dashboard). Mechanical extension of `app/movements.py::item_detail` (route `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/detail` mounted via `router = APIRouter(prefix="/admin/items")`) with a `?format=csv` query branch using R5e's `csv_branch` helper. **No DoD ticked** (DoD #7 already ticked iter-57 / D7; this is polish that extends MISSION §3's "Export any list view to CSV" coverage to the per-item movement timeline). **Scope**: route extension on `app/movements.py::item_detail` adds `format: str = ""` query param + walrus-form `csv_branch` call before the layers + pagination work; module-level `_MOVEMENTS_CSV_HEADERS` constant + `_csv_rows_for_movements(movements)` helper + `_all_movements_for_csv(db, item_id)` helper that returns every movement for the item in newest-first order (no pagination — same posture as AC1's audit-CSV; the receiver of a per-item movement export wants the whole timeline, not just one HTML page) plus `_attach_directions_and_breakdown(...)` so the `direction` column reads `+` / `-` / `""` with identical semantics to the HTML render; return type widened from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; decorator simplified from `@router.get("/{item_id}/detail", response_class=HTMLResponse)` to `@router.get("/{item_id}/detail")`. **Nine columns**: `id`, `created_at`, `type`, `direction`, `qty`, `total_cost`, `actor_email`, `reason`, `note`. `created_at` is an ISO timestamp via `csv_export._coerce_cell`'s `datetime.isoformat()` branch; `type` is `IN` / `OUT` / `ADJUSTMENT` / `TRANSFER` (the `MovementType.value`); `direction` is empty for transfers (the only movement type that bypasses the cost engine — no layer-side or consumption-side join target); `total_cost` renders empty for `None` (transfers + any movement that hasn't been through the engine); `actor_email` renders empty for orphaned actors (matches AC1's audit-CSV posture; HTML uses `—` placeholder but CSV uses empty cell for clean spreadsheet sorting); `reason` and `note` render empty for `None`. The cost-layer breakdown rows that appear under each OUT/negative-adjust row in the HTML timeline are deliberately NOT in the CSV — they have a different shape (one row per layer consumed, joined to a parent movement). If a follow-up needs the breakdown, a separate route or `?include=breakdown` flag is the right approach. Filename `movements_item_{item_id}.csv` — the item id is in the URL path, so a downstream collator that exports many items can keep the files distinct without renaming. **Role gate inherited**: existing `Depends(require_role(Role.WORKSHOP, Role.OFFICE, Role.MANAGER))` (Admin via blanket override) blocks every non-allowed role at the dependency layer — anon 401, pending 403, every active role 200. Workshop sees the CSV link too (consistent with the HTML view, which already shows per-row `total_cost` to Workshop). **Archived item**: still exports (matches HTML's "archived items show their history" posture from `test_archived_item_still_renders` — pinned by `test_archived_item_still_exports_movements`). **Empty state**: header-only CSV (matches every prior R5* surface; pinned by `test_no_movements_renders_header_only_csv`). **HTML branch unchanged**: pagination still applies; `format=blank` and `format=garbage` paths fall through to HTML render (pinned by `TestItemDetailCsvHtmlBranch`). **Audit-quiet**: read-only, no audit row written (pinned by `TestItemDetailCsvReadOnly` against `AuditLog` row count). **Template** `app/templates/item_detail.html`: new `<a data-testid="movements-csv-link" href="/admin/items/{{ item.id }}/detail?format=csv">Download CSV →</a>` link block placed directly under the `<h2>Movement history</h2>` heading, visible whether or not the item has movements (a user inspecting an empty timeline can still pull a header-only CSV — same posture as R5j's empty-state CSV link). **No new modules**; no new migrations. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Tests** (25 new in `tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py`): `TestItemDetailCsvRoleEnforcement` (6: anon 401; pending 403; Workshop 200; Office 200; Manager 200; Admin 200). `TestItemDetailCsvHeaders` (3: unknown item 404; Content-Type carries `text/csv; charset=utf-8`; default filename `movements_item_{id}.csv` carries the item id verbatim). `TestItemDetailCsvBody` (10: header row is first line `id,created_at,type,direction,qty,total_cost,actor_email,reason,note`; IN movement renders full row including `direction=+` + `total_cost=25.0000`; OUT emits `direction=-` + layer-weighted `total_cost`; positive ADJUSTMENT emits `direction=+` + `reason="seed adjust increase"` carry-through; negative ADJUSTMENT emits `direction=-`; TRANSFER emits empty `direction` + empty `total_cost` cells (transfers bypass the cost engine); multi-movement order newest-first; archived item still exports; CSV ignores pagination — seed 25 movements (>= `_PAGE_SIZE=20`) and assert all 25 data rows present in CSV; orphaned actor renders empty `actor_email` cell). `TestItemDetailCsvEmptyState` (1: no movements → header-only CSV exact `f"{header}\\r\\n"` match). `TestItemDetailCsvHtmlBranch` (2: blank format renders HTML; unknown `?format=garbage` renders HTML). `TestItemDetailCsvReadOnly` (1: GET-CSV writes no audit row). `TestItemDetailCsvLink` (2: HTML page renders `movements-csv-link` with full URL `/admin/items/{id}/detail?format=csv` href; CSV link visible on empty-state page alongside `movements-timeline-empty`). **First-run failure on 1 of 25** (`test_movements_ordered_newest_first` — initial `body.index(f",{first.id},")` substring sanity check failed because the first column has no leading comma; dropped the redundant sanity check, the `ids = [int(row.split(",")[0]) for row in data_rows]` parsing is the load-bearing assert). All 25 pass after the fix. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: route at `app/movements.py:1134` was `@router.get("/{item_id}/detail", response_class=HTMLResponse)` (pre-edit) mounted at `/admin/items` (router prefix at `:117`); role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.WORKSHOP, Role.OFFICE, Role.MANAGER))`; `_get_item_or_404` returns archived items (no archived check in the helper at `:216`); `_PAGE_SIZE=20` at `:962`; `_attach_directions_and_breakdown` does two queries (cost layers + cost layer consumptions) and attaches `direction` ("+"/"-"/"" — defensive empty for transfers) + `breakdown` (list of consumption rows) per movement at `:1039-1104`; `StockMovement` columns: `qty`/`total_cost` are `Numeric(14, 4)` so a `Decimal("10")` round-trips as `Decimal("10.0000")` (pinned at `app/models.py:684,702`); `MovementType.value` is `"in"` / `"out"` / `"adjustment"` / `"transfer"`. **Repo health**: ruff clean; mypy clean (29 source files, no change); pytest 2471 passed + 3 skipped (was 2446 + 3 at iter-72 HEAD `6b9ac21`, **+25**); Playwright e2e — `test_movements_e2e.py` + `test_items_e2e.py` re-run (3/3 passing) + full e2e suite re-run (16/16 passing — pure mechanical CSV-export extension; no UI behaviour change beyond the new download link). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. After R5k the only remaining list view without CSV export is the sub-category list (`/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/sub`) — R5l if/when needed. |

Names project as Claude Code autonomous-build-loop project; explains the loop is the primary author + a human reviewer ticks DoD items + `MISSION.md` is the canonical scope document; lists 4 source-of-truth files (`MISSION.md` / `PROGRESS.md` / `CHANGELOG.md` / `loop.sh`); states explicitly that "external pull requests are not the workflow" (this is an internal UC build, not an open-source project); points readers at `MISSION.md` §8 ("How to work"); closes saying "if you have feedback or have spotted a bug, open an issue" (mirrors line 5's existing "Open an issue" copy). **README §`## License`** — replaced single `_TODO_` line with ~80-word paragraph naming the licence as **Proprietary** to match the canonical declaration in `pyproject.toml` line 7 (`license = { text = "Proprietary" }`). Explains: internal UC tool not open-sourced; source hosted only for UC + its build loop; no rights granted to outside parties; for licensing enquiries contact UC. The README §License is the user-facing docs surface for `pyproject.toml`'s `license` metadata; both must agree (same docs↔source consistency posture as DOC9a's PDF-library check vs. `pyproject.toml`). **Why "Proprietary" and not MIT/Apache** (the candidate the Next-slice prose floated): `pyproject.toml` already declares `license = { text = "Proprietary" }` — this is a load-bearing legal field. Silently swapping it would either change the project's licence (illegal scope change without authorization) or leave the README + `pyproject.toml` disagreeing on the licence — both worse than matching the existing declaration. MISSION.md doesn't say "open source" anywhere; the project is described as UC's internal inventory tool. The conservative move is to match `pyproject.toml`. **No edits to lines 14, 47, 390** — those remain `_TODO` until P4 lands. **Test extension** `tests/integration/test_readme.py`: new `TestContributingAndLicenseFooter` class (~50 LoC, **8 forcing-function tests**, mirrors DOC9a's two-pin shape): `test_contributing_section_is_filled` — `_h2_section("Contributing")` doesn't contain `_TODO_` AND length > 200 chars. `test_contributing_section_references_mission_and_progress` — body contains both `MISSION.md` AND `PROGRESS.md` (the section's whole purpose is to point readers at the loop's two source-of-truth files). `test_contributing_section_names_loop_driven_posture` — body contains case-insensitive `loop` substring. Pins the project's "Claude Code autonomous build loop" identity. `test_contributing_section_says_external_prs_are_not_workflow` — body contains case-insensitive `pull request` OR `external` substring (one-of-two disjunction tolerates both natural phrasings). Forces the section to call out the no-strangers-with-PRs convention. `test_license_section_is_filled` — `_h2_section("License")` doesn't contain `_TODO_` AND length > 50 chars. `test_license_section_names_proprietary` — body contains case-sensitive `Proprietary` substring (canonical capitalisation matching `pyproject.toml`). `test_license_matches_pyproject` — opens `pyproject.toml` from disk; asserts the exact `license = { text = "Proprietary" }` substring is present. Same docs↔source consistency-pinning posture as DOC9a's `test_pdf_choice_matches_pyproject`. Forces a docs update on a future swap of `pyproject.toml`'s licence field. `test_no_silent_open_source_license_in_readme` — 4-way absence pin against `MIT`, `Apache`, `GPL`, `BSD`. Forces a future PR that swaps the licence to open-source to *either* update `pyproject.toml` (in which case the README+pyproject must still agree) *or* update `PROGRESS.md` "Proposed scope changes" + `MISSION.md` first. **Helper reuse**: `_h2_section(heading)` (already added in DOC9a at line 36) sliced both Quick Links and Tech Stack; DOC10 reuses the same helper for `## Contributing` and `## License`. No new helpers needed. **No `app/` change**. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **First-run pass on all 8 tests.** **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing prose**: `pyproject.toml` line 7 is `license = { text = "Proprietary" }`; `MISSION.md` §10 ("Files this loop maintains") is at lines 220-226; `MISSION.md` §8 ("How to work") describes the iteration flow (lines 185-208); existing `## How this gets built` section at lines 394-409 already names the loop's flow + the human touch-points; `loop.sh` exists at repo root. **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests read the on-disk `README.md` + `pyproject.toml` and pin the resolved state. | 68 | OAuth1-DC — Drift-catcher integration tests pinning `_dev-login` ↔ `google_callback` parity | `a333b49` | Smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #1 closer. The originally-queued OAuth-stub Playwright walk has a technical flaw: Playwright's `context.route` only intercepts browser-issued requests, so it cannot intercept the server-side `oauth.google.authorize_access_token(request)` call inside `google_callback` (that's an `httpx`-level call from FastAPI to Google's token endpoint). A genuine real-route Playwright walk requires server-side Authlib stubbing (~150-200 LoC across a stub module + state/nonce echo + `id_token` JWT signing); too large for one iteration. **OAuth1-DC** is the smaller forcing-function step that pins the structural invariant *both routes share `upsert_user_from_userinfo`*, justifying the existing `tests/e2e/test_admin_users_e2e.py` walk via `_dev-login` as legitimate evidence for DoD #1's user-creation + pending-state + role-assignment chain. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #1's real-Google leg still needs either a server-side OAuth stub slice (queued in Next slice) or a human-driven sanity check with a real GCP project. **Scope** (`tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py` only — pure tests-only addition): added `TestDevLoginAndCallbackParity` class with 4 forcing-function tests at the bottom of the file. **No `app/` changes**. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Tests added** (4 new in `tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py`): `test_both_routes_call_upsert_user_from_userinfo` — source-text inspection via `inspect.getsource` of `auth_module.google_callback` and `auth_module.dev_login`; both must contain `upsert_user_from_userinfo(`. Catches a future PR that inlines user-creation logic on either side. Same posture as A2's `test_audit_coverage.py` cross-cutting source-text sweep. `test_callback_and_dev_login_produce_equivalent_user_row` — drives both routes with userinfo of the same shape (different sub + email per path because `users.email` is unique-constrained and `audit_log` immutability blocks DELETE — so we use distinct identities and assert equivalence on the load-bearing fields: name + role + status). Both paths must produce `(name=Drift Tester, role=None, status=PENDING)`. `test_callback_and_dev_login_produce_equivalent_audit_row` — runs both paths and asserts each produces exactly one `audit_log` row with `action="user.created"`, `entity_type="user"`, `before_json IS NULL`, and an `after_json` dict with the same KEYS across paths (the path-specific email value differs because the identities differ; the structural contract is the key set + the presence of `email`). Pins the audit-side parity that A2's coverage sweep alone doesn't pin (A2 only pins that *some* `record_audit` call happens, not that the shape matches across two routes). `test_callback_and_dev_login_set_session_user_id` — drives each path inside the same TestClient (clearing cookies between) and asserts that an immediate follow-up GET `/auth/me` returns 403 (`account not active`) for each path. The 403-vs-401 distinction is the observable: 401 means no session was set; 403 means the session resolved to a pending user. Both paths must hit 403 — confirms `request.session["user_id"]` is set in both branches. **Why this shape, not a full Playwright walk**: the existing `TestGoogleCallback` integration tests (2 tests) already cover `google_callback` with mocked Authlib. The existing `TestAuthMe` + `TestIndexPage` + `test_admin_users_e2e.py` tests cover `_dev-login` end-to-end. What was missing was a single forcing function that **pins the equivalence** of the two paths so a future PR can't silently drift one without the other. After OAuth1-DC, the test suite says: "if `_dev-login` works, the real OAuth callback works the same way for the same userinfo payload" — a property the e2e walks transitively rely on. **Why this doesn't tick DoD #1**: DoD #1 says "A new user can sign in with Google, land in pending state, and an Admin can assign them a role. They can then access the appropriate parts of the app." The phrase "with Google" is the unticked leg — the real OAuth round-trip with Google's authz + token + userinfo endpoints. OAuth1-DC pins the post-Google-userinfo path (which the existing tests already cover) but does not exercise the actual Authlib network calls to `accounts.google.com`. To tick #1, either: (a) a future slice adds a server-side OAuth stub that re-registers the google client with local stub URLs (`/auth/_test-stub/authorize` + `/token` + `/userinfo`) so a Playwright walk can drive `/auth/google/login` → real callback → real Authlib state validation → upsert chain end-to-end without external network; or (b) a human runs the flow against a real GCP test project (out of scope for the loop). **First-run failures**: 2 of the 4 tests failed on the first run — `test_callback_and_dev_login_produce_equivalent_user_row` + `test_callback_and_dev_login_produce_equivalent_audit_row` both hit `sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: users.email` because the initial design tried to reuse the same email across the two paths. Fixed by using distinct identity per path + asserting equivalence on the structural fields (name + role + status; key set in audit `after_json`) rather than the per-identity values. The fix sharpened the test's intent: parity is structural, not literal. **Lint fixes**: ruff flagged `RUF012` (mutable class default — `_USERINFO: dict[str, Any] = {...}`) → fixed by importing `ClassVar` and annotating `_USERINFO: ClassVar[dict[str, Any]]`. ruff flagged `PT018` (compound assertions — `assert row_a.before_json is None and row_b.before_json is None`) → split into separate asserts per ruff convention. **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests *are* the manual sanity check — they drive the actual route handlers with TestClient + monkeypatch + an isolated SQLite session, and the assertions pin the exact equivalence claim. Run as part of `make test` which is part of `make check`. **Tests** (4 new in `tests/integration/test_auth_routes.py`): the 4 listed above. **Total pytest 2414 passed + 3 skipped** (was 2410 passed + 3 skipped at iter-67 HEAD `343f955`, **+4**). Auth + admin-users e2e re-run clean (3/3 passed). Full e2e suite re-run (16/16 passed; no count change). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new app modules). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. DoD #1 still needs the real-OAuth leg — queued as the next slice with a corrected technical plan. |

DOC8 inherits 2402 as the true baseline and brings the total to 2410. Going forward the canonical anchor is `pytest -q | tail -1` against on-disk HEAD, not retcons of prior narratives. **Scope** (`README.md` + extension of `tests/integration/test_readme.py` only — pure docs slice): replaced the `_TODO` block under `### Reading the audit trail for an item` with a concrete walk-through anchored on the actual UI from `app/templates/admin_audit.html` + the read route in `app/audit_routes.py` (`GET /admin/audit` at `:89` + `?format=csv` branch at `:105-125` + `_AUDIT_CSV_HEADERS` at `:39-48`), the role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` at `:94` plus Admin's blanket pass via `app/auth.py`, the nav-link Manager+Admin visibility gate at `app/templates/base.html:175-183`, the immutability invariant via `apply_immutability_triggers` at `app/audit.py:128`, and the cross-cutting "every state change writes one row" guarantee enforced by `tests/integration/test_audit_coverage.py` (A2). ~3.4 KiB markdown replacing 1 line of `_TODO`. Walk-through shape: opening paragraph names audit trail as the system's append-only memory of every state-changing action; immutability per MISSION §9 (cannot be edited or deleted, even by Admin; corrections via new compensating movements); Manager + Admin only (Office + Workshop 403; nav link hidden for those two roles). → "**Every state change writes one row.**" paragraph: cross-cutting guarantee + reference to `test_audit_coverage.py` sweep enforcing it on every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE route in `app.routes`. Names the row shape: actor (signed-in user or `(system)` for background events) + ISO timestamp + action wire-name + `entity_type:entity_id` + before/after JSON dicts. → "**Browsing the log.**" 4 numbered steps: 1. Sign in as Manager (or Admin). 2. Click **Audit** in nav (or `/admin/audit`). 3. Six-column newest-first paginated table at 50 rows/page (Time / Actor / Action / Entity / Before / After) — em-dash for null before/after; pagination semantics. 4. **Previous** / **Next** at bottom; over-page renders empty-table not 404. → "**Finding entries for a specific item.**" paragraph: explicitly names the v1 limitation (no per-item filter) + two workarounds: browser Cmd+F search on the paginated list; CSV export at `/admin/audit?format=csv` ignores pagination + lists the eight CSV columns (`id`, `created_at`, `actor_email`, `action`, `entity_type`, `entity_id`, `before_json`, `after_json`) + filtering on `entity_type=item` + `entity_id=<n>` or grepping `entity_type=stock_movement` JSON cells for `"item_id":<n>`. Forward-pointer to A1b backlog slice. → "**Action vocabulary.**" bulleted list of canonical wire-names: `item.created/updated/archived/unarchived`, `stock_movement.in/out/adjustment/transfer`, `stock_take.created/started/counted/committed`, `purchase_order.created/updated/sent/cancelled/received`, `checkout.created/returned`, plus a tail naming `supplier`, `location`, `taxonomy_node`, `taxonomy_field_def`, `user`, `item_unit` entity-types. → "**Immutability.**" closing paragraph: DB-level UPDATE + DELETE triggers (SQLite + Postgres) via `apply_immutability_triggers`; corrections by recording compensating adjustment movements with explanatory reasons. **Why this section eighth (and last in the workflows series)**: with PO send (DOC6) + PO receive (DOC7) documented, every state-changing surface in the app has a workflow walk-through that *writes* audit rows. DOC8 documents how to *read* them — the natural cross-cutting capstone that ties every prior workflow section together via the `audit_log`. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before committing.** Specifically verified: route `GET /admin/audit` at `app/audit_routes.py:89`; role gate at `:94`; `PAGE_SIZE = 50` at `:36`; CSV branch + headers list at `:39-48` + `:105-125`; over-page renders empty (no 404) at `:127-141`; nav-link Manager+Admin visibility gate at `app/templates/base.html:175-183`; six-column header at `app/templates/admin_audit.html:25-30`; em-dash for null before/after at `:45-48`; pagination Previous/Next at `:60-65`; immutability triggers via `apply_immutability_triggers` at `app/audit.py:128`; canonical wire-name action strings via `grep -rEn 'action="(item\|stock_movement\|purchase_order\|stock_take\|checkout)\.'` (`item.created` at `app/items.py:1162`, `stock_movement.in` at `app/movements.py:366`, `purchase_order.received` at `app/purchase_orders.py:1264`, `stock_take.committed` at `app/stock_takes.py:1121`, `checkout.created` at `app/checkouts.py:343`); 11 distinct `entity_type` values across `app/`. **Test extension** `tests/integration/test_readme.py`: new `TestReadingAuditTrailSection` class (~80 LoC, **8 forcing-function tests**, mirrors DOC7's 8-test shape): `test_section_is_filled` (no `_TODO_`, body length > 400 chars); `test_section_references_admin_audit_route` (`/admin/audit` substring); `test_section_names_manager_and_admin_roles` (both `Manager` + `Admin` — pinning both halves catches a future PR that narrows the role gate); `test_section_names_csv_export` (`/admin/audit?format=csv` literal — pinned to the literal path so a future rename of the route prefix or query param fails); `test_section_names_audit_columns` (case-sensitive `Time` + `Actor` + `Action` + case-insensitive `before` + `after` — pins four of the six column labels; `Entity` + remaining cells are not pinned by name to avoid over-locking); `test_section_names_immutability_invariant` (case-insensitive `append-only` + `cannot be edited` — pins MISSION §9's invariant); `test_section_names_canonical_audit_actions` (case-sensitive `item.created` + `stock_movement.in` + `purchase_order.received` + `stock_take.committed` — pins the developer-facing wire-name vocabulary); `test_section_explains_no_item_filter_limitation` (case-insensitive `filter` + `CSV` + at-least-one-of `cmd+f`/`ctrl+f`/`cmd-f` — forces the v1 limitation to be called out + the two workaround paths to be documented). **First-run pass on all 8 tests.** No iteration needed (unlike DOC7's `/receive` first-run failure). The cross-checking discipline before writing prose is now stable. **No `app/` change**. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests read the on-disk `README.md` and pin the section is filled in with the correct content. | 66 | DOC7 — README "Receiving stock against a PO" workflow walk-through | `696e0a5` | Smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #12 closer. Continues DOC1-DOC6's pattern: fills the seventh of 7 `_TODO` sections under "Common workflows" ("Receiving stock against a PO") — the last unfilled workflow section other than `### Reading the audit trail for an item` (DOC8). DoD #12 wording mentions documenting workflows generally; PO-receive is the natural follow-up to DOC6 (PO send) since receiving is the FIFO-cost-layer-creating leg of MISSION §3's "Reorder and purchase orders" + "Cost tracking" sections. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #12 still needs DOC8 (audit-trail) + deploy section (gated on P4) + contributing/license. **Iteration-65 e2e flake recovered:** the iteration-65 commit `e608a66` failed `make check` with a 30s Playwright timeout in `test_office_reads_dashboard_with_low_stock_widget`'s second `_admin_promote` call. Re-running both isolated and within the full e2e suite passed cleanly (5.24s isolated; 20.83s full e2e; 21.56s full check). Treated as a transient flake — no code change needed; flake-risk logged in self-critique notes for the next iteration. **Test count anchor reset:** iteration 65's PROGRESS.md "Current state" line claimed `pytest 2394 unit+integration ✓ (+7 DOC6 forcing-function tests)` and rationalised that iteration 64's 2371 figure understated by 16. But on-disk `.loop_logs/check_65.log` shows `2378 passed, 3 skipped` — meaning iter-64-baseline was correctly 2371 and DOC6's `+7` brought it to 2378 cleanly. Iteration 65 invented a false +16 in prose. Refreshed "Current state" this iteration to the truth: 2386 = 2378 (true iter-65 baseline) + 8 (DOC7). **Scope** (`README.md` + extension of `tests/integration/test_readme.py` only — pure docs slice): replaced the `_TODO` block under `### Receiving stock against a PO` with a concrete walk-through anchored on the actual UI from `app/templates/purchase_order_receive_form.html` + the receive routes in `app/purchase_orders.py` (`GET /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/receive` at `:1110` + `POST .../receive` at `:1137`, `_RECEIVABLE_STATUSES = (SENT, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED)` at `:1059-1062`, status-flip rule at `:1257-1258`, audit shape at `:1261-1272`). ~35 lines of markdown replacing 1 line of `_TODO`. Walk-through shape: opening paragraph names FIFO-cost-layer creation as the load-bearing receive consequence (each line received → one **in** stock movement + one cost layer with `source = po_receipt` at the actual unit cost), explicitly distinguishes the **actual unit cost** (creates the FIFO layer; what stock valuation is calculated against) from the **expected unit cost** on the PO line (gets emailed to the supplier; *not* authoritative for valuation). Names Office as the primary role per MISSION §3.103 + DoD #6 (Manager + Admin also pass). Names the `purchase_order.received` audit row carrying before/after status + per-line `(line_id, received_qty, actual_unit_cost, movement_id)` and the cross-link to `/admin/audit`. → "Only `sent` and `partially_received` POs can be received against." paragraph: explicitly names the receivable statuses, explains the receive-button visibility gate via `is_receivable` at `purchase_order_detail.html:116`, and notes the link is hidden on draft/received/cancelled. → 5 numbered steps: 1. Sign in as Office. 2. Open PO detail page. 3. Click **Receive against this PO** (or visit `/admin/purchase-orders/{id}/receive` directly). The form lists every line's SKU / Qty ordered / Already received / Outstanding plus two per-line inputs (Receiving now + Actual unit cost). 4. Fill the inputs — blank/0 for lines that didn't arrive yet; cost defaults to expected, override on supplier-invoice mismatch; cost is required when receiving > 0. 5. Click **Record receipt** → app records **in** movements + creates FIFO cost layers + increments cumulative qty_received. 6. Status flips to **received** (every line fully received) or **partially_received** (any line still outstanding) based on cumulative state across every line. → "**Over-receipt is rejected.**" paragraph: cumulative `qty_received + Receiving now > qty_ordered` → 400 with line ID + offending qty; pragmatic v1 path is a manual stock-in for the overage (also creates FIFO layer). → "**An all-zero submit is a no-op.**" paragraph: blank/0 across every line writes no movement, no layer, no audit row, no status change; flashes _"PO #{id} — no receipts entered."_. **Why this section seventh**: with PO send (DOC6) documented, every prerequisite for receiving is now in the README (a user must have a sent PO before they can receive). **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before committing.** Specifically verified: receivable statuses tuple at `app/purchase_orders.py:1059-1062`; role gate `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE))` at `:1114`/`:1141` plus Admin's blanket pass via `app/auth.py`; receive routes at `:1110`/`:1137`; per-line form-field naming `received_<line_id>` + `cost_<line_id>` at `:1177-1180`; over-receipt rejection at `:1185-1193`; all-zero no-op flash at `:1248-1249`; FIFO-cost-layer source `CostLayerSource.PO_RECEIPT` at `:1233`; cumulative-state status flip rule at `:1257-1258`; `purchase_order.received` audit shape at `:1261-1272`; receive-button visibility gate at `purchase_order_detail.html:116`; form column labels at `purchase_order_receive_form.html:25-34`. **Test extension** `tests/integration/test_readme.py`: new `TestReceivingStockAgainstPOSection` class (~70 LoC, **8 forcing-function tests** — plan said "~7" but enumerated 8 by name; ship truth is 8): `test_section_is_filled` (no `_TODO_`, body length > 400 chars); `test_section_references_admin_purchase_orders_route` (`/admin/purchase-orders` substring); `test_section_references_receive_path` (`/receive` substring — pinned independently of the prefix so a future rename to `/record-receipt` fires a granular failure); `test_section_names_office_role` (`Office`); `test_section_names_receivable_statuses` (`sent` case-insensitive + `partially_received` case-sensitive — pins the `_RECEIVABLE_STATUSES` tuple); `test_section_names_fifo_cost_layer_creation` (`FIFO` case-sensitive + `cost layer` case-insensitive — pins MISSION §3 Cost-tracking wiring); `test_section_names_actual_vs_expected_cost` (case-insensitive `expected unit cost` + `actual unit cost`, mirrors DOC6's same-named test); `test_section_names_partial_vs_full_status_flip` (`received` case-insensitive + `partially_received` case-sensitive, pins the two-branch outcome at `:1257-1258`). Same forcing-function shape as DOC6's `TestGeneratingPurchaseOrderSection`. **First run failed on `test_section_references_receive_path`** because the README walk-through described the **Receive against this PO** button click but never the literal URL path `/receive`. The test caught it (exactly the forcing-function purpose). Fix: added "(or visit `/admin/purchase-orders/{id}/receive` directly)" parenthetical to step 3, mirroring DOC4's `/scan/item/{id}` direct-link pattern. A user debugging the form load can now bookmark the URL — small UX win on top of the test pass. **`partially_received` substring overlap between two tests is intentional** — `test_section_names_receivable_statuses` pins the status as a *precondition* for receipt; `test_section_names_partial_vs_full_status_flip` pins it as an *outcome* of receipt. Both invariants need their own forcing-function. A future PR that drops `partially_received` from the docs would fail both tests with two distinct error messages — the granularity is worth the substring redundancy. **No mention of `purchase_order.received` audit-action wire-name in the test set.** The audit-coverage sweep in `test_audit_coverage.py` already pins that the route writes the row (cross-cutting integration test), and per-route tests in `test_purchase_orders_routes.py` pin the action name (runtime assertion). A README forcing-function on the wire-name would over-pin a brittle string the user doesn't see in the UI. **No `app/` change**. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests read the on-disk `README.md` and pin the section is filled in with the correct content. The existing `test_purchase_orders_routes.py` (~150+ tests) covers Office/Manager → receive form → submit → status flip end-to-end across the role + status-guard + over-receipt + atomic-validation + all-zero-no-op + audit-shape + FIFO-cost-layer matrices, so the README walk-through is verifiably accurate against current behaviour. **Tests** (8 new in `tests/integration/test_readme.py`): the 8 listed above. **Total pytest 2386** (was 2378 baseline; **+8**). 16 e2e (no count change — pure docs slice; e2e would re-test what the integration forcing-function tests pin from a higher cost). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new app modules). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. DoD #12 has 1 workflow section (audit trail) + deploy + contributing + license remaining; DOC7 closes 1 of those. |

Click **Draft PO from this supplier** → `POST /admin/reorder/draft-po` → 303 redirect to `/admin/purchase-orders/{id}` (status **draft**, one PO line per low-stock item, qty + cost defaulted from item + last-receipt). 5. Blocker prose for missing supplier + archived supplier (verbatim copy from `reorder_dashboard.html:19,23`). → "**Editing the draft.**" paragraph: edit form on detail page (Expected date, Notes, per-line Qty ordered, per-line Expected unit cost), **Save changes** button. Names the **expected unit cost is what gets emailed; actual unit cost is entered at receipt time and creates the FIFO cost layer** distinction (load-bearing for MISSION §3 Cost-tracking comprehension; deferred to DOC7's flow). → "**Cancelling a draft.**" paragraph: `Cancel this draft` button → status `cancelled`; read-only after. → "**Downloading the PDF.**" paragraph: `Download PDF` link visible on every status except cancelled. → "**Sending the PO.**" 2 numbered steps: 1. Confirm supplier has **email**; blocker prose if not (verbatim from `purchase_order_detail.html:99`). 2. Click **Send to supplier** → builds email with PDF attached → sends via configured `EMAIL_BACKEND` (`console` dev / `smtp` prod) → status flips to **sent**, `sent_at` recorded, `purchase_order.sent` audit row written; re-clicking is rejected. → "**Browsing past POs.**" paragraph: `/admin/purchase-orders` with status filter (`all` / `draft` / `sent` / `partially_received` / `received` / `cancelled`); CSV download link respects the filter. **Why this section sixth**: with supplier + category + item + scan + stock-take documented, every prerequisite for purchase orders is now in the README. POs reference suppliers + items, so a future user reading the README in order needs both before this section. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before committing.** Carried forward DOC4 / DOC5's "did I just promise something that doesn't exist?" lesson. | 64 | DOC5 — README "Running a stock take" workflow walk-through | `6f7b57f` | Smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #12 closer. Continues DOC1 + DOC2 + DOC3 + DOC4's pattern. The README has 7 `_TODO` sections under "Common workflows" — DOC5 fills the fifth one ("Running a stock take"). DoD #12 wording explicitly names "**how to do a stock take**" as a required workflow — DOC5 is the section that closes that wording at the README-prose level. **No DoD ticked** — DoD #12 needs every piece (run / test / deploy / SSO / supplier / item / stock take, plus PO send / PO receive / audit-trail workflows + contributing/license). **Scope** (`README.md` + extension of `tests/integration/test_readme.py`): replaced the `_TODO` block under `### Running a stock take` with a concrete walk-through anchored on the actual UI from `app/templates/stock_takes_list.html` + `stock_take_form.html` + `stock_take_detail.html` + the route source in `app/stock_takes.py`. ~30 lines of markdown replacing 1 line of `_TODO`. Walk-through shape: opening paragraph names the FIFO-cost-engine integration (variances → adjustment movements via the same engine; positive adjustments need unit cost + create cost layers; negative adjustments consume FIFO oldest-first), names Office as the primary role per MISSION §3.103 + DoD #5 (Manager + Admin also pass; Workshop cannot run stock takes). Names the three derived statuses: **scheduled** → **in_progress** → **completed**, plus the four audit actions (`stock_take.created` / `started` / `counted` / `committed`). → "**Schedule a stock take.**" 6 numbered steps: 1. Sign in as Office. 2. Click **Stock takes** in nav (or `/admin/stock-takes`). 3. Click **New stock take**. 4. Pick a **Scope** — All items / Category (with `↳` prefix for sub-categories) / Location. 5. Pick a **Scheduled for** date (required) + optional notes. 6. Click **Schedule stock take** → list reappears with the new row in the **Open** tab; **Completed** tab for history. → "**Start counting.**" paragraph: View → row + click **Start counting**. System snapshots `current_qty` for every active in-scope item into `StockTakeLine` rows — the snapshot is the baseline for variance, so a count that lags real movements stays internally consistent. → "**Enter counts.**" paragraph: count table per line; **Counted qty** input drives **Variance = counted − system** column; counts are sparse (no-op writes write no audit row); **Counted / Uncounted / With variance** progress summary at top. → "**Commit the variances.**" paragraph: Commit form appears once at least one line has non-zero variance. **Positive variance** lines need a **Unit cost** (defaults to most recent receipt price); each commit creates one `ADJUSTMENT` movement + a new FIFO cost layer at the entered cost. **Negative variance** lines show `—` for unit cost; the cost engine consumes the oldest open FIFO layers automatically and records each `cost_layer_consumption`. Click **Commit count**. **Atomic-on-error rollback** for negative-variance shortages — the whole commit is rolled back, no movements are written, no layers are touched, the stock take stays `in_progress`, and the page re-renders with the typed unit costs preserved and an error block naming the offending SKU. → "**Audit trail.**" trailing paragraph: every committed adjustment carries `stock_take_id` FK on `StockMovement` so `/admin/audit` lets a Manager trace any adjustment back to the count session. The `stock_take.committed` audit row records the per-movement snapshot. **Why this section fifth**: with supplier + category + item + scan documented, every prerequisite for stock takes is now in the README. DoD #5 is the only ticked DoD that has *no* docs section yet (DoD #2/#3/#4 each get partial coverage from DOC2/DOC3/DOC4); DOC5 is the only DoD #12 sub-slice that directly closes a DoD-named workflow ("how to do a stock take") at the prose level. **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before committing** — carried forward DOC4's "did I just promise something that doesn't exist?" lesson. Specifically verified: the four audit actions all exist at `app/stock_takes.py:738`/`:844`/`:953`/`:1121`; `system_qty=item.current_qty` snapshot semantics at `:829`; the negative-variance atomic rollback re-renders with `commit_form_values=raw_unit_costs` preserved at `:1083-1093`; the `data-direction` attr at `stock_take_detail.html:190`; the **All items** / **Category** / **Location** vocabulary matches the radio labels at `stock_take_form.html:23/31/48`; the role gate is `require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE)` plus Admin pass-through (`app/auth.py:164`); the `stock_take_id` FK on `StockMovement` at `:1051`. **Test extension** `tests/integration/test_readme.py`: new `TestRunningAStockTakeSection` class (~70 LoC, 7 forcing-function tests) reading `README.md` from disk: `test_section_is_filled` (no `_TODO_`, body length > 400 chars — comparable to DOC2/DOC3 since stock takes have a multi-step lifecycle); `test_section_references_admin_stock_takes_route` (`/admin/stock-takes` substring); `test_section_names_office_role` (`Office` substring — pins MISSION §3.103); `test_section_names_scope_options` (`Category` + `Location` + `All items` substrings — pins the user-facing radio-button labels rather than the wire `node`/`location`/`all` enum values, which is what readers actually see); `test_section_names_variance_and_commit_chain` (`variance` + `commit` case-insensitive — pins the load-bearing chain); `test_section_names_fifo_engine_wiring` (`FIFO` case-sensitive — pins the cost-engine wiring per ST3, since negative variances consuming FIFO is non-obvious); `test_section_names_audit_trail_link` (`audit` case-insensitive — pins the link back to A1's `/admin/audit` surface). Same forcing-function shape as DOC4's `TestPrintingQrAndScanningSection`. **No mention of "ST cancel route"** — backlog item; section documents only what's shipped (per DOC4's lesson). **No mention of CSV export** on the stock-takes list — R5c's link is on the list page but the walk-through stays focused on the running-a-stock-take flow. **No `app/` change**. mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the integration tests read the on-disk `README.md` and pin the section is filled in with the correct content. The existing `test_stock_takes_routes.py` (~120 tests) covers Office → schedule → start → count → commit end-to-end across the role + status-guard + validation + atomic-rollback + audit-shape matrices, and `test_stock_takes_e2e.py` exercises the full chain in Chromium (re-run this iteration; green), so the README walk-through is verifiably accurate against current behaviour. **Tests** (7 new in `tests/integration/test_readme.py`): the 7 listed above. **Total pytest 2371** (was 2364, **+7**). 16 e2e (no count change — pure docs slice; e2e would re-test what the integration forcing-function tests pin from a higher cost). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new app modules). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick. Keyboard fallback for missing/unreadable labels. → "**How a code resolves.**" paragraph: `_resolve_code` looks up `qr_code` first, then `sku`; QR-coded item wins on accidental cross-column collisions; unknown codes redirect back to `/scan` with a flash. → "**The action picker on `/scan/item/{id}`.**" paragraph: 303 redirect from successful resolve, scan input still focused for next-item flow, action picker (Stock out qty / Stock in qty + unit cost — creates new FIFO cost layer / Adjust direction + qty + optional unit cost + required reason), Check out → link for `requires_checkout` items. → "**Archived items still resolve.**" trailing paragraph: scan still resolves to scan page, action forms hidden, note redirects to items list — keeps physical labels working without re-opening the archive on the audit trail. **Why this section fourth**: with supplier + category + item documented, every downstream workflow reaches an item. Documenting a non-existent surface would violate DoD #12's "written for someone who has never seen the project". The existing `test_suppliers_routes.py` covers Manager → New supplier → form → create → archive → unarchive end-to-end, so the README walk-through is verifiably accurate against current behaviour. **Tests** (5 new in `tests/integration/test_readme.py`): the 5 listed above. **Total pytest 2343** (was 2338, **+5**). 16 e2e (no count change — pure docs slice; e2e would re-test what the integration forcing-function tests pin from a higher cost). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new app modules). **DoD impact**: no DoD tick.
