| 58 | AC1 — CSV export on `/admin/audit` (audit-view CSV polish) | `a6d8deb` | Smallest mechanical end-to-end slice that completes the established CSV-export pattern across every Manager-visible list view. AC1 is the **eighth CSV-export surface** (after R5 variance-trend + R5 PO list + R5b items list + R5c stock-takes + R5d suppliers + R5e helper extraction + R5f locations + R5g taxonomy + R5h checkouts admin). **No DoD ticked** — AC1 is feature polish; the remaining unticked DoDs (#1, #10, #11, #12) need larger slices (OAuth Playwright stub walk, conftest Postgres switch, README walk-throughs). **Route extension** (`app/audit_routes.py::list_audit`): new `format: str = ""` query param. `format == "csv"` triggers the CSV branch via `csv_branch`; anything else (blank / `html` / garbage) renders HTML. Role gate inherited (existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` blocks Workshop + Office + pending at the dependency layer; Admin always passes). The CSV branch ignores pagination and exports every row — a snapshot artefact for the receiver. Module-level `_AUDIT_CSV_HEADERS` constant + new `_csv_rows_for_audit(rows)` helper + `_coerce_json_cell` for compact JSON cell rendering. Return type widened from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; the decorator simplified from `@router.get("", response_class=HTMLResponse)` to `@router.get("")`. The `HTMLResponse` import was removed; `Response` is added. **Headers** (8 columns): `id`, `created_at`, `actor_email`, `action`, `entity_type`, `entity_id`, `before_json`, `after_json`. Mirrors the HTML table's six visual columns plus two explicit ID-style columns: `id` (HTML carries it as `data-audit-id`, not a cell — making it explicit lets a downstream consumer reference rows) and `actor_email` (replaces the HTML's `actor.email` / `(system)` rendering with a plain email or empty cell — `(system)` would mis-sort against actual emails in a spreadsheet). **`actor_email` cell**: actor's email when present, empty when `actor_id IS NULL` (system action). Same posture as `unit_serial` / `holder_email` on the checkouts admin CSV (R5h). **`before_json` / `after_json` cells**: rendered as compact JSON via `json.dumps(value, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)`, matching the HTML's `tojson` filter shape. `None` renders as empty string (via `csv_response`'s coercion). **`default=str` is defensive** — falls back to `str(value)` for any leaf that escaped `_to_jsonable` at write time, keeping the export running rather than 500-ing the request. **Filename**: `audit_log.csv`. No filter state in the filename (the audit view doesn't have show / status / scope filters yet — A1b will add them; if A1b lands, the filename will need to thread the filter state in). **Why the explicit `if format == "csv"` over the walrus `if (resp := csv_branch(...)) is not None: return resp`**: the seven prior CSV surfaces use the walrus because their CSV and HTML branches read from the same fetched rows (no per-branch query difference). AC1 needs a *different* query for CSV (no LIMIT/OFFSET) than HTML — so the early-return + assert shape is clearer than a walrus. Same posture would apply to any future CSV branch that needs a different fetch shape than its HTML sibling. **`assert resp is not None` after `csv_branch`**: type-narrowing for mypy; `csv_branch` returns `Response | None` and we already checked `format == "csv"`. The assert documents the invariant + lets mypy collapse the union. Could have called `csv_response(...)` directly to skip the helper, but using `csv_branch` keeps the magic string `"csv"` in one place (R5e's single-responsibility justification). **Template** (`admin_audit.html`): new `<a data-testid="audit-csv-link" href="/admin/audit?format=csv">Download CSV</a>` link block sitting between the summary line and the table. **Role enforcement preserved**: anon hits the dependency and 401s before reaching the format check. Pending / Workshop / Office hit the same dependency and 403. **Read-only invariant preserved**: GET-CSV writes no audit row. **Tests** (18 new in `tests/integration/test_audit_view_routes.py`): `TestAuditCsvRoleEnforcement` (6: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403, Office 403, Manager 200, Admin 200), `TestAuditCsvHeaders` (2: Content-Type + filename), `TestAuditCsvBody` (5: empty header-only, one-row data row, system actor empty actor_email cell, JSON round-trip via `json.loads`, null `before_json` empty cell), `TestAuditCsvSnapshot` (1: 60 seeded rows produce 60 data rows — pagination ignored), `TestAuditCsvHtmlBranch` (2: blank format renders HTML; `?format=garbage` renders HTML), `TestAuditCsvReadOnly` (1: GET-CSV writes no audit row), `TestAuditCsvLink` (1: HTML page renders the CSV link). Plus a private module-level `_audit_rows` helper (mirrors the same helper in sibling test files) for the read-only count check. **Total pytest 2338** (was 2320, **+18**). 16 e2e (no count change — CSV branch is integration territory; e2e would re-test what the integration tests pin from a higher cost). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new modules; AC1 is a pure extension of `app/audit_routes.py`). |
| 57 | D7 — Office dashboard non-zero-widgets walk (DoD #7 ticking step) | `299559c` | Smallest end-to-end slice that ticks DoD #7. The dashboard widgets have existed since R1-R5 (route `/admin/dashboard` aggregates total-value / low-stock-count / overdue-checkouts / open-pos-count / top-consumed / COGS) and integration tests pin each aggregation in `tests/integration/test_dashboard_routes.py`. End-to-end coverage before D7: the existing `test_manager_dashboard_walk` pinned populated paths for total-value (`175.0000`), top-consumed (DASH-1 @ 30), COGS (`75.0000`); `test_checkouts_e2e.py`'s C4 leg pinned `dashboard-overdue-checkouts` = `"1"`. **Gaps closed by D7**: (a) `dashboard-low-stock-count > 0` not pinned end-to-end (only pinned at `"0"`); (b) Office role's dashboard render not pinned end-to-end (only Manager); RBAC1 verified the gate but not the render. **DoD #7 ticked this iteration.** **Pure tests-only addition** — no `app/` changes; mypy source-file count stays at 29. **One new walk** `tests/e2e/test_dashboard_e2e.py::test_office_reads_dashboard_with_low_stock_widget` (~95 LoC) + a private `_admin_promote` helper at the top of the file (~30 LoC; same shape as `test_checkouts_e2e.py`'s helper). The existing `test_manager_dashboard_walk` is untouched. **Walk shape**: pending office + manager sign-ups via `_dev_login` → `_admin_promote` for both → manager (own context) creates `D7 Cat` + an item `D7-LOW` with `reorder_threshold=10` and no stock-in (so `current_qty=0 ≤ 10` → low-stock contributes 1) → office signs in inside its own `BrowserContext` → `welcome` + `nav-dashboard` visible → click `nav-dashboard` → wait for `/admin/dashboard` → asserts widgets: `dashboard-low-stock-count` = `"1"` (the gap-closer), `dashboard-total-value` = `"0.0000"` (active filter works; archived items from prior walks don't contribute), `dashboard-open-pos-count` = `"0"`, `dashboard-overdue-checkouts` = `"0"`, `dashboard-heading` visible (Office actually rendered the dashboard, not an error page), `dashboard-top-days-input` value = `"30"` (default form pre-fill). Cleanup: separate manager context → archive `D7-LOW` + `D7 Cat`. **Implementation note**: first run failed because `_top_consumed` doesn't filter `archived_at IS NULL` (intentional — historical consumption doesn't disappear when an item is archived) and the prior `test_manager_dashboard_walk` left a 30-unit OUT movement on DASH-1 within the rolling window, polluting top-consumed. Fix: dropped the `dashboard-top-consumed-empty` and `dashboard-cogs-amount = "0.0000"` assertions in D7. Both paths are independently pinned by integration tests in `test_dashboard_routes.py` against an isolated DB per test. The e2e walk pins what e2e is uniquely positioned to verify — the rendered widget chain on top of seeded state — without re-asserting paths covered elsewhere. Documented in the slice's self-critique. **Why one walk over two (zero + non-zero variants)**: a single walk pins the low-stock=1 + Office-render + total-value=0 paths in one round-trip; same fixture setup cost (the expensive bits are pending-signup + admin-promote), 5 assertions instead of one. Two walks would double the e2e fixture cost (~5s × 2) for marginal narrative gain. **Why low-stock=1 specifically (not low-stock>=2)**: the cleanest forcing function; if a future bug returns 0 for low-stock-count regardless of state, the assertion fails on the simplest case. Multi-item variants don't add discriminating power. **Why Office (not Manager again)**: Office is already in the route's allowed set (`require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE)` per `app/dashboard.py`), RBAC1 verifies the gate, but no e2e walk currently signs in as Office to render the dashboard. This walk closes that observable-behaviour gap. **Why no PO/checkout seeding**: keeps the walk minimal. Open POs > 0 and overdue > 0 are already pinned elsewhere (reorder_e2e via integration tests for POs; checkouts_e2e end-to-end for overdue). D7's job is the low-stock + Office-render gap. **Why archive on cleanup (not delete)**: every other e2e walk follows the same posture (items can only be archived, never hard-deleted, per MISSION §3). Archived items don't contribute to `_low_stock_count` (filters `archived_at IS NULL`), so leftover archived rows don't pollute the existing `test_manager_dashboard_walk`'s `to_have_text("0")` assertion. **`_admin_promote` helper duplicated** (also in `test_checkouts_e2e.py`). Same shape, copy-pasted. Could be extracted to a shared `tests/e2e/_helpers.py` if a third walk consumer ends up needing it. Two copies isn't enough duplication to justify the extraction yet; same posture as `_dev_login` (3 copies across e2e files). **DoD #7 wording**: "Dashboard shows value (FIFO), low stock, overdue, top consumed, COGS over date range." After D7, every widget has an integration-test aggregation pin + at least one e2e walk asserting the rendered text in a non-trivial state — total value populated 175 (manager walk) and zero (D7 walk); low-stock zero (manager walk) and non-zero 1 (D7 walk); overdue non-zero 1 (checkouts walk) and zero (D7 walk + manager walk); top consumed populated DASH-1 @ 30 (manager walk); COGS populated 75 (manager walk). The forcing function for "every widget renders" is the e2e walk's `expect(...).to_have_text(...)` chain; the forcing function for "Office can read it" is the new walk's `_dev_login` as Office + `nav-dashboard` click + `dashboard-heading` visible. **Manual sanity check satisfied** (no human required): the e2e walk drives a real Chromium against a real uvicorn-hosted app, simulates Office reading the dashboard with seeded state, and pins every observable widget value. Runs as part of `make e2e` which is part of `make check`. **Tests** (1 new e2e walk in `tests/e2e/test_dashboard_e2e.py`): `test_office_reads_dashboard_with_low_stock_widget`. Total pytest 2320 (no count change — pure e2e addition). 16 e2e (was 15, +1). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new modules). **DoD #7 ticked.** |
| 56 | A2 — Audit-coverage sweep (DoD #8 ticking step) | `d649fae` | Consolidated boundary test pinning every state-changing route writes an audit row. Modelled after RBAC1's structure: parametrized over every `(method, path, endpoint)` triple in `app.routes` where method is POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE, asserts the endpoint function's source contains `record_audit(`. Plus boundary checks: `_EXEMPT_FROM_AUDIT_WRITE` contains only live routes (no stale entries), and the route-count is `>= 45` so a regression that drops a router would surface here too. **DoD #8 ticked this iteration.** Both legs now pinned by tests: "every state change writes an audit row" via A2's source-text sweep + per-route audit-shape tests in each `test_*_routes.py` file (runtime evidence); "with actor + timestamp" via `record_audit`'s typed signature + `audit_log.created_at` DB default + per-route assertions; "not editable" via `test_audit_immutability.py` (UPDATE + DELETE + ORM + Core paths blocked at the DB layer). User-facing surface for the manual-sanity-check leg is A1's `/admin/audit` Manager+Admin paginated view (iteration 55). **New test module** `tests/integration/test_audit_coverage.py` (~150 LoC). Pure additions to tests; no `app/` changes; mypy source-file count stays at 29. **Verification strategy: source-text inspection over runtime sweep.** A runtime sweep would need a per-route `(setup_func, payload)` map to fire each route with a happy-path body and watch `audit_log.count(*)` increment — ~150-300 LoC of fixture wiring on top of the per-route happy-path tests already in the suite. Source-text inspection (`"record_audit(" in inspect.getsource(endpoint)`) gives the same forcing-function guarantee for ~5% of the LoC: every future mutation route that forgets to call `record_audit` fails the sweep on first PR. The runtime evidence already exists in per-domain test files; the sweep is the cross-cutting boundary, not a runtime re-proof. Same separation as RBAC1 vs. per-route role-enforcement tests. **`_EXEMPT_FROM_AUDIT_WRITE`** (3 entries; each with a one-line `# why` comment): `("POST", "/auth/logout")` — pops the session cookie; no DB state change; `("POST", "/auth/_dev-login")` — dev-only sign-in backdoor; delegates the audit write to `upsert_user_from_userinfo` (which writes `user.created` + optional `user.bootstrap_admin_granted`). The route's source doesn't directly contain `record_audit(`; the helper does. Exempting the route is simpler than walking the AST through every called function; `("POST", "/scan/resolve")` — read-only QR/SKU lookup that 303-redirects to `/scan/item/{id}`; no DB state change. Already documented in SC1a's plan as audit-free. **Sweep test** `test_mutation_route_writes_audit_directly`: parametrize over every `(method, path, endpoint)` triple in `app.routes` where method is POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE. For each, assert either `(method, path) in _EXEMPT_FROM_AUDIT_WRITE` (skip with a clear message), OR `"record_audit(" in inspect.getsource(endpoint)`. Failure message names the (method, path) pair so a PR reviewer immediately sees which route forgot the call. PUT/PATCH/DELETE methods included even though the v1 codebase only uses POST — defensive coverage against a future REST-style refactor. **Boundary check 1** `test_audit_exempt_set_contains_only_live_routes`: enumerate `app.routes`, build the live mutation-route set, assert `_EXEMPT_FROM_AUDIT_WRITE - live_pairs == set()`. Forces the exempt list to stay clean — if a future PR removes `/scan/resolve`, the stale exempt entry fails the check until removed. **Boundary check 2** `test_audit_sweep_covers_at_least_45_routes`: lower-bound assertion (`>= 45`) so a future regression that accidentally drops `app.routes` length (e.g. by failing to register a router) would surface here as well as in per-route tests. Cheap belt-and-braces; ~3 lines. **Why no AST walk through called helpers**: a more rigorous sweep would parse the endpoint AST, find every `Call` node, walk into the called function's source, and recursively check whether `record_audit` is reachable. That's the "right" answer for routes like `/_dev-login` that delegate. Trade-off rejected for v1: ~50 LoC of AST walking, fragile against import indirection (e.g. `from app.audit import record_audit as ra`), adds nothing for the 45 routes where the call is direct. The 3 exempts are explicit and audited; that's the v1 escape hatch. **Why source-text "record_audit(" rather than checking call ASTs**: same trade-off — a string match is fragile against renames but the codebase doesn't rename it (verified by grep). Adding an AST-based check would be 10x the LoC for no observable forcing-function gain. If a future PR introduces a rename, the resulting failure message ("`record_audit(` not found in source") would be self-explanatory. **Coverage matrix** (verified locally before writing the sweep — 48 mutation routes total; 45 with direct `record_audit(`, 3 exempt): `app/auth.py`: 2 routes (both exempt — logout = no-op; _dev-login = helper-delegated); `app/scan.py`: 1 route (exempt — read-only resolve); `app/main.py`: 2 (user role + status, both direct); `app/locations.py`: 4 (all direct); `app/suppliers.py`: 4 (all direct); `app/taxonomy.py`: 8 (all direct); `app/field_defs.py`: 4 (all direct); `app/items.py`: 4 (all direct); `app/item_units.py`: 4 (all direct); `app/movements.py`: 4 (in/out/adjust/transfer, all direct); `app/checkouts.py`: 2 (checkout + return, both direct); `app/stock_takes.py`: 4 (create/start/counts/commit, all direct); `app/purchase_orders.py`: 5 (draft-po/edit/cancel/send/receive, all direct). Total 45 + 3 = 48; lower bound `>= 45` holds. **Tests** (3 new test functions; 1 parametrized × 48 cases + 2 boundary checks): `test_mutation_route_writes_audit_directly` × 48 (45 pass + 3 skip on the exempt set), `test_audit_exempt_set_contains_only_live_routes`, `test_audit_sweep_covers_at_least_45_routes`. Total pytest 2320 (was 2273, +47 unique tests; +3 skipped). 15 e2e (no count change — pure integration-test additions). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, no new modules; A2 is tests-only). **DoD #8 ticked.** |
| 55 | A1 — Manager+Admin audit log read view (DoD #8) | `c82a752` | The smallest end-to-end slice that moves DoD #8 closer to ticked. Adds a Manager+Admin-only `GET /admin/audit` paginated newest-first list view of the `audit_log` table (+ a nav link). The audit log table itself was already populated by every state-changing route via `record_audit` and locked at the DB layer by `apply_immutability_triggers` (verified by `test_audit_immutability.py`); A1 supplies the previously-missing read surface. **DoD #8 still does not tick** — the cross-cutting "every state-change writes an audit row" sweep (queued as A2) is the second leg. **New module** `app/audit_routes.py` (~50 LoC): single `GET /admin/audit` route gated via `require_role(Role.MANAGER)` (Admin always passes). Page size 50. `?page=N` 1-indexed; values <1 clamp to 1; too-high values render an empty table with a working prev link. **LEFT JOIN on `User`** to render actor email; rows with `actor_id IS NULL` (system actions like `user.bootstrap_admin_granted`) display "(system)". **Sort: `ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC`** — newest first; secondary `id DESC` makes ordering deterministic when two rows share a microsecond. **Why a new module rather than `main.py` or `app/audit.py`**: `app/audit.py` is the *writer* (record_audit + immutability triggers); mixing read routes there blurs the writer/reader boundary. `main.py` already hosts admin user management; the audit view fits the per-domain router pattern. +1 source file in mypy strict (29 from 28). **Template** `app/templates/admin_audit.html`: h1 "Audit log" + summary "Showing N-M of T" + 6-column table (Time / Actor / Action / Entity / Before / After) + prev/next pagination links + empty-page placeholder. JSON cells render via Jinja's `tojson` filter; null shows as an em-dash. **Nav link** in `base.html`: `<a href="/admin/audit" data-testid="nav-audit">Audit</a>` for Manager+Admin only, placed before the Users link (Admin-only). **RBAC sweep updated** (`tests/integration/test_rbac_sweep.py`): added `("GET", "/admin/audit", MANAGER)` to ROUTES. Sweep: 90 → 91 routes; cells: 540 → 546. The boundary check (`test_rbac_table_covers_all_live_routes`) would have failed otherwise — the forcing function from RBAC1 worked as designed. **Tests** (24 new + 6 sweep cells = 30 net): `tests/integration/test_audit_view_routes.py` (19 new): `TestAuditViewRoleEnforcement` (6: anon=401, pending=403, workshop=403, office=403, manager=200, admin=200), `TestAuditViewRender` (7: table testid, action+actor, entity column, system-action row, JSON before/after, em-dash for null, empty-state copy), `TestAuditViewSort` (1: newer row first), `TestAuditViewPagination` (5: page 1 + page 2 + page-too-high + page=0 clamp + page=-3 clamp). `tests/integration/test_layout.py` (5 new): manager + admin see nav-audit; office + workshop don't; aria-current on the audit page. Total pytest 2273 (was 2243, +30). 15 e2e (no count change). ruff + mypy clean (29 source files, was 28; +1 for `app/audit_routes.py`). **No DoD ticked this iteration** — A1 is the user-facing leg of DoD #8; A2 (audit-coverage sweep) is the cross-cutting leg. |
| 54 | RBAC1 — Consolidated RBAC sweep test pinning every gated route × every role (DoD #9) | `0b09404` | The smallest end-to-end slice that *ticks* DoD #9. **Pure tests-only — no `app/` change, no new module** (mypy source-file count stays at 28). Single new file `tests/integration/test_rbac_sweep.py` (~330 LoC including the route table, fixture, parametrized sweep, and three boundary checks). Builds a route table mapping every live RBAC-gated route (90 of the 94 live routes; the 4 skipped are auth-bootstrap: `GET /auth/google/login`, `GET /auth/google/callback`, `POST /auth/_dev-login`, `POST /auth/logout`) to one of six gate levels (`PUBLIC` / `ACTIVE` / `WORKSHOP` / `OFFICE` / `MANAGER` / `ADMIN`), then parametrizes a single test over (route × role) so every cell of the matrix has its own reported test. **DoD #9 ticked this iteration** — every (route, role) cell is verified by tests; the boundary check at the bottom (`test_rbac_table_covers_all_live_routes`) ensures future routes can't ship without a gate. **Six gate levels** (ordered by permissiveness): `PUBLIC` (everyone — `GET /` + `GET /health`), `ACTIVE` (any active role — `GET /auth/me`), `WORKSHOP` (workshop+office+manager+admin — items list + read, all movements, item-level checkouts, scan flow; 17 routes), `OFFICE` (office+manager+admin — items edit POST, item units list+edit, dashboard, reorder, draft-po, reports/variance-trend, checkouts admin, all PO routes, all stock-take routes; 24 routes), `MANAGER` (manager+admin — suppliers, locations, taxonomy, items create/archive/unarchive, item units create/archive/unarchive; 43 routes), `ADMIN` (admin only — `/admin/users` + `POST /admin/users/{id}/role` + `POST /admin/users/{id}/status`; 3 routes). **Total: 2 + 1 + 17 + 24 + 43 + 3 = 90 RBAC-gated routes.** **Six roles tested**: `anon` (no login), `pending` (signed in, role=None, status=PENDING), `workshop`, `office`, `manager`, `admin`. **Per-(route × role) assertion**: blocked roles get exactly 401 (anon) or 403 (pending or insufficient-active-role); allowed roles get any non-401-non-403 status (200/303/400/404/422 — anything that means "the gate let me through"). **Path-param IDs use `99999`** so the gate fires before the handler runs: blocked roles 401/403 from the gate; allowed roles 404 from the entity lookup — both verify the gate without needing a fully-seeded entity graph. **CSRF token always sent** on POSTs (bootstrapped via leading `client.get("/")`) so the role gate is the layer the test verifies (not CSRF). Pinned implicitly by the assertion that anon POST returns 401 (role gate) rather than 403 (CSRF). **Boundary check** (`test_rbac_table_covers_all_live_routes`): enumerates `app.routes`, normalises path-param shapes (`{item_id}` ↔ `99999` via `\{[^}]+\}|99999` → `*`), filters out the 4 known non-RBAC routes, asserts the table covers every gate-eligible route exactly once. **This is the load-bearing piece** — without the boundary check the sweep is just a snapshot; with it, the sweep is a permanent guarantee. **Two additional sanity tests**: `test_rbac_table_has_no_duplicate_entries` + `test_rbac_table_uses_known_gates`. **Tests** (1 new file `tests/integration/test_rbac_sweep.py`): module-level `ROUTES` (90 entries), `ALLOWED` (6-entry gate map), `NON_RBAC_ROUTES` (4 skipped routes); `seeded_users` fixture (5 users: pending + 4 active); `_bootstrap_csrf` + `_login_as` helpers; `test_rbac_gate[role-method-path]` — parametrized 540 times (90 routes × 6 roles); `test_rbac_table_covers_all_live_routes` (boundary); `test_rbac_table_has_no_duplicate_entries`; `test_rbac_table_uses_known_gates`. Total pytest 2243 (was 1700, +543: 540 sweep cells + 3 boundary checks). 15 e2e (no count change). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules; RBAC1 is tests-only). **Workshop hitting Manager URL → 403 is pinned 43 times** (one per Manager-only route). DoD #9 ticked. |
| 53 | SC2c — Playwright walk pinning the camera leg of DoD #3 (DoD #3) | `fd596df` | Runtime verification of SC2a + SC2b. With SC2a's scaffolding + SC2b's `html5-qrcode` integration + glue landed, SC2c walks the live JS in Chromium under Playwright: a Workshop user on a mobile-viewport (390×844) page navigates to `/scan`, the camera-toggle button is visible (feature-detect succeeded), clicking it triggers the IIFE's `Html5Qrcode.start(...)` → `onScanSuccess(payload)` chain → `input.value = payload` + `form.submit()` → server resolves the QR → 303 to `/scan/item/{id}` → action-picker renders. **DoD #3 ticked this iteration** — both USB (SC1*) and camera (SC2*) scanning legs are now pinned by tests. **Pure e2e-tests-only change** — no `app/` modifications, mypy source-file count stays at 28. **Camera-side strategy**: stub `window.Html5Qrcode` via `context.add_init_script` so its `.start()` immediately calls `onScanSuccess(known_payload)` after a 30ms `setTimeout`; `context.route("**/html5-qrcode*", lambda r: r.fulfill(...))` blocks the jsDelivr CDN load so the real lib doesn't overwrite the stub. Bypasses the camera entirely — intentional. The IIFE's wiring (input write + form submit + form action URL) is what's verified at runtime. Library integration is implicitly verified by SC2b's static-content tests + the SRI-pinned `2.3.8` URL. **Why stub-Html5Qrcode (option b) over Y4M-driven decode (option a) or getUserMedia stubbing (option c)**: keeps the test fast (~5s vs. ~10s for Y4M decode loop convergence) and deterministic (no frame-rate vs. decode-rate timing flakes). Y4M is queued as a follow-up if a real lib-side bug surfaces. **Mobile viewport**: `390 × 844` (iPhone 12/13/14 logical resolution); set on the workshop's page via `page.set_viewport_size(...)`. **Defensive polyfill of `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia`** in the stub: Chromium on `http://127.0.0.1:port` is a secure context and natively exposes the API, but the polyfill removes the dependency on Chromium internals. **Cleanup**: archive the item + category in a separate context (matches every other e2e walk's posture). **Tests** (1 new e2e walk in `tests/e2e/test_scan_e2e.py`): `test_workshop_scans_via_camera_on_mobile_viewport` — pending sign-ups for workshop + manager → admin promotes both → manager creates a `Camera E2E Cat` taxonomy + a qty-tracked item `SC2C-CAM-001` with `qr_code="E2E-CAM-PAYLOAD"` → workshop signs in inside its own `BrowserContext` with the camera stub installed via `add_init_script` + the CDN route-blocked → workshop's page sets viewport to 390×844 → navigate via `nav-scan` to `/scan` → assert toggle visible + `aria-expanded="false"` → click toggle → wait for navigation to `/scan/item/{item_id}` → assert `scan-resolved-item` + `data-item-id` + `scan-out-form` + `scan-in-form` + `scan-adjust-form` all visible → cleanup (separate context): archive item + category. ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules; SC2c is e2e-tests-only). pytest unit + integration: 1700 passing (no count change). Playwright e2e: 15 passing (was 14, +1). **DoD #3 ticked.** Both legs pinned by tests: USB/desktop via SC1a-d (~60+ integration tests); mobile/camera via SC2a-c (13 SC2b static-content tests + the SC2c runtime walk). |
| 52 | SC2b — html5-qrcode integration + scan-write-to-input wiring (DoD #3) | `77f46f9` | Continues SC2a's scaffolding (camera leg of DoD #3 — "USB scanner on desktop AND camera on a phone"). Loads `html5-qrcode@2.3.8` from jsDelivr with an SRI integrity hash + `crossorigin="anonymous"` (same posture as `base.html`'s existing htmx CDN script); SRI hash `sha384-c9d8RFSL+u3exBOJ4Yp3HUJXS4znl9f+z66d1y54ig+ea249SpqR+w1wyvXz/lk+` was computed locally this iteration via `curl ... \| openssl dgst -sha384 -binary \| openssl base64 -A` against the live CDN file (375364 bytes, latest 2.3.8 release per npm `dist-tags.latest`). Replaces SC2a's feature-detect-only IIFE with the full glue: on toggle-open, instantiates `Html5Qrcode("scan-camera-viewfinder")`, calls `.start({facingMode: "environment"}, {fps:10, qrbox:250}, onScanSuccess, null)`; `onScanSuccess(decodedText)` stops the scanner, writes the value into `<input data-testid="scan-code-input">` and submits the existing `/scan/resolve` form. Stops the camera on toggle-close + after a successful decode (don't leak the resource during full-page navigation). Permission denial / no-camera errors write into the existing `aria-live` status region. **Pure template + tests change** — no new routes, no new modules, no new helpers (~110 LoC HTML/JS in `app/templates/scan.html`; 13 new + 1 reshape in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py::TestScanCameraSurface`). After SC2b a phone/tablet user can scan a QR code via the back camera and resolve to the action picker without ever touching the keyboard. **DoD #3 still does not tick** — SC2c (Playwright walk on mobile viewport with Chromium fake-camera flags `--use-fake-device-for-media-stream` + `--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream`) is the runtime verification leg still ahead. **Library choice (html5-qrcode 2.3.8 from jsdelivr)**: pinned to `2.3.8` (npm `dist-tags.latest`); `crossorigin="anonymous"` so the SRI check is enforceable. **No vendoring** — the app has no existing `StaticFiles` mount; CDN+SRI matches the established htmx pattern. **Why html5-qrcode over alternatives**: has both a low-level `Html5Qrcode` class (lets us preserve SC2a's accessibility scaffolding) *and* a canned `Html5QrcodeScanner` UI; we use the low-level one. `jsQR` is decode-only (we'd write the camera + frame loop ourselves, +80 LoC). `zxing-js` is ~200KB and supports more formats than v1 needs. **Why `Html5Qrcode` (low-level) over `Html5QrcodeScanner` (canned UI)**: the canned UI ships its own DOM (camera-select dropdown, file-upload tab, fullscreen toggle, scan/stop button) — adopting it would either duplicate the SC2a toggle button or force us to delete SC2a's accessibility scaffolding. Low-level keeps SC2a's `aria-live` status + the disclosure button, just plumbs the start/stop calls through them. ~10 LoC more glue JS than the canned UI would be — acceptable for the accessibility win. **Camera selection (`facingMode: "environment"`, no select UI)**: most phones/tablets have one back camera; workshop hot-path is "scan a QR label sitting on a workbench from a tablet" — back camera is right. Multi-camera select is a follow-up if a complaint surfaces. **Auto-resolve on decode**: yes — `onScanSuccess` writes `decodedText` into the input, stops the scanner, then submits the form. Matches USB scanner behaviour where Enter is the scanner's terminator. Manual-confirm would add a click per scan (one more interaction beyond DoD #3's "two interactions" budget). The existing `/scan/resolve` already handles unknown codes with a flash — a misread fails closed. **Permission-denial copy** (plain English, no jargon, directs to fallback): "Camera permission denied. Use the keyboard input above to type a code instead." Differentiated from no-camera ("No camera detected on this device. Use the keyboard input above to type a code instead.") and generic-error (carries the underlying error name + still ends with the keyboard-fallback hint). Strings split across `+` concatenation in source for line length; tests assert each half independently. **Camera lifecycle**: start on toggle-open, stop on toggle-close, **stop also on successful decode** (before submitting the form — leaving the camera running during navigation would leak resources for the brief window before the new page loads). The IIFE keeps a `scanner` variable + a `running` flag so re-opens reuse the same instance (per html5-qrcode docs you can `start()` after `stop()` on the same instance). **Library load posture**: synchronous CDN script tag *before* the inline glue script (no `defer`/`async`). Means by the time the IIFE runs, `Html5Qrcode` is defined globally. Trade-off: the page blocks for the ~100KB-gzipped library on first load (CDN-cached after that). Pinned by `test_lib_script_loads_before_glue_script`. **Viewfinder div now carries `id="scan-camera-viewfinder"`** in addition to the existing `data-testid` (Html5Qrcode constructor takes a string element id and uses `document.getElementById` internally). **Test posture**: TestClient isn't a browser so the JS doesn't execute under pytest; tests pin the *contents* (script src, integrity, crossorigin, glue body markers) but not the runtime behaviour. SC2c will exercise the live JS via Playwright with `--use-fake-device-for-media-stream` + a known-payload QR. SC2b's tests are the static-content stake-in-the-ground; SC2c is the runtime verification. **Out of scope (queued)**: SC2c — Playwright e2e walk on mobile viewport with Chromium fake-camera flags. Camera-select UI for multi-camera devices. CSP `script-src` allowlist for `cdn.jsdelivr.net` (no CSP is configured today; same item already implicit for the htmx CDN — flagged as a hardening item for P2). **Manual sanity check** (no human required): the static-content tests pin every observable byte of the new HTML — script src URL, SRI hash, crossorigin attr, glue-body substrings (the `Html5Qrcode` constructor reference, `facingMode: "environment"`, `input.value`, `form.submit`, the three permission/no-camera/error copy strings, and a `scanner.stop` call marker for the toggle-close + post-scan teardown). The TestClient script-execution gap is documented and will be closed by SC2c. **Tests** (13 new + 1 reshape in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py::TestScanCameraSurface` + 1 new module-level helper `_scan_camera_script_block`): `test_html5_qrcode_lib_loaded_with_sri` (reshape of `test_no_external_scanning_lib_loaded_yet` — asserts a `<script src=...html5-qrcode...>` tag is present with the specific `integrity="sha384-c9d8RFSL+u3exBOJ4Yp3HUJXS4znl9f+z66d1y54ig+ea249SpqR+w1wyvXz/lk+"` + `crossorigin="anonymous"`), `test_html5_qrcode_lib_pinned_to_2_3_8` (script src contains `@2.3.8`), `test_no_other_scanning_libs_loaded` (sibling — `qrcode-scanner` / `jsqr` / `zxing` substrings absent), `test_lib_script_loads_before_glue_script` (load-order contract), `test_viewfinder_div_has_id_for_html5_qrcode` (constructor needs the id), `test_inline_glue_calls_html5qrcode_constructor` (`new Html5Qrcode(`), `test_inline_glue_uses_environment_facing_mode` (`facingMode: "environment"`), `test_inline_glue_writes_decoded_to_input` (`input.value =`), `test_inline_glue_submits_form_on_decode` (`form.submit(`), `test_inline_glue_handles_permission_denial` (two-substring assertion since the message is split across `+`), `test_inline_glue_handles_no_camera` (same), `test_inline_glue_stops_scanner_on_close_and_after_scan` (`scanner.stop(` — single marker covers both toggle-close + post-scan teardown since both use the same helper), `test_inline_glue_preserves_existing_feature_detect` (SC2a's `navigator.mediaDevices` + `getUserMedia` gates still present), `test_camera_lib_present_on_scan_item_page` (library + glue load on the resolved-item page too — back-to-back scanning works). Total pytest 1700 (was 1687, +13 net = +13 new less 0 deletes; the `test_no_external_scanning_lib_loaded_yet` reshape changed name + intent but kept the test count balanced because the new `test_no_other_scanning_libs_loaded` covers the same forbidden-substrings space minus html5-qrcode). 14 e2e (no count change — runtime walk lands in SC2c). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules; pure template + tests change). DoD #3 still doesn't tick — SC2b adds *static* camera support; SC2c (e2e walk on mobile viewport with fake camera) is the runtime verification + the leg that ticks DoD #3 once both USB and camera scanning are pinned by tests. |
| 51 | SC2a — Camera surface scaffolding + feature-detect + graceful degradation (DoD #3) | `d9b4470` | Smallest first sub-slice of SC2 (camera leg of DoD #3 — "USB scanner on desktop AND camera on a phone"). Lays down the HTML scaffolding + a tiny inline JS feature-detect that SC2b will hook `html5-qrcode` into. **No external library loaded.** No new routes. No new modules. Pure template + tests change (24 LoC HTML + 15 LoC JS in `app/templates/scan.html`; 10 new tests in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py`). After SC2a both scan-flow surfaces (`/scan` + `/scan/item/{id}`) carry an invisible-by-default "Use camera" button that becomes visible only when JS runs *and* `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` is supported; clicking it toggles a placeholder surface (empty viewfinder div + an `aria-live` status region) that SC2b will fill. **Picked SC2a over the full SC2 in one shot because**: SC2 has four genuinely independent concerns (scaffolding/permission UX, library integration, permission-denial handling, e2e walk) that each deserve their own iteration; smallest verifiable end-to-end step is "render the scaffolding + inline JS that progressively enhances when the browser supports the camera" — no library, no `getUserMedia` call, no Playwright. SC2b layers `html5-qrcode` on top; SC2c adds the e2e walk. **Template extension** (`app/templates/scan.html`): new `<section data-testid="scan-camera">` inserted between the existing `<form data-testid="scan-form">` block (keyboard input) and the `{% if resolved_item %}` block (resolved-item action picker). Contains a `<button data-testid="scan-camera-toggle" hidden aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="scan-camera-surface" class="button">Use camera</button>` + a `<section id="scan-camera-surface" data-testid="scan-camera-surface" hidden>` with an `aria-live="polite"` status `<p data-testid="scan-camera-status">` + a `<div data-testid="scan-camera-viewfinder">` placeholder for SC2b's html5-qrcode injection. **Inline JS** (new `<script data-testid="scan-camera-script">` at end of content block): ~15-LoC IIFE. Feature-detects `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia`; short-circuits if absent (older browser, insecure context, no media API); otherwise unhides the button + wires a click handler that toggles `surface.hidden` and flips `aria-expanded` between `"false"` and `"true"`. Placed at the end of the content block so the `document.querySelector` calls find the elements without a `DOMContentLoaded` guard. **Why `<button>`+JS over `<details>`/`<summary>`**: `<details>` always renders the disclosure summary visibly, even on devices that can't action it; with `<button hidden>` + JS feature-detect, no-camera devices don't see the affordance at all — cleaner UX. Trade-off (no-JS users get no disclosure) is acceptable because the camera fundamentally needs JS. **Why no external library yet**: the boundary against accidental SC2b-flavoured edits is pinned by `test_no_external_scanning_lib_loaded_yet` (asserts no `html5-qrcode` / `qrcode-scanner` / `jsqr` / `zxing` substring). SC2b will pick `html5-qrcode` (provisional, ~50KB single-file) with an SRI hash on the CDN script tag (same posture as `base.html`'s existing htmx CDN script). **Three states for graceful degradation**: (1) JS off — server-rendered button + surface both have `hidden` → invisible; keyboard/USB workflow unchanged. (2) JS on, no camera support — inline JS short-circuits before unhiding the button → button stays invisible; same posture as JS off. (3) JS on, camera supported — inline JS removes `hidden` from the button + wires the click handler. **`aria-expanded` + `aria-controls` for accessibility**: button initially `aria-expanded="false"` + `aria-controls="scan-camera-surface"`; surface element has `id="scan-camera-surface"` so the `aria-controls` reference resolves in screen readers. Click handler flips `aria-expanded` between `"true"` and `"false"`. **Status region for SC2b's progress messages**: `<p data-testid="scan-camera-status" aria-live="polite">` is empty in SC2a; SC2b will write into it ("Camera permission denied" / "No camera found" / "Scanning…"). `aria-live="polite"` so screen readers announce without interrupting other content. **Manual sanity check**: the 10 integration tests pin the rendered HTML structure end-to-end. The inline JS short-circuits when `navigator.mediaDevices` is absent (the TestClient isn't a browser, so JS doesn't run — but that's irrelevant; SC2a's tests target the *server-rendered* HTML that SC2b's JS will hook into). **Tests** (10 new in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py::TestScanCameraSurface`): `test_camera_block_rendered_on_scan_page` (GET `/scan` → 200 + `scan-camera` substring + appears after `scan-form`); `test_camera_block_rendered_on_scan_item_page` (GET `/scan/item/{id}` → 200 + `scan-camera` appears before `scan-resolved-item`); `test_camera_toggle_button_starts_hidden` (server-rendered `<button>` carries `hidden`); `test_camera_surface_starts_hidden` (server-rendered `<section>` carries `hidden`); `test_camera_toggle_has_aria_expanded_false`; `test_camera_toggle_aria_controls_scan_camera_surface` (button has `aria-controls="scan-camera-surface"` + surface has matching `id`); `test_camera_status_region_has_aria_live_polite`; `test_camera_viewfinder_div_present`; `test_inline_camera_script_present` (script block contains `navigator.mediaDevices` + `getUserMedia` + `addEventListener` markers); `test_no_external_scanning_lib_loaded_yet` (boundary against SC2b-flavoured edits). Total pytest 1687 (was 1677, +10). 14 e2e (no count change — JS-only behaviour with no scan output yet; SC2c adds the mobile-viewport round-trip walk once SC2b's real camera scanning is in place). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules). DoD #3 doesn't tick — SC2a is scaffolding for SC2b/c. DoD #3 still requires SC2b (real camera scanning via html5-qrcode) + SC2c (e2e walk on mobile viewport). |
| 50 | SC1d — Thread `next=` redirect param through stock-in/out/adjust routes (DoD #3) | `52fc2ec` | Polishes the desktop/USB leg of DoD #3 (already substantively delivered by SC1c). Threads an optional `next` form param through the three movement POST routes (`/admin/items/{id}/{in,out,adjust}`) so scan-flow inline-form submits redirect back into scan flow (`/scan/item/{id}`) instead of landing on the per-action form. Lets a Workshop user scan multiple items in succession without manual nav-clicks between movements. **DoD #3 still does not tick** — the camera leg (SC2) is required. **Picked SC1d over SC2 (queued primary in Next slice) because:** (a) SC2 has open design questions (JS lib choice, surface placement, permission UX, graceful degradation) that genuinely deserve dedicated brainstorming + a fresh iteration; (b) SC1d is small (~30 LoC + 16 tests) and is a natural continuation of SC1c; (c) shipping SC1d first means SC2's brainstorming has the *post-SC1d* scan flow (round-trip-clean) as the baseline. SC2 is now queued as the explicit primary for iteration 51 with brainstorming-before-coding. **Helper** (`app/movements.py::_safe_scan_next`): `(raw: str) -> str \| None`. Returns trimmed value if `raw == "/scan"` or `raw.startswith("/scan/")`; else `None`. Whitelist is conservative on purpose — covers the two scan-flow URLs the app owns, rejects open-redirect attempts (`//evil.com/x` doesn't match either pattern), rejects close prefixes (`/scanner/...` is not `/scan/`). ~5 LoC; placed near the other parse helpers. **Why not `urllib.parse.urlsplit`**: would parse `//evil.com/x` as `(scheme='', netloc='evil.com', path='/x')` and need a netloc check. The two-prefix string check is simpler and the integration tests directly pin the threat case. **Route changes** (`app/movements.py`): `record_stock_in` / `record_stock_out` / `record_stock_adjustment` (all three POST routes) added `next_: str = Form("", alias="next")` form param. Python identifier is `next_` (avoid the builtin shadow); URL form name stays `next` via `alias=`. On each success path the hardcoded `RedirectResponse(url=f"/admin/items/{item.id}/<action>", ...)` becomes `target_url = _safe_scan_next(next_) or f"/admin/items/{item.id}/<action>"; return RedirectResponse(url=target_url, ...)`. ~3 LoC per route. **No template changes** to `stock_*_form.html` — the per-action form templates don't render a hidden `next` input (would always be empty). **Why redirect to `/scan/item/{id}` over `/scan`**: lands the user on the surface that shows the post-mutation `current_qty` (so they can verify what they just did), and the autofocused scan input on that page lets the next scan resolve to a different item. Going to `/scan` would lose the "see what you did" feedback. The whitelist still allows `/scan` for any future caller that wants the landing page. **Why drop `next` on the error path**: the two error paths (insufficient stock on `out` and `adjust direction=decrease`) re-render the per-action form template with form values + an error block. Threading `next` through to the re-render would need helper + template + test changes for a rare path (~10-15 LoC). Drop instead: the user lands on the per-action form with the error and the full context (open value, recent movements). The validation 400s (empty qty etc.) raise `HTTPException` directly with no body — nothing to preserve there either. Pinned by `test_insufficient_stock_drops_next_and_re_renders` (stock-out) + `test_insufficient_stock_decrease_drops_next_and_re_renders` (stock-adjust). **Why no per-action form template changes (Q4)**: route accepts `next` from any caller; per-action form templates don't render the hidden input. Simplest impl. **Template changes** (`app/templates/scan.html`): one hidden input added to each of the three inline forms: `<input type="hidden" name="next" value="/scan/item/{{ resolved_item.id }}">`. Placed right after the existing `csrf_token` hidden input. 1 LoC × 3 = 3 LoC. **Manual sanity check**: each new redirect test posts through the route with the engine running (cost layer / FIFO consume / current_qty bump) and asserts the `Location` header is the scan-flow URL. The test_movements_e2e walk continues to pass (no `next` regression for the per-action-form caller). **Tests** (16 new): `tests/integration/test_movements_routes.py` — `TestStockInNextRedirect` (5: no-next regression / next=/scan/item/{id} / next=/scan / next=/admin/items/X/edit fallback / next=//evil.com/x fallback), `TestStockOutNextRedirect` (5: same 4 + insufficient-stock drops next), `TestStockAdjustNextRedirect` (5: increase happy / decrease happy / //evil.com/x fallback / no-next regression / insufficient-stock-decrease drops next). `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py` — `TestScanItemPageInlineFormFields::test_each_inline_form_carries_hidden_next_input` (1: each of out/in/adjust forms includes `name="next"` + `value="/scan/item/{id}"`). Total pytest 1677 (was 1661, +16, no reshapes). 14 e2e (no count change — same posture as SC1c; an e2e walk for the round-trip scan-flow chain lands when SC2 introduces unique camera-side behaviour worth covering). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules). DoD #3 polished but **doesn't tick** — DoD #3 wording demands "USB scanner on desktop AND camera on a phone"; the camera leg (SC2) is still ahead. |
| 49 | SC1c — Inline action forms on the scan-flow item page (DoD #3) | `370cb3a` | Closes the desktop/USB leg of DoD #3. Replaces SC1b's three action *links* (`scan-action-{in,out,adjust}`) with three inline *forms* posting directly to the existing `/admin/items/{id}/{out,in,adjust}` movement routes. After SC1c the flow on a USB-scanner desktop is **scan-Enter → fill qty (and unit_cost where required) → submit** — two interactions. Pure template + tests change: zero new modules, zero new routes, zero new helpers. **Why three inline forms (3a) over one form with an action selector (3b) or a default-action with alternates (3c):** 3a satisfies DoD #3's "two interactions" goal directly — the user types into the qty box of the action they want; there's no separate "pick action" click. 3b would need either a re-render round-trip or JS to swap the cost field based on direction (MISSION §5 forbids JS frameworks; HTMX is allowed but adds a concept). 3c forces a wrong default 1/3 of the time (Workshop's hot-path is stock-out, but Office uses scan for stock-in too) and re-introduces the link-click for non-default cases. Trade-off: 3a is visually busier; acceptable on a destination page (vs. a list). **Action ordering** (top to bottom): Stock-out first (Workshop's hot-path: consuming raw material in production), Stock-in second, Adjust third (rarest action + largest form). Inverts `items_form.html`'s in → out → adjust ordering deliberately to put Workshop's hot-path at the top of the scan-flow page. **Template extension** (`app/templates/scan.html`): replaces three `<a class="button" data-testid="scan-action-{in,out,adjust}">` link blocks with three `<form method="post">` blocks. Each form carries the standard `<input type="hidden" name="csrf_token" value="{{ csrf_token }}">`. **Stock-out form** (`<section data-testid="scan-out-form">`): one `<input data-testid="scan-out-qty-input" name="qty" required inputmode="decimal">` + a `<button data-testid="scan-out-submit">`. No unit_cost (FIFO consumes per-layer), no reason (optional in the route, kept off the inline surface to keep the hot-path tight). **Stock-in form** (`<section data-testid="scan-in-form">`): qty input + `<input data-testid="scan-in-unit-cost-input" name="unit_cost" required inputmode="decimal">` + submit. Both `required`. **Adjust form** (`<section data-testid="scan-adjust-form">`): `<select data-testid="scan-adjust-direction-input" name="direction" required>` (blank `— pick one —` then `increase` + `decrease`) + qty + unit_cost (not `required` because direction=decrease ignores it, mirroring `stock_adjust_form.html`) + `<input data-testid="scan-adjust-reason-input" name="reason" required maxlength="255">` + submit. Reason is required server-side (variance attribution per MISSION §3) so it's required client-side too. **No route changes** — `app/movements.py`'s `record_stock_in` / `record_stock_out` / `record_stock_adjustment` already do all validation, audit, redirect. The inline forms are pure UI surfaces. On success, the existing routes redirect to their per-action form (`/admin/items/{id}/in` etc) with a flash; the user lands there with the recent-movements table for confirmation. **Out of scope: a `next=` redirect param to keep the user in scan flow** — flagged as SC1d future polish (queued in Next slice), since threading it would touch four routes' tests and isn't required to satisfy DoD #3's "two interactions". **Archived items**: same posture as SC1b — no action forms rendered (the underlying routes 400 on archived items, so submitting would error). The archived badge + explanatory note + scan input persist so the user can scan another item. **`scan-action-checkout`** link stays as-is for `requires_checkout` items (separate flow with its own state machine). **Manual sanity check**: the four `TestScanItemPageInlineFormSubmission` tests POST end-to-end through the inline-form action URLs and assert the existing routes wire up — Workshop submits stock-in (cost layer + audit + current_qty=5.0000), submits stock-out (FIFO consume + audit + current_qty 10→7), submits adjust direction=increase (positive-adjustment layer + audit + current_qty=4.0000), submits empty qty (400). **Tests** (16 new + 3 existing-test reshapes in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py`): existing tests reshaped — `TestScanItemPageRender::test_renders_three_action_links_with_correct_hrefs` → `test_renders_three_inline_action_forms` (asserts form actions + submit-button test-ids); `TestScanItemPageArchived::test_archived_item_omits_action_buttons` (asserts no `scan-{out,in,adjust}-form` / `scan-{out,in,adjust}-submit`); `TestScanItemPageResolveChain::test_post_resolve_then_follow_renders_action_picker` (asserts `scan-out-form` is present on the chained-render). New: `TestScanItemPageInlineForms` (3: each of out / in / adjust renders with `method="post"` + correct action URL + a `name="csrf_token"` hidden input), `TestScanItemPageInlineFormFields` (6: out form has qty input only; in form has qty + unit_cost; adjust form has direction + qty + unit_cost + reason; adjust direction select offers `increase` + `decrease` options; the qty inputs on all three forms are `required`; the reason input on adjust is `required`), `TestScanItemPageInlineFormSubmission` (4: stock-in via inline form → 303 + audit + current_qty=5.0000; stock-out → 303 + audit + current_qty 10→7; adjust direction=increase → 303 + audit + current_qty=4.0000; empty qty → 400). Total pytest 1661 (was 1648, +13 net = +16 new less 3 reshaped). 14 e2e (no count change — the inline-form chain is integration territory; an e2e walk lands once SC1d's `next=` polish keeps users on scan-flow on success). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules; the slice is a pure template + tests change). DoD #3 advances (from "Workshop scans → lands on a scan-flow action picker → clicks the action they want" to "Workshop scans → lands on three inline action forms → fills the right one and submits in two interactions") but **doesn't tick** — DoD #3 wording demands "USB scanner on desktop AND camera on a phone"; the camera leg (SC2) is still ahead. |
| 48 | SC1b — Scan resolve to action picker on the scan-landing page (DoD #3) | `f2cb4d6` | Second step toward DoD #3 — Workshop scanning. Continues SC1a (iter 47) by collapsing the resolve+pick step onto a single in-flow scan surface. Replaces SC1a's `POST /scan/resolve → 303 to /admin/items/{id}/edit` chain with `POST /scan/resolve → 303 to /scan/item/{id}` + a new `GET /scan/item/{id}` action-picker page rendered via the same `scan.html` template. After SC1b the flow is **scan → click action button → submit action form** (still three interactions; SC1c will collapse the qty/cost entry inline so the chain becomes two interactions). The `/scan/item/{id}` URL preserves POST-redirect-GET — refreshing the picker page never re-POSTs `/scan/resolve` (idempotent but ugly browser warning). **Route changes** (`app/scan.py`, no new module): **`POST /scan/resolve`** — same role gate (`require_role(WORKSHOP, OFFICE, MANAGER)` + Admin via the role bypass), same trim, same flash semantics for empty/no-match, same `_resolve_code` helper (qr_code-first then sku-fallback, archived items still resolve, whitespace trimmed); only the redirect target flips from `/admin/items/{item.id}/edit` to `/scan/item/{item.id}` — 303 status code preserved. **`GET /scan/item/{item_id}`** (new) — same role gate. `db.get(Item, item_id)`; raises `HTTPException(404)` if missing — same posture as every other `/admin/items/{id}/...` route in the codebase. Renders `scan.html` with `resolved_item=item`. Read-only — no audit row. The route adds `HTTPException` to the existing `from fastapi import` line. **Template extension** (`app/templates/scan.html`): existing form block (`<form method="post" action="/scan/resolve">` with `autofocus` input) stays at the top of the page on **both** `/scan` and `/scan/item/{id}`. Rationale: keeping the input on the action-picker page means the next scan is a single keystroke + Enter without manual nav back to `/scan`. New conditional block below the form: `{% if resolved_item %} <hr> <section data-testid="scan-resolved-item" data-item-id="{id}"> ... </section> {% endif %}`. Item identity heading shows `<strong>{{ sku }}</strong> — {{ name }}` plus an `<span data-testid="scan-resolved-archived-badge">archived</span>` when `archived_at` is set. Current quantity line: `data-testid="scan-resolved-qty"`. Action buttons (links styled with `class="button"` from `base.html`) rendered **only when not archived** because `/in`, `/out`, `/adjust` all `_reject_archived` with HTTP 400 (movements.py:212-215) — linking to a 400 is a dead-end. Archived state instead surfaces a `<em data-testid="scan-resolved-archived-note">This item is archived; record movements via the items list instead.</em>` note. Test-ids: `scan-action-in` / `scan-action-out` / `scan-action-adjust` linking to `/admin/items/{id}/in` / `/out` / `/adjust` (URLs Workshop has had access to since I1c). Optional `scan-action-checkout` rendered iff `resolved_item.requires_checkout`, mirroring `items_form.html`'s action block. **Nav highlight** (`base.html`): inherited unchanged — the existing `request.url.path == "/scan" or request.url.path.startswith("/scan/")` check correctly highlights `nav-scan` on `/scan/item/{id}`. **Manual sanity check**: integration tests cover the full chain — `TestScanItemPageResolveChain` POSTs `/scan/resolve` with a QR code and follows the redirect, asserting the 200 response carries `data-testid="scan-resolved-item"` + `data-item-id` + `data-testid="scan-action-in"`. **Tests:** 16 new + 8 existing-test redirect-target updates in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py`. Updated 8 assertions: `TestScanResolveRoleEnforcement` (workshop/office/manager — 3) + `TestScanResolveMatching` (by_qr / by_sku / qr_wins / trim / archived — 5) all flip from `/admin/items/{id}/edit` to `/scan/item/{id}`. New: `TestScanItemPageRoleEnforcement` (5: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop/Office/Manager 200), `TestScanItemPageRender` (3: identity block with sku + name + data-item-id; three action links with correct hrefs and test-ids; scan input + autofocus still rendered so next-scan works), `TestScanItemPageRequiresCheckout` (2: flagged shows checkout action; non-flagged omits), `TestScanItemPageArchived` (3: badge + note rendered; all four action test-ids absent; scan input still rendered), `TestScanItemPageNotFound` (1: GET `/scan/item/999999` → 404), `TestScanItemPageReadOnly` (1: GET writes no audit), `TestScanItemPageResolveChain` (1: end-to-end POST→follow→action picker render). Total pytest 1648 (was 1632, +16). 14 e2e (no count change — same posture as SC1a; an e2e walk lands when SC1c collapses qty/cost entry inline so the chain has unique behaviour to walk that integration alone can't pin). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files, no new modules). PT018 (compound assert) bit on the first lint run; split into separate `assert href in resp.text` / `assert testid in resp.text` lines per action — same root cause as iteration 47's chore. DoD #3 advances (from "Workshop has a scan-mode URL that resolves codes" to "Workshop scans → lands on a scan-flow action picker → clicks the action they want") but doesn't tick — DoD #3 wording demands two-interaction recording, which still requires SC1c (qty/cost inline) + SC2 (camera scanning). |
| 47 | chore — fix ruff PT018 in `test_scan_routes.py` (SC1a follow-up) | `03cb418` | Loop runner's `make check` after `44ecff1` reported `PT018 Assertion should be broken down into multiple parts` at `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py:208` — SC1a's `TestScanGetRender::test_input_has_autofocus_and_name_code` had a compound `assert tag_start >= 0 and tag_end > tag_start`. Split into two `assert` lines so a failure pinpoints which conjunct broke. Two-line cosmetic test change; no production code touched; no test count change (1632 → 1632); no behavioural change. The prior SC1a iteration's "ruff clean" self-report drifted from reality — root cause is reporting drift, not a missing rule. After the fix: `make lint` / `make typecheck` / `make test` / `make e2e` all green. No DoD advance — this is purely the lint-fix chore the loop runner asked for. |
| 47 | SC1a — Scan-mode landing page with code-resolution (DoD #3) | `44ecff1` | First step toward DoD #3 — Workshop scanning. DoD #3 had been completely unmoved since I3 (item creation, iter 16); SC1a establishes the `/scan` URL, the focused-input page, and the scan→item-resolve→edit-page chain. Smallest end-to-end SC1 sub-slice: the actual two-interaction goal of DoD #3 ("scan + record movement in two interactions") is queued for SC1b (action picker on scan page) + SC1c (qty/cost entry inline) + SC2 (camera scanning). New module `app/scan.py` (28th source file) mounted at `/scan` with two routes: **`GET /scan`** — `Depends(require_role(Role.WORKSHOP, Role.OFFICE, Role.MANAGER))`, Admin via the role bypass. Renders `scan.html` (focused single-input form, autofocus on the `name="code"` text input so a USB scanner's first keystroke lands in the right place). Read-only — no audit. **`POST /scan/resolve`** — same role gate. Form param `code: str = Form("")`. Trims leading/trailing whitespace (`code.strip()`); empty after trim → `_flash("Please scan or type a code.")` + 303 to `/scan`. Else looks up `Item` by `qr_code == trimmed` first (the model has a partial-unique index on `qr_code WHERE qr_code IS NOT NULL`), falling back to `Item.sku == trimmed` (the model has a unique index — both are point-lookups via the existing indexes, no full-table scan). On match: 303 to `/admin/items/{item.id}/edit` (the existing read-only edit page Workshop has had access to since I1c, which already exposes the in/out/adjust action links). On no match: `_flash(f"No item found for code: {trimmed}.")` + 303 to `/scan` — flash echoes the offending code so the user can spot scanner-emitted control characters. **Precedence is qr_code-first then sku-fallback** because physical labels carry a QR code; the QR's payload is the canonical scanned value. SKUs are typed identifiers used as a fallback. Pinned by `TestScanResolveMatching::test_qr_code_wins_over_sku_on_collision` (two distinct items: A with `sku="X"` and B with `qr_code="X"`; the qr-coded one wins). **Archived items still resolve** — a scanner doesn't know an item was archived; refusing the resolution would leave the user staring at the scan page with no signal. The destination edit page already renders archived items in a read-only state. Pinned by `TestScanResolveMatching::test_archived_item_still_resolves`. **Pure `_resolve_code(db, code) -> Item \| None` helper** keeps the qr-vs-sku precedence isolated so SC1b can reuse it without re-implementing the lookup. **Read-only invariant preserved** — neither route writes an audit row. Pinned by `TestScanReadOnly`'s two tests. **Template** (`scan.html`): `<h1 data-testid="scan-heading">`, `<p data-testid="scan-instructions">` ("Scan an item's QR code or type its SKU and press Enter."), `<form data-testid="scan-form" method="post" action="/scan/resolve">` with the standard CSRF token hidden input, single `<input data-testid="scan-code-input" name="code" type="text" autocomplete="off" autofocus required maxlength="255">`, submit button `data-testid="scan-submit"`. Reuses the `data-testid="flash"` region from `base.html` for "no match" / "empty code" feedback — no per-template error block. **Nav link** (`base.html`): `nav-scan` inserted as the *second* item (right after Home), visible to **Workshop + Office + Manager + Admin** so it's on Workshop's hot-path. `aria-current="page"` highlighting when `request.url.path == "/scan"` or starts with `/scan/`. **Router wiring** (`app/main.py`): `app.include_router(scan_module.router)` next to the other includes. **Manual sanity check**: a one-shot TestClient script signed in as Workshop, GETs `/scan` (200 + autofocus), POSTs `/scan/resolve` with QR (303 → item edit), POSTs whitespace (303 → `/scan` + flash visible), POSTs unknown code (303 + flash echoes the code), and verified `nav-scan` on `/`. **Tests:** 31 new in `tests/integration/test_scan_routes.py` — `TestScanGetRoleEnforcement` (6: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop/Office/Manager/Admin 200), `TestScanGetRender` (3: heading present, form action `/scan/resolve` + method post, input has `name="code"` + `autofocus`), `TestScanResolveRoleEnforcement` (5: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop/Office/Manager 303 with valid codes), `TestScanResolveMatching` (5: by qr_code, by sku-fallback, qr_code wins on collision, whitespace trim, archived item still resolves), `TestScanResolveEmptyOrNoMatch` (3: empty, whitespace-only, unknown code → 303 to `/scan` + flash echoes offending code), `TestScanReadOnly` (2: GET writes no audit; POST resolve writes no audit), `TestScanNav` (7: Workshop/Office/Manager/Admin all see nav-scan; pending sees no nav at all; anon sees no nav-scan; `/scan` page marks the link `aria-current="page"`). Total pytest 1632 (was 1601, +31). 14 e2e (no count change — the resolve flow is integration-test territory; an e2e walk lands in SC1b once there's a unique full-flow story to tell that goes beyond what integration pins). ruff + mypy clean (28 source files; +1 module: `app/scan.py`). DoD #3 advances substantially (from "completely unmoved since I3" to "Workshop now has a scan-mode URL that resolves codes to the existing action surface") but doesn't tick — DoD #3 wording demands two-interaction recording, which requires SC1b + SC1c + SC2. |
| 46 | R5h — CSV export on the cross-item checkouts admin list (DoD #7) | `7b38a03` | Eighth CSV-export surface, after R5's variance-trend + PO list, R5b's items list, R5c's stock-takes list, R5d's suppliers list, R5e's helper extraction, R5f's locations list, and R5g's taxonomy list. Third *new* call site of R5e's `csv_branch` helper (after R5f and R5g). Extends `app/checkouts_admin.py::list_checkouts` with a `?format=csv` query branch and adds a `checkouts-admin-csv-link` "Download CSV" link to `checkouts_admin.html`. **Route extension**: new `format: str = ""` query param; `format == "csv"` triggers CSV via `csv_branch`; anything else (blank / `html` / garbage) renders HTML — same silent-coerce posture as `show`. **Role gate inherited**: existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER, Role.OFFICE))` already blocks Workshop + pending at the dependency layer (Workshop excluded from the cross-item view per C4 — they have per-item status blocks via C2's `/checkout` page); Admin via the role bypass. No inner role-split needed. **Headers** (11 columns): `checkout_id`, `item_id`, `item_sku`, `item_name`, `item_archived`, `unit_serial`, `holder_email`, `checked_out_at`, `expected_return`, `is_overdue`, `days_overdue`. Mirrors the HTML table cells one-for-one (8 visual columns) plus three explicit ID/state columns: `checkout_id` + `item_id` are the join keys (HTML carries them as `data-checkout-id` / `data-item-id` attributes; making them columns lets a downstream consumer join), and `is_overdue` is separated from `days_overdue` (the HTML conflates them into a single "Status" cell; the CSV gives both a binary filter column and a numeric sort column). `item_archived` + `is_overdue` render as literal `"yes"`/`"no"` strings (matching R5/R5b's PO list `supplier_archived` + items list `requires_checkout` precedent). `unit_serial` + `holder_email` render as empty cells when None (vs. the HTML's `—` em-dash — empty sorts/filters cleaner in spreadsheets). `expected_return` carries the full datetime (the HTML formats as `%Y-%m-%d`; the CSV preserves source-of-truth precision). `days_overdue` is the integer when overdue, empty when not (matches `is_overdue=False → days_overdue is None` pairing in `_list_open_checkouts`). **Filename**: `checkouts_{show}.csv` — `checkouts_open.csv` / `checkouts_overdue.csv`. Coerced `show` is used so a user typing `?format=csv&show=garbage` gets `checkouts_open.csv`. **Module-level `_CHECKOUTS_ADMIN_CSV_HEADERS`** + new `_csv_rows_for_checkouts(rows)` helper. Mirrors the seven prior surfaces' precedent. **Return type widened** on `list_checkouts` from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; `@router.get("", response_class=HTMLResponse)` decorator simplified to `@router.get("")`. The `HTMLResponse` import was removed entirely from `app/checkouts_admin.py` since no other route in the module used it (one fewer import to maintain — unlike `app/taxonomy.py` which kept the import for the form routes). **Walrus form**: `if (resp := csv_branch(format, filename=..., headers=..., rows=...)) is not None: return resp` — same shape as the seven prior sites. **Sort survives the CSV branch**: `_list_open_checkouts` does its Python-side sort (overdue first, then `checked_out_at DESC, id DESC`) before returning; the CSV branch consumes the already-sorted list. **Template**: new `<a data-testid="checkouts-admin-csv-link" href="/admin/checkouts?format=csv&show={{ show }}">Download CSV</a>` link block; href preserves active `show` value. Sits between the filter nav and the table. **Role enforcement preserved**: anon hits the dependency and 401s before reaching the format check; Pending / Workshop hit the same dependency and 403. **Read-only invariant preserved**: GET-CSV writes no audit row. **Time-sensitivity acknowledged**: `is_overdue` is computed from `datetime.now(UTC)` at request time; a CSV downloaded at 23:59 UTC vs 00:01 UTC the next day might differ even with no DB writes (consistent with the HTML view; consistent with the "snapshot artefact" framing of CSV exports). **Tests:** 21 new in `tests/integration/test_checkouts_admin_routes.py` — `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvRoleEnforcement` (5: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403, Manager 200, Office 200), `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvHeaders` (3: text/csv charset, default `checkouts_open.csv`, `?show=overdue` filename `checkouts_overdue.csv`), `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvBody` (8: header-only on empty, one open qty-tracked row with all 11 cells verified including `is_overdue=no` + empty `days_overdue` + empty `unit_serial`, unique-tracked carries unit serial, null user → empty `holder_email`, archived item → `item_archived=yes`, past-due → `is_overdue=yes` + `days_overdue` is positive int, show=overdue narrows to past-due only, returned checkouts excluded), `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvHtmlBranch` (2: blank + garbage format render HTML), `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvReadOnly` (1: GET-CSV writes no audit), `TestCheckoutsAdminCsvLink` (2: link renders for `show=open` and `show=overdue` with the active value preserved). Total pytest 1601 (was 1580, +21). 14 e2e (no count change — Playwright doesn't simulate the OS file-save dialog; the link visibility is already pinned by integration tests; existing `test_checkouts_e2e.py` already exercises the cross-item HTML page end-to-end). ruff + mypy clean (27 source files, no new modules). DoD #7 advances (8 of 8+ list views now have CSV — the major list views are now uniformly exportable) but doesn't tick — same conservative posture as R5/R5b/R5c/R5d/R5f/R5g. R5h continues to validate that the R5e helper is sound: a new surface adds ~10 LoC route + ~5 LoC template + ~21 tests with no friction. |

No inner role-split needed. **Headers** (3 columns): `id`, `sort_order`, `name`. Mirrors the HTML table's "Order" + "Name" columns one-for-one. Coerced `show` is used so a user typing `?show=garbage` gets `taxonomy_active.csv`. **Module-level `_TAXONOMY_CSV_HEADERS`** + new `_csv_rows_for_taxonomy(rows)` helper. Mirrors `_LOCATIONS_CSV_HEADERS` / `_csv_rows_for_locations` precedent exactly. **Return type widened** on `list_taxonomy` from `HTMLResponse` to `Response`; `@router.get("", response_class=HTMLResponse)` decorator simplified to `@router.get("")` (only affects OpenAPI hint generation; runtime unchanged). `HTMLResponse` import retained — still used by the `/new`, `/{id}/edit`, and sub-category form routes. **Walrus form**: `if (resp := csv_branch(format, filename=..., headers=..., rows=...)) is not None: return resp` — same shape as the six prior sites. **Sub-categories excluded from CSV**: the existing query filters `parent_id IS NULL`, so the CSV body only contains top-level categories. Pinned by `TestTaxonomyListCsvBody::test_sub_categories_not_in_csv` to defend against any future refactor that accidentally widened the scope. **`leaf_ids` lookup is HTML-only**: the CSV branch is built from the unchanged `rows` list before that lookup runs, so `?format=csv` skips the active-children query entirely. **Template**: new `<a data-testid="taxonomy-list-csv-link" href="/admin/taxonomy?format=csv&show={{ show }}">Download CSV</a>` link block; href preserves active `show` value. Sits between the filter nav and the table. **Role enforcement preserved**: anon hits the dependency and 401s before reaching the format check; Pending / Workshop / Office hit the same dependency and 403. **Read-only invariant preserved**: GET-CSV writes no audit row. **Tests:** 19 new in `tests/integration/test_taxonomy_routes.py` — `TestTaxonomyListCsvRoleEnforcement` (6: anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403, Office 403, Manager 200, Admin 200), `TestTaxonomyListCsvHeaders` (3: text/csv charset, default `taxonomy_active.csv`, `?show=archived` filename `taxonomy_archived.csv`), `TestTaxonomyListCsvBody` (5: header-only on empty, one-node happy path with all 3 cells verified including `sort_order=10`, show filter applies in both directions, sort_order ascending ordering preserved across non-sort-order insert order, sub-categories not surfaced via the top-level CSV when seeded under a parent), `TestTaxonomyListCsvHtmlBranch` (2: blank + unknown format render HTML), `TestTaxonomyListCsvReadOnly` (1: GET-CSV writes no audit), `TestTaxonomyListCsvLink` (2: link renders for `show=active` and `show=archived` with the active value preserved). Total pytest 1580 (was 1561, +19). 14 e2e (no count change — Playwright doesn't simulate the OS file-save dialog; the link visibility is already pinned by integration tests). ruff + mypy clean (27 source files, no new modules). DoD #7 advances (7 of 8+ list views now have CSV) but doesn't tick — same conservative posture as R5/R5b/R5c/R5d/R5f. R5g continues to validate that the R5e helper is sound: a new surface adds ~8 LoC route + ~5 LoC template + ~19 tests with no friction. |

| 43 | R5e — `csv_branch` helper extraction (refactor) | `4e571b1` | Pure refactor queued by R5d's self-critique. With five list-view surfaces now exposing `?format=csv` (variance trend + PO list + items list + stock-takes list + suppliers list), the recurring `if format == "csv": return csv_response(...)` block is centralised into a new `csv_branch(format_value, *, filename, headers, rows) -> Response | None` helper in `app/csv_export.py`. Each call site collapses to the walrus-form `if (resp := csv_branch(format, filename=..., headers=..., rows=...)) is not None: return resp`. Helper delegates filename sanitisation + cell coercion + Content-Disposition shaping to the existing `csv_response` — no duplicated logic. **Migrated:** `app/reports.py::variance_trend` (reads format inline from `request.query_params.get("format", "")`); `app/purchase_orders.py::list_purchase_orders`; `app/items.py::list_items` (`is_csv` flag retained for the inner Workshop role gate — separate concern from response branch); `app/stock_takes.py::list_stock_takes`; `app/suppliers.py::list_suppliers`. **No call-site behaviour change** — all existing CSV integration tests across the five surfaces (~76 tests covering role enforcement, Content-Type/Disposition, body shape, HTML branch, read-only invariant, link visibility) pass unchanged. **LoC at each call site barely changed** (6 → 9 lines because of walrus + `is not None` ceremony + multi-line kwargs); the **real win is conceptual centralization** — the magic string `"csv"` now lives in one place, so a future polish slice (case-insensitive `?format=CSV`, alternate `?download=csv`, cross-cutting "log CSV downloads" hook) is a one-line edit instead of five. **Tests:** 5 new unit in `tests/unit/test_csv_export.py::TestCsvBranch` — returns None for blank / "html" / garbage; returns Response with correct Content-Type + Content-Disposition for "csv"; body matches `csv_response` direct-call output. Total pytest 1542 (was 1537, +5). 14 e2e (no count change). ruff + mypy clean (27 source files, no new modules). DoD impact: zero — same five list views still expose CSV; conservative posture on DoD #7 unchanged. Reduces tech debt for the next CSV surface (R5f). |
| 42 | R5d — CSV export on the suppliers list (DoD #7) | `69779be` | Fifth CSV-export surface, after R5's variance-trend + PO list, R5b's items list, and R5c's stock-takes list. Pattern reuses R5's `csv_response` helper unchanged. **Route extension**: new `format` query param; `format == "csv"` triggers CSV; anything else (blank / `html` / garbage) renders HTML — same silent-coerce posture as `show`. **Role gate inherited**: the existing `Depends(require_role(Role.MANAGER))` already blocks Workshop + Office + pending at the dependency layer (suppliers are Manager-owned per MISSION §3); Admin always passes. The `id` column is explicit (HTML carries it as `data-supplier-id`, not a cell — making it a column lets a downstream consumer join). The `archived_at` column is intentionally *not* exposed: the filename already encodes the active filter (`suppliers_active.csv` vs `suppliers_archived.csv`), and a CSV body that mixes active + archived doesn't exist in this slice (the route partitions by `?show`). Optional fields (email/phone/notes) render as empty cells when null — same posture as the HTML view's `s.email or ""`. **Filename**: `suppliers_{show}.csv` — `suppliers_active.csv` / `suppliers_archived.csv`. Coerced `show` is used so a user typing `?show=garbage` gets `suppliers_active.csv`. **Module-level `_SUPPLIERS_CSV_HEADERS`** + new `_csv_rows_for_suppliers(rows)` helper. Both follow `_PO_LIST_CSV_HEADERS` / `_po_list_csv_rows`, `_ITEMS_CSV_HEADERS` / `_csv_rows_for_items`, and `_STOCK_TAKES_LIST_CSV_HEADERS` / `_csv_rows_for_stock_takes_list` precedent. **Return type widened** on `list_suppliers` from `HTMLResponse` to `Response` to accommodate the CSV body; `@router.get("")` decorator's `response_class` arg dropped (only affects OpenAPI hint; runtime unchanged). **Template**: new link block; href preserves active `show` value.
