| 38 | R4 — Stock-take variance trend report (DoD #7) | `71f877a` | New module `app/reports.py` mounted at `/admin/reports`. One route this slice (R5 CSV will live alongside). New template `variance_trend.html`. New `dashboard-variance-trend-link` on `dashboard.html`. **Route**: `GET /admin/reports/variance-trend[?days=N]` for **Manager + Office** (Workshop + pending → 401/403; anon 401 — same posture as R1). Read-only — no audit, no mutations, no engine touches. `days` clamped to `[1, 365]`, default 90; out-of-range / non-int silently coerces (same posture as R1's `top_days`). Filters to stock takes where `completed_at IS NOT NULL AND completed_at >= now() - days`. **Per-stock-take aggregation**: only `committed=True` lines with non-zero `variance` contribute. Aggregates per row: `lines_with_variance` (count), `positive_variance` (sum of variance > 0), `negative_variance_abs` (sum of `abs(variance)` where < 0), `net_variance` (signed sum), `abs_variance` (sum of absolute values). Invariant `abs_variance == positive + negative_abs` pinned by unit test. **Totals card** sums per-side aggregates across in-window stock takes plus a `stock_take_count` counter. Always renders, even when zero rows (consistent framing). Newest-first ordering by `completed_at DESC, id DESC`. **Why "committed only"**: a `StockTakeLine.variance` is written by ST2 the moment a count is typed, but until ST3 commits, no adjustment movement exists in the cost engine. The report aggregates **what was actioned**, not "what was observed". Audit log of `stock_take.committed` rows is the canonical record this report rolls up. **Helpers** (`app/reports.py`, all pure): `_coerce_days(raw)`; `_window_cutoff(days)`; `_aggregate_lines(lines)` (pure rollup over Python objects, testable in isolation); `_combine_aggregates(rows)`; `_scope_label` (local copy of `app.stock_takes._scope_label` to keep modules decoupled — duplication-tax flagged); `_load_trend_rows` (3 round-trips: stock takes + batched node lookup + batched location lookup + batched lines fetch grouped in Python by `stock_take_id`). **Templates**: `variance-trend-heading`, `variance-trend-form` + `variance-trend-days-input` + `variance-trend-days-submit`; `variance-trend-totals` block with `variance-trend-stock-take-count` + `variance-trend-total-lines-with-variance` + `variance-trend-total-positive` + `variance-trend-total-negative-abs` + `variance-trend-total-net` + `variance-trend-total-abs`; `variance-trend-table` with per-row `variance-trend-row` (`data-stock-take-id`) + cells `variance-trend-row-id` (link) / `variance-trend-row-scope` / `variance-trend-row-scheduled-for` / `variance-trend-row-completed-at` / `variance-trend-row-lines-with-variance` / `variance-trend-row-positive` / `variance-trend-row-negative-abs` / `variance-trend-row-net` / `variance-trend-row-abs`. Empty state `variance-trend-empty`. **Dashboard wiring**: `dashboard-variance-trend-link` block in `dashboard.html` between the at-a-glance card row and the top-consumed section. **No new nav link** — discovery via dashboard only (a single-page Reports menu would clutter; once R5 lands a Reports nav becomes worth it). **Tests:** 39 new (18 unit in `tests/unit/test_reports.py::TestCoerceDays/TestAggregateLines/TestCombineAggregates` + 21 integration in `tests/integration/test_reports_routes.py::TestVarianceTrendRoleEnforcement/TestVarianceTrendEmptyState/TestVarianceTrendWindowFilter/TestVarianceTrendAggregation/TestVarianceTrendTotals/TestVarianceTrendReadOnly/TestVarianceTrendDashboardLink`). Total pytest 1422 (was 1383, +39). 14 e2e (unchanged count; existing `test_office_schedules_a_stock_take` walk extended in-place: after the ST3 commit leg, office navigates `nav-dashboard` → `dashboard-variance-trend-link` → asserts the just-completed take appears with `lines_with_variance=1`, `net=-2`, totals card `stock_take_count=1`). ruff + mypy clean (26 source files; new module `app/reports.py`). DoD #7 not yet ticked — R4 advances it but variance trend isn't in DoD #7's explicit list ("value, low stock, overdue, top consumed, COGS"); ticking deferred until R5 + manual sanity-check. |
| 37 | ST3 — Commit stock-take variances as adjustment movements (DoD #5) | `6c1df76` | Closes DoD #5 — Office can now run a stock take end-to-end (schedule from ST1 → start + count + see variance from ST2 → commit from ST3). New route `POST /admin/stock-takes/{stock_take_id}/commit` on `app/stock_takes.py` for **Manager + Office** (Workshop + pending → 401/403; anon 401). Walks every line with non-zero variance, builds one `StockMovement(type=ADJUSTMENT)` per line via the cost engine: `record_receipt(..., source=POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT)` for positive variance (creates layer + bumps qty); `consume_fifo` for negative variance (creates consumption rows + decrements qty). `stock_movements.stock_take_id` is now actively populated (FK had been live since ST1's migration but never written until ST3). Flips `StockTakeLine.committed=True` per non-zero-variance line. Sets `StockTake.completed_at = now()`. Writes one `stock_take.committed` audit row with `before={"completed_at": null}`, `after={"completed_at": <ISO>, "movements": [{line_id, item_id, movement_id, variance (str), direction, unit_cost (str | null), total_cost (str)}, ...]}`. 303s back to detail page (now in completed state). **Engine ownership preserved**: route never mutates `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `item.current_qty`, `movement.total_cost`, or `cost_layer_consumptions.*` directly — the engine is the single owner. Caller pattern matches M2/M3/M4: `db.add(movement); db.flush(); engine_call(...)`. **Validation order** (atomic): 404 → 400 not-in-progress → 400 no-variances → per-positive-variance line `unit_cost_<id>` parses as non-negative Decimal (blank required → 400; non-numeric / negative → 400; zero allowed). **Atomic-on-raise**: insufficient stock on a negative-variance line raises `InsufficientStockError` *before any layer mutation* (M1 invariant). Route catches it, calls `db.rollback()` (drops every successful in-flight movement / layer / consumption / `committed` flag from the same transaction), re-loads the stock take, re-renders the detail page with HTTP 400 + `<p data-testid="stock-take-commit-error" role="alert">` block + the operator's typed `unit_cost_<id>` inputs preserved via `commit_form_values`. Pinned by `TestCommitInsufficientStock::test_partial_failure_leaves_no_state` — even if the first (positive) line would have succeeded in isolation, the second (negative) line's shortage rolls the whole transaction back. **Default unit cost** for positive-variance lines: `_last_unit_cost(db, item_id)` (newest layer's `unit_cost`; `ORDER BY received_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1`; same shape as PO2's helper). Items with no prior layers render an empty input. **Audit `total_cost` decimal scale wart**: positive carries `"7.500000"` (qty scale 4 × form-cost scale 2 = scale 6); negative carries `"4.00000000"` (qty scale 4 × layer-cost scale 4 = scale 8). Same wart as M3/M4. **All commit-batch movements share `reason="stock take #{N}"`**; per-line nuance lives on the audit row's `movements` list. **Helpers** (`_can_commit`, `_last_unit_cost`, `_parse_unit_cost_for_commit`, `_lines_with_non_zero_variance`). **`_detail_context` extended**: in-progress branch now carries `commit_lines` (variance rows with direction + pre-filled unit_cost_default) + optional `commit_error` + `commit_form_values`; line view dict gains `is_committed` flag. **Templates** (`stock_take_detail.html`): in-progress branch grows new "Commit" sub-section below the count form (`stock-take-commit-form` with per-row `stock-take-commit-row` carrying `data-direction` — positive renders `stock-take-commit-unit-cost-input`, negative renders `stock-take-commit-unit-cost-na`); zero-variance state shows `stock-take-no-variances-note`; completed branch's per-line row gains `data-committed="true|false"` + a 7th cell `stock-take-line-committed`. **Tests:** 49 new (18 unit in `tests/unit/test_stock_takes.py::TestCanCommit/TestLastUnitCost/TestParseUnitCostForCommit/TestLinesWithNonZeroVariance` + 31 integration in `tests/integration/test_stock_takes_routes.py::TestCommitRoleEnforcement/TestCommitValidation/TestCommitPositiveHappyPath/TestCommitNegativeHappyPath/TestCommitMixed/TestCommitInsufficientStock/TestCommitAuditShape/TestCommitFormRendering/TestCommittedBadgeOnCompleted`). Total pytest 1383 (was 1334, +49). 14 e2e (unchanged count; the existing `test_office_schedules_a_stock_take` walk is extended in-place to also commit and verify `current_qty` drops 50.0000 → 48.0000 via the cost engine's FIFO consume). ruff + mypy clean (25 source files, no new modules). DoD #5 ticks — Office runs a stock take end-to-end with positive + negative variance handling, FIFO consumption, audit attribution. |
| 36 | ST2 — Stock take session UI (DoD #5 foundation) | `a025126` | Lands the count + variance flow on top of ST1's scheduling foundation. Three new routes on the existing `/admin/stock-takes` prefix in `app/stock_takes.py` for **Manager + Office** (Workshop + pending → 401/403; anon 401): `GET /admin/stock-takes/{id}` (read-only detail with three render branches gated on the derived status — scheduled / in_progress / completed), `POST /admin/stock-takes/{id}/start` (freezes in-scope items into `StockTakeLine` rows + flips `started_at`), `POST /admin/stock-takes/{id}/counts` (per-line `counted_<line_id>` form fields → updates `counted_qty` + `variance`; sparse diff so a no-op submit writes no audit row). New template `stock_take_detail.html`. **Engine isolation:** ST2 doesn't touch `cost_layers`, `cost_layer_consumptions`, `item.current_qty`, or `stock_movements` — variances are *recorded* (`StockTakeLine.counted_qty` + `StockTakeLine.variance`) but not *actioned*. ST3 will be the slice that walks the lines + builds adjustment movements via the cost engine. **Scope resolution** (`_resolve_scope_items`): both nulls → all active items; `scope_node_id` set → items at that node OR its direct sub-categories (v1 taxonomy is two levels deep, so the descendant lookup is a single `WHERE parent_id == scope_node_id`); `scope_location_id` set → items at that location. Archived items always excluded. **Validation order** (atomic — all 400 *before* any DB write): start: 404 → 400 not-scheduled → resolve scope → 400 zero-items; counts: 404 → 400 not-in-progress → fetch lines → parse every present field → 400 on any non-numeric / negative → compute diff → write only changed lines → audit only if any changed. Lines whose key is missing from the form are left unchanged (defends against stale tabs). **Audit shapes:** `stock_take.started` carries `before={"started_at": null}`, `after={"started_at": <ISO>, "lines": [{"line_id", "item_id", "system_qty"}, ...]}` (line snapshot at start time pins what was in scope; `system_qty` stringified at scale 4). `stock_take.counted` carries sparse `before={"lines": [{"line_id", "counted_qty": <prev str | null>}, ...]}` and `after={"lines": [{"line_id", "counted_qty": <new str | null>, "variance": <new str | null>}, ...]}` — only changed lines appear; a no-op submit writes no audit row. Decimal scale wart inherited from M3: variance is `counted - system` where `system` is read back from the column at scale 4, so the variance audit value is `"2.0000"` not `"2"` (pinned with explanatory comment). **Helpers** (`_can_start`, `_can_count`, `_resolve_scope_items`, `_compute_variance`, `_format_variance`, `_variance_sign`, `_parse_optional_count`, `_lines_with_items`, `_count_progress`, `_detail_context`). **Tests:** 66 new (18 unit in `tests/unit/test_stock_takes.py` — `TestComputeVariance` 4, `TestFormatVariance` 4, `TestVarianceSign` 4, `TestResolveScopeItems` 6; 48 integration in `tests/integration/test_stock_takes_routes.py` — `TestDetailRoleEnforcement` 5, `TestDetailRenderScheduled` 3, `TestDetailRenderInProgress` 3, `TestDetailRenderCompleted` 1, `TestStartRoleEnforcement` 4, `TestStartValidation` 5, `TestStartHappyPathAllScope` 4, `TestStartHappyPathNodeScope` 2, `TestStartHappyPathLocationScope` 1, `TestCountsRoleEnforcement` 4, `TestCountsValidation` 6, `TestCountsHappyPath` 5, `TestCountsAuditShape` 1, `TestEngineIsolation` 2, `TestListDetailLink` 1). Total pytest 1334 (was 1268, +66). 14 e2e (unchanged count; the existing `test_office_schedules_a_stock_take` walk is extended in-place to also seed an item via the stock-in form, click the row's detail link, start the count, fill counted=48 against system_qty=50, and assert variance=-2 plus engine isolation by re-visiting the items list and confirming `current_qty` is still 50.0000). ruff + mypy clean (25 source files, no new modules). DoD #5 still untickable until ST3 lands the commit-as-adjustment flow. |
| 35 | ST1 — Stock take scheduling and scope (DoD #5 foundation) | `8499440` | First link in DoD #5's chain. New module `app/stock_takes.py` with three routes mounted at `/admin/stock-takes` for **Manager + Office** (Admin via the bypass; Workshop + pending → 403). `GET /admin/stock-takes[?show=open|completed]` lists scheduled / in_progress (default `open`) or completed rows; `GET /admin/stock-takes/new` renders the form; `POST /admin/stock-takes` validates + writes one `StockTake` row + a `stock_take.created` audit row. New tables `stock_takes` + `stock_take_lines` (migration `0014_create_stock_takes.py`); new ORM models `StockTake` + `StockTakeLine`. **Lifecycle is derived from timestamps** (no status enum, matching MISSION §6): `scheduled` (both null), `in_progress` (started_at set, completed_at null), `completed` (completed_at set). **Scope is mutually exclusive** across `scope_node_id` and `scope_location_id` — at most one set; both null = "all items". The XOR invariant is route-layer (same posture as taxonomy leaf invariants). **`stock_take_lines` table is created but no rows are written in ST1** — schema lands now so ST2 doesn't need a follow-up migration. **`stock_movements.stock_take_id` FK is now active** via `op.batch_alter_table` (same SQLite-compat path as `0012_add_stock_movements_po_id_fk.py`); FK is RESTRICT. **Validation order** (all 400 *before* any DB write — atomic): `scope_type` ∈ `{all, node, location}`; `scheduled_for` is non-blank ISO date; `notes` ≤ 2000 chars; if `scope_type==node`: `scope_node_id` parses + references active `TaxonomyNode`; if `scope_type==location`: `scope_location_id` parses + references active `Location`; if `scope_type==all`: both ids ignored on input (friendlier UX than rejecting). **Audit shape — `stock_take.created`**: `before=None`, `after={scope_node_id, scope_location_id, scheduled_for (ISO), notes}`. Decimal-free; ISO date via `.isoformat()`. New entity_type `stock_take` (joins user/supplier/location/taxonomy_node/taxonomy_field_def/item/item_unit/stock_movement/purchase_order/checkout). **Helpers**: `_active_nodes`, `_active_locations` (form selects), `_status_label`, `_scope_label` (view-shape), `_coerce_show` (defaults `open` like `checkouts_admin._coerce_show`), `_resolve_node` / `_resolve_location` (parse + validate). **Templates / test-ids**: `stock_takes_list.html` — heading `stock-takes-heading`, empty-state `stock-takes-empty`, filter tabs `filter-open` + `filter-completed`, `stock-takes-table` with per-row `stock-takes-row` (`data-stock-take-id`, `data-status`), cells `stock-takes-row-scope` / `stock-takes-row-scheduled-for` / `stock-takes-row-status-badge` / `stock-takes-row-created-by` / `stock-takes-row-created-at`, "New stock take" CTA `stock-takes-new-link`. `stock_take_form.html` — heading `stock-take-form-heading`, form `stock-take-form`, `stock-take-scope-type-input` (radio), `stock-take-scope-node-input` / `stock-take-scope-location-input` (selects), `stock-take-scheduled-for-input`, `stock-take-notes-input`, `stock-take-submit`. New `nav-stock-takes` link in `base.html` between Checkouts and Users for admin/manager/office (Workshop hidden); `aria-current` on `path == "/admin/stock-takes" or startswith("/admin/stock-takes/")`. **Engine isolation**: read + a single insert. No DB writes touch `cost_layers` / `cost_layer_consumptions` / `stock_movements` / `item.current_qty` / `item.archived_at`. **Tests** (58 new — 47 integration in `tests/integration/test_stock_takes_routes.py` + 11 unit in `tests/unit/test_stock_takes.py`): `TestRoleEnforcement` (10) — anon GET-list / GET-new / POST 401; pending 403; Workshop GET / POST 403; Office / Manager / Admin GET 200; Office POST 303; `TestListRendering` (8) — empty state; `?show=open` shows scheduled rows; `?show=completed` shows completed rows (seeded directly with `started_at` + `completed_at`); unrecognised `?show=foo` falls through to `open`; in-progress renders in open tab; scope label for node ("Category: …") / location ("Location: …") / all ("All items"); `TestNewFormRendering` (4) — inputs + CSRF + active node `<select>` + active location `<select>` populated; archived nodes excluded from select; archived locations excluded; `TestValidation` (12) — invalid / blank scope_type 400; blank / bad-format scheduled_for 400; node-scope blank / non-int / unknown / archived id 400; location-scope blank / archived id 400; oversize notes 400; failed validation writes no `StockTake` + no audit; `TestHappyPathAll` (3) — creates row with both nulls; audit shape; flash visible after redirect; `TestHappyPathNode` (2) — creates row with node id; audit carries node id; `TestHappyPathLocation` (2) — creates row with location id; audit carries location id; `TestScopeAllIgnoresIdInputs` (1) — node_id stripped when scope_type=all (friendlier UX); `TestLayoutNav` (5) — manager / office / admin nav-stock-takes visible; workshop hidden; `aria-current` on stock-takes page. Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_stock_takes.py` (11): `TestStockTakeModel` (3) — minimal row with scope=all; with node scope; with location scope; `TestStockTakeLineModel` (2) — minimal line; completed line with counted_qty + variance + committed; `TestStatusLabel` (3) — scheduled / in_progress / completed across the three timestamp combinations; `TestScopeLabel` (3) — all / category / location. **E2E walk** (1 new in `tests/e2e/test_stock_takes_e2e.py`): pending office + pending manager promoted by admin → manager creates a category "ST1 Materials" → office signs in → `nav-stock-takes` → empty state → "New stock take" CTA → form → picks scope=node + the category + 2026-08-15 + a note → submits → flash includes the date → list shows the row with scope label "Category: ST1 Materials" + scheduled_for "2026-08-15" + status badge "scheduled" + `created_by` showing the office email. Cleanup archives the category. **DoD #5**: ST1 lays the schedule + scope foundation; ST2 (count session) + ST3 (commit variances as adjustments) are required to tick. 1268 unit+integration (was 1210, +58). 14 e2e (was 13, +1). ruff + mypy clean (25 source files, +1 — `app/stock_takes.py`). |

| 31 | C1 — Per-item requires_checkout flag UI + checkouts schema foundation (DoD #4) | `232edc3` | First link in DoD #4's chain. New ORM model `Checkout` in `app/models.py` mirroring MISSION §6 columns: `id, item_id (FK items RESTRICT NOT NULL), item_unit_id (FK item_units RESTRICT nullable), user_id (FK users SET NULL nullable), checked_out_at (DateTime tz NOT NULL), expected_return (DateTime tz nullable), returned_at (DateTime tz nullable), condition_note (String 2000 nullable), created_at, updated_at`. No status enum: open vs returned is derived from `returned_at IS NULL`; overdue is `returned_at IS NULL AND expected_return < now()`. Migration `0013_create_checkouts.py` creates the table + four indexes (per item / item_unit / user / returned_at). FK posture: `user_id` SET NULL (user soft-deletes shouldn't cascade through history); `item_id` and `item_unit_id` RESTRICT (a checkout row blocks any future hard-delete path on the parent). No `archived_at` — checkout rows are part of the audit trail. **Items list filter** (`GET /admin/items?requires_checkout=yes`): new query param filters to `requires_checkout=True` items. Anything other than the literal `"yes"` (blank, `"no"`, garbage) silently coerces to no filter — same posture as `show=active|archived`. The filter combines with `show=` so a manager can see archived flagged items. **Items list UI affordance**: new "Checkout?" column showing "Yes" / "—" with `data-testid="item-requires-checkout"` per cell. New filter nav with `data-testid="filter-all"` + `data-testid="filter-requires-checkout"` — `aria-current="page"` flips between them; both links preserve the active `show` param so navigating between filters keeps the archived/active context. **Items form**: existing `requires_checkout` checkbox gains an `<em data-testid="item-requires-checkout-help">` note ("Track who has the item out and when it's due back."). **No new routes** — check-out / check-in routes land in C2 / C3, manager "currently out / overdue" view in C4. C1's job is to land the foundation so those slices don't have to also ship a migration. Also unblocks R1's `dashboard-overdue-checkouts` placeholder which can flip to real numbers once C4 lands. **Tests** (21 new): `tests/unit/test_checkouts.py` — `TestCheckoutDefaults` (4) covers minimal open round-trip + checkout with item_unit + expected_return + condition_note + returned checkout + long condition_note (1500 chars within 2000 col); `TestCheckoutForeignKeys` (5) covers `item_id` required + `checked_out_at` required + orphan `item_id` rejected (with PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON) + orphan `item_unit_id` rejected + `user_id` accepts NULL; `TestCheckoutReturnedDerivation` (2) covers open `returned_at IS NULL` + setting `returned_at` after creation. `tests/integration/test_items_routes.py::TestRequiresCheckoutFlag` (10): list shows "Yes" for flagged + "—" for unflagged; "—" only when no items flagged; `?requires_checkout=yes` filters to flagged only; blank does not filter; unrecognised value (`foo`) does not filter; `?requires_checkout=no` does not filter (2-state, not 3-state); filter nav links with `aria-current` flip across views; filter combines with `?show=archived` (archived flagged shown, archived non-flagged hidden); form renders the help note; filter nav preserves `show=archived` in both link hrefs. **DoD #4 progress**: schema foundation laid + UI affordance live; C2 builds the check-out flow on top. 1109 unit+integration (was 1088, +21). 12 e2e (unchanged — schema-only slice; e2e walk lands in C2). ruff + mypy clean (22 source files, unchanged — `Checkout` extends the existing `app/models.py`). |
| 30 | R1 — Reporting dashboard with FIFO valuation, low-stock count, top consumed, COGS (DoD #7) | `666e7c1` | First link in DoD #7's chain. New module `app/dashboard.py` with one route `GET /admin/dashboard` for **Manager + Office** (Admin via the bypass; Workshop + pending 403 — MISSION §3 "Workshop... cannot see aggregated cost data or reports"). Read-only — no mutations, no audit, no movement type, no new tables. **Aggregations** (each is a single round-trip): total inventory value = `SUM(qty_remaining * unit_cost)` across active items' open `cost_layers` rows (joined to `items` filtered `archived_at IS NULL`); low-stock count = `COUNT(items)` where `archived_at IS NULL AND current_qty <= reorder_threshold`; open POs count = `COUNT(purchase_orders)` where `status IN (draft, sent, partially_received)`; overdue checkouts = hardcoded 0 with `<small>` "checkouts not yet implemented" note (C-series will replace it; pinning the test-id `dashboard-overdue-checkouts` now means C-series doesn't have to re-template); top consumed = `SUM(qty)` over `stock_movements WHERE type=OUT AND created_at >= now() - top_days` grouped by `item_id`, joined to `items`, ordered desc, LIMIT 10 (window via `?top_days=N` defaulting 30; clamped `[1, 365]`; non-int / out-of-range silently coerce to default — same posture as M6 pagination); COGS = `SUM(total_cost)` over `stock_movements` where `id IN (SELECT DISTINCT movement_id FROM cost_layer_consumptions) AND created_at BETWEEN cogs_start AND cogs_end+1d` (consumption-row subquery covers OUT + negative-adjustment ADJUSTMENT — both go through `consume_fifo`; adjustment-increase has no consumption row so it's correctly excluded; IN has total_cost set but no consumption row; TRANSFER has neither). The COGS date range is end-inclusive (resolves to `[start 00:00 UTC, end+1 00:00 UTC)`). Default range: last 30 days. Bad-format ISO date 400. **Helpers**: `_coerce_top_days(raw)` (silent coerce on bad / out-of-range), `_parse_cogs_date(raw, *, field_name)` (400 on bad format; blank → None), `_resolve_cogs_range(start, end)` (defaults end=today, start=end-30d). Five aggregation helpers (`_total_inventory_value`, `_low_stock_count`, `_open_pos_count`, `_top_consumed`, `_cogs`) are pure DB reads — no side effects, no Request, no HTTPException. **Decimal coercion**: `func.coalesce(func.sum(...), 0)` returns Decimal under SQLAlchemy's `Numeric(14,4)` round-trip but mypy types `scalar_one()` as Any; defensively branch `if isinstance(total, Decimal): return total else Decimal(str(total))`. The `Decimal(str(...))` lands when SQLite reports SUM as int (all-zero-rows case). Both paths produce a deterministic Decimal output with no precision loss. **Templates / test-ids**: new `dashboard.html` with at-a-glance card row (`dashboard-heading`, `dashboard-total-value`, `dashboard-low-stock-count`, `dashboard-open-pos-count`, `dashboard-overdue-checkouts`); top-consumed table (`dashboard-top-consumed-table` + per-row `dashboard-top-consumed-row` with `data-item-id` + cells `dashboard-top-consumed-sku` / `dashboard-top-consumed-name` / `dashboard-top-consumed-qty` / `dashboard-top-consumed-detail-link`; empty state `dashboard-top-consumed-empty`); two filter forms (`dashboard-top-days-form` + `dashboard-top-days-input` + `dashboard-top-days-submit`; `dashboard-cogs-form` + `dashboard-cogs-start-input` + `dashboard-cogs-end-input` + `dashboard-cogs-submit`); COGS card (`dashboard-cogs-amount`). Each filter form carries the *other* param as a hidden input so submitting one preserves the other's value. `base.html` gains a `nav-dashboard` link (between Home and Suppliers) visible to Admin / Manager / Office; Workshop hidden. The `aria-current` is on `path == "/admin/dashboard" or startswith("/admin/dashboard/")` (same tightening pattern as PO2's `nav-reorder` fix). **Tests** (37 new — 32 in `tests/integration/test_dashboard_routes.py` + 5 in `tests/integration/test_layout.py` + 1 e2e walk in `test_dashboard_e2e.py`): `TestDashboardRoleEnforcement` (6) — anon 401; pending 403; Workshop 403; Manager / Office / Admin 200; `TestDashboardTotalValue` (5) — empty zero; single layer `total_value="25.0000"` (10×2.50 column-quantised); multi-item sum (10×2 + 5×3 = 35); archived item's layers excluded; fully-consumed layer contributes 0 (0 + 5×3 = 15); `TestDashboardLowStockCount` (3) — zero state; positive count (2 below + 1 above-threshold = 2); archived items excluded; `TestDashboardOpenPOsCount` (2) — zero; counts draft + sent + partially_received but excludes received + cancelled (3 of 5 in seed); `TestDashboardTopConsumed` (7) — empty when no OUTs; orders by sum(qty) desc with sku-asc tiebreak (B=20 > C=10 > A=5); window excludes 100-day-old OUT (default 30-day); `?top_days=200` includes the 100-day-old OUT; `?top_days=foo` coerces to 30; `?top_days=99999` coerces to 30; TRANSFER not counted; `TestDashboardCOGS` (8) — zero with no movements; sums OUT total_cost (3×2 = "6.0000"); sums ADJUSTMENT decreases AND excludes ADJUSTMENT increases (only the 2-unit decrease counts: 2×2 = "4.0000"); IN movements not counted; default-window picks only recent OUT (6.0000); explicit 90-day window picks up 60-day-old OUT (7×2 = 14); bad `cogs_start` 400; bad `cogs_end` 400; `TestDashboardReadOnly` (1) — GET writes no audit row (compares pre/post audit ids); 5 nav tests in `test_layout.py` — manager / office / admin nav-dashboard visible; workshop nav-dashboard hidden; `aria-current` on `/admin/dashboard` page sets it on the dashboard link. **E2E walk**: a manager promoted from pending creates a category + item (threshold=0), stocks in 100 @ 2.50 (current_qty=100), stocks out 30 (current_qty=70), visits `/admin/dashboard` via the nav link, asserts: total value = 175.0000 (70 × 2.50), low-stock count = 0 (qty=70 > threshold=0), open POs count = 0, overdue checkouts = 0, top-consumed table has 1 row with sku=DASH-1 + qty=30.0000, COGS = 75.0000 (30 × 2.50), top-days input pre-fills with "30". Cleanup archives the item + category. **DoD #7 progress**: all four DoD #7 widgets are live; the chain is exhaustively integration-tested AND walked end-to-end. Ticking #7 waits on C-series unblocking the overdue-checkouts placeholder + a manual sanity-check across the full widget matrix on cloud Postgres in P-series. 1088 unit+integration (was 1051, +37). 12 e2e (was 11, +1). ruff + mypy clean (22 source files, +1 — `app/dashboard.py` is the new typed source file). |
| 29 | PO5 — Receive against a PO with actual unit costs (DoD #6) | `fa8b138` | Closes DoD #6's chain. New routes `GET /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/receive` (form) + `POST /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/receive` (write) on `app/purchase_orders.py`'s `list_router`, both **Manager + Office** (Workshop + pending 403; anon 401). Status guard: `_require_receivable(po)` accepts only `sent` + `partially_received`; draft / received / cancelled all 400. The POST is `async def` (per-line dynamic keys via `request.form()` — same precedent as PO2b's edit + items.py's create / update). Validates per line in a first pass before any DB mutation: `received_<id>` parses non-negative Decimal (blank → 0; negative / non-numeric reject), `cost_<id>` only validated when received > 0 (zero allowed for sample stock — same as M2/M4; blank-with-qty rejects so the operator must affirm a price; negative / non-numeric reject), cumulative `line.qty_received + new_received <= line.qty_ordered` (over-receipt rejects). Second pass writes one `StockMovement(type=IN, po_id=po.id)` + one `CostLayer(source=PO_RECEIPT)` per line with received > 0 via `record_receipt(item, qty, unit_cost, source=PO_RECEIPT, movement, received_at=now())`; increments `line.qty_received`. After the per-line loop, the cumulative status flips: RECEIVED if every line is fully received (`line.qty_received >= line.qty_ordered`), else PARTIALLY_RECEIVED. **All-zero submit is a no-op**: writes no movement, no layer, no audit row, no status change; flashes "no receipts entered" + 303s. **Engine ownership maintained**: route never mutates `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `item.current_qty`, or `movement.total_cost` — `record_receipt` is the single owner per M1's contract. Each line generates one movement + one layer. **Migration `0012_add_stock_movements_po_id_fk.py`** adds the FK that M1 deferred (`stock_movements.po_id` → `purchase_orders.id`, `ON DELETE RESTRICT`). SQLite-compat via `op.batch_alter_table` (recreates the table with the new constraint; data preserved); Postgres falls through to a regular `op.create_foreign_key`. The column stays nullable — manual M2 stock-ins still write `po_id=None`. Model column on `StockMovement` updated to declare the ForeignKey. **Audit shape — `purchase_order.received`**: `before={"status": prev_status_value}`, `after={"status": new_status_value, "lines": [{"line_id", "received_qty" (str), "actual_unit_cost" (str), "movement_id"} ...]}`. Only lines with received > 0 appear in `lines`. The `movement_id` lets a future audit reader pin to the exact `StockMovement` row this transaction created (and the cost layer is reachable via `cost_layers.source_movement_id`). The `actual_unit_cost` audit value is the user-typed string ("2.50") not the column-quantised representation ("2.5000") — pragmatic: the operator's *intent* is what should land in the audit log; the column-quantised value is reachable via `cost_layer.unit_cost` join. Multi-receive transactions write one audit row each (not one combined). **Detail page extension**: `purchase_order_detail.html`'s read-only branch gains an `is_receivable` flag from the route context that lights up a `data-testid="po-receive-link-block"` paragraph with a `data-testid="po-receive-link"` for status `(sent, partially_received)`. Received and cancelled hide the link. Drafts hide the link (drafts go through PO4 send first). **New template `purchase_order_receive_form.html`** with the form (`po-receive-form` + `po-receive-submit`), per-line `po-receive-line-row` (with `data-line-id` + `data-item-id`), `po-receive-line-sku` / `po-receive-line-qty-ordered` / `po-receive-line-qty-received` / `po-receive-outstanding` cells, plus per-line `po-receive-received-input` + `po-receive-cost-input` (both with `data-line-id`). Cost input pre-fills with `expected_unit_cost` to reduce typing in the common case. **Tests** (47 new — all integration in `tests/integration/test_purchase_orders_routes.py` + e2e walk extension in `test_reorder_e2e.py`): `TestPOReceiveRoleEnforcement` (9) — anon GET / POST 401; pending GET / POST 403; Workshop GET / POST 403; Manager / Office / Admin GET 200; `TestPOReceiveStatusGuard` (7) — unknown GET / POST 404; draft GET / POST 400; received GET 400; cancelled GET 400; partially_received GET 200; `TestPOReceiveValidation` (8) — negative received 400; non-numeric received 400; negative cost 400; non-numeric cost 400; blank cost with received > 0 400; over-receipt single-shot 400; cumulative over-receipt 400 (after partial); failed validation writes no movement / cost layer / status flip / audit (atomic); `TestPOReceiveHappyPathSingleFull` (8) — status flips to RECEIVED; movement carries `type=IN`, `po_id=po.id`, qty, total_cost (`"25.0000"`); cost layer with `source=PO_RECEIPT`, qty_received + qty_remaining, unit_cost (`"2.5000"`); `line.qty_received` bumped; `item.current_qty` bumped; audit shape with `before={"status":"sent"}`, `after.status="received"`, `after.lines=[{line_id, received_qty="10", actual_unit_cost="2.50", movement_id=int}]`; redirect to detail; flash "fully received"; `TestPOReceiveHappyPathPartial` (3) — partial 5/10 flips to PARTIALLY_RECEIVED; second receive completes to RECEIVED; multi-receive creates two cost layers at different unit costs (2.0000 + 2.2000), both `source=PO_RECEIPT`; `TestPOReceiveHappyPathMultiLine` (3) — mixed full + partial flips to PARTIALLY_RECEIVED; zero-received line writes no movement (only the non-zero line's audit + movement); both movements carry the same `po_id`; `TestPOReceiveNoOpSubmit` (2) — all-zero submit writes nothing + flashes "no receipts" + status unchanged; explicit 0 received with cost present writes nothing; `TestPOReceiveDetailRender` (5) — draft hides link; sent shows link + read-only banner; partially_received shows link; received hides link; cancelled hides link; `TestPOReceiveFormRender` (2) — receive form renders with line row + outstanding equal to `qty_ordered` for fresh sent PO; after partial receipt the form re-renders with reduced outstanding (10 → 4). **E2E walk extension** (in-place in `test_reorder_e2e.py`): after PO4's send leg lands a sent PO with `po-receive-link` visible, the manager clicks the link, asserts `po-receive-form` + 1 line row + outstanding=120.0000, fills `po-receive-received-input`=120 + `po-receive-cost-input`=2.00, submits, lands back on detail with `po-status-badge="received"` + `po-receive-link` count=0 + `po-line-qty-received="120.0000"`. Then visits the items stock-in form for the linked item to verify `item-current-qty="120.0000"` (the cost engine bumped current_qty). Finally returns to the reorder dashboard via nav and asserts the empty state (the same dashboard that started with RD-LOW below threshold is now empty because the cost engine moved stock through). The previous "manual stock-in to clear the dashboard" step is removed — PO5 does that work end-to-end now. **DoD #6 ticks**: the chain reorder → draft → edit/cancel → PDF → send → receive → cost layers + valuation update is exhaustively integration-tested AND walked end-to-end. 1051 unit+integration (was 1004, +47). 11 e2e (unchanged file count; reorder walk extended in-place). ruff + mypy clean (21 source files, unchanged — PO5 extends `app/purchase_orders.py` in-place + adds a new template `purchase_order_receive_form.html`). |
| 28 | PO4 — Email the PO PDF to the supplier (DoD #6 partial) | `372f452` | Fourth link in DoD #6's chain (after PO1 dashboard, PO2 draft create, PO2b edit/cancel, PO3 PDF). New module `app/email_backend.py` exposes a tiny `EmailMessage` dataclass + `EmailAttachment` dataclass + `EmailBackend` Protocol + two implementations: `ConsoleEmailBackend` (records every sent message in a process-level `_CONSOLE_OUTBOX` list and prints a one-line dev summary to stdout) and `SmtpEmailBackend` (stdlib `smtplib.SMTP` over `email.message.EmailMessage` with optional STARTTLS + login). Factory `get_email_backend(settings)` reads `settings.email_backend` ("console" / "smtp") and returns the right impl; unknown values raise. Outbox helpers `console_outbox()` + `clear_console_outbox()` give tests in-memory access. New route `POST /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/send` (Manager + Office; Workshop + pending 403; anon 401), gated on `status == draft` (sent / partially_received / received / cancelled all 400 via the existing `_require_draft` predicate). Validation order (all 400 *before* delivery / DB write — atomic): (1) PO exists (404); (2) `_require_draft(po)` rejects non-drafts; (3) supplier resolves to an active row (archived → 400, defence in depth); (4) supplier has a non-blank email (None / blank → 400 with "supplier has no email address — add one before sending"). After validation: render PDF bytes via the existing `render_po_pdf` helper (same shape PO3's PDF route uses); build an `EmailMessage` (subject `"Purchase Order #{id} from UC"`; HTML body with greeting + PO id + line count + supplier name + optional `Expected delivery: {date}` + optional `Notes: {notes}` + "Reply to this email to confirm receipt" — no Jinja template; concatenated string for ~6 lines of HTML); attach the rendered PDF as `po-{id}.pdf` with content-type `application/pdf`; deliver via the configured backend. **Persist after delivery**: if `backend.send` raises, no DB change and no audit row (same atomic-on-raise contract as M3's insufficient-stock path). On success: `po.status = SENT`; `po.sent_at = datetime.now(UTC)`; record `purchase_order.sent` audit row with `before={"status": "draft"}`, `after={"status": "sent", "sent_at": iso, "to_email": supplier_email}`; commit; flash `"PO #{id} sent to {email}."`; 303 redirect to detail page. Detail template (`purchase_order_detail.html`) — draft branch — gains either a `data-testid="po-send-form"` form (when `supplier.email` is non-blank) with a `data-testid="po-send-submit"` button labelled "Send to supplier", or a `data-testid="po-send-blocked-no-email"` paragraph (when blank) — defence in depth for the same condition the route enforces. The cancel form stays on the page; the send form sits between edit and cancel. **Audit shape — `purchase_order.sent`**: mirrors `purchase_order.cancelled`'s minimal shape (status flip + minimal extras). Including `to_email` lets reporting trace "where did this PO get sent" without joining the supplier table on a name that may have rotated. SQLite stores `sent_at` as naive when read back via SQLAlchemy; tests attach UTC tzinfo for the comparison range — same idiom as cost-layer received_at tests. **`_make_supplier` test helper extended** with optional `email` kwarg (default `None`) so PO4 tests can seed suppliers with email; pre-existing PO2 / PO2b / PO3 callers unaffected (the new arg is keyword-only with a default). **`pyproject.toml`**: no new dependency (stdlib `smtplib` + `email.message`); `premailer` and `apscheduler` remain in the deps list but are still unused — flagged for cleanup in a future polish slice. **Tests** (26 new — all integration in `tests/integration/test_purchase_orders_routes.py`): `TestPOSendRoleEnforcement` (6) — anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403 (all assert empty outbox), Manager / Office / Admin 303; `TestPOSendStatusGuard` (5) — unknown 404, sent / partially_received / received / cancelled all 400 with empty outbox; `TestPOSendValidation` (3) — supplier with `email=None` → 400 + no audit + no status flip + sent_at still None + empty outbox; supplier with `email="   "` (whitespace) → 400 + empty outbox; supplier archived between draft and send → 400 + empty outbox; `TestPOSendHappyPath` (8) — status flips draft→sent; `sent_at` is set within ±2s of now; audit row carries `before={"status": "draft"}` + `after={"status": "sent", "sent_at": <iso>, "to_email": "supplier@example.test"}` with `sent_at` parseable as a tz-aware ISO timestamp; redirect target `/admin/purchase-orders/{id}`; flash includes the supplier email; outbox receives one message with the supplier email as recipient + subject `Purchase Order #{id} from UC` + body contains supplier name + body contains "1 line(s)" + one PDF attachment with filename `po-{id}.pdf` + `application/pdf` content-type + `%PDF` magic; double-send: second call 400s via status guard + outbox still has only one message + audit log still has only one sent row; failed validation (no-email supplier) writes no audit + no outbox message; `TestPOSendDetailRender` (4) — draft + supplier-with-email shows `po-send-form` + `po-send-submit` and hides the blocked-note; draft + supplier-without-email shows `po-send-blocked-no-email` and hides the form; sent renders `po-readonly-banner` and hides the send form; cancelled hides the send form. **Module-level `_reset_email_outbox` autouse fixture** drains the in-memory outbox before every test in this file — keeps assertions clean across cases without leaking across the rest of the suite (only the PO route tests use `console_outbox()`). **E2E walk extension** (in-place in `test_reorder_e2e.py`): the supplier-create step now fills `supplier-email-input` with `reorder-supplier@uc.test` so PO4's send route accepts the draft. After PO3's PDF-download leg, the manager navigates to the PO list, clicks `po-row-detail-link`, lands on the detail page, clicks `po-send-submit`, asserts `po-status-badge=sent` + `po-readonly-banner` visible + `po-edit-form`/`po-cancel-form`/`po-send-form` count=0 + `po-pdf-link` still visible (sent POs can re-download the PDF). The previous cancel leg is replaced — sent POs can't be cancelled (the cancel form's gone, and the route would reject anyway). 1004 unit+integration (was 978, +26). 11 e2e (unchanged file count; reorder walk extended in-place). ruff + mypy clean (21 source files, +1 — `app/email_backend.py`). |
| 27 | PO3 — PDF generation for a Purchase Order (DoD #6 partial) | `77acd26` | Third link in DoD #6's chain (after PO1 dashboard, PO2 draft create, PO2b edit/cancel). New module `app/pdf.py` with one public function `render_po_pdf(po, supplier, lines) -> bytes` using `reportlab.pdfgen.canvas.Canvas` on `BytesIO`. New route `GET /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/pdf` (Manager + Office; Workshop + pending 403; anon 401), 404 on unknown id, 400 on cancelled (nothing to send). All other statuses (draft / sent / partially_received / received) render fine. Disposition: `inline; filename="po-{id}.pdf"` so browsers preview in a new tab; PO4's email path can re-use the same bytes as a `MIMEApplication` attachment. Detail page (`purchase_order_detail.html`) gains a `data-testid="po-pdf-link"` link with `target="_blank" rel="noopener"`, gated on `po.status.value != "cancelled"`. **Backend choice — reportlab not WeasyPrint**: MISSION §5 lists both. WeasyPrint requires native libs (libgobject-2.0-0 / pango / cairo) that aren't installed on this dev / loop env — `import weasyprint` raises `OSError` before any rendering. Reportlab is pure-Python. `pyproject.toml`: `reportlab>=4.5.0` added, `weasyprint` removed; mypy override `weasyprint.*` → `reportlab.*`. Tradeoff is no HTML templating — layout is built imperatively via `Canvas`. Acceptable: a PO PDF is closer to a printed form than the on-screen page, and the surface is small. **Stream compression disabled** (`canvas.setPageCompression(0)`) so the supplier name, SKUs, line cells appear as ASCII in the byte stream — integration tests can byte-search the output without a PDF parser dependency. Documented in module docstring as a deliberate test-friendly tradeoff (~10 KB unc vs ~3 KB compressed for typical POs). **Layout** (single-page; multipage spillover is a polish follow-up): title `Purchase Order #{id}` + status text right-aligned; supplier block (with `(archived)` suffix when applicable) + meta block (created date + expected date); optional notes block; lines table with columns SKU / Name / Unit / Qty ordered / Unit cost / Line total drawn manually as rules + drawString cells; grand total at the bottom (sum of priced lines; "—" when no line carries a cost). **Audit / engine ownership**: read-only route — no audit row, no DB mutations, no engine call. **Tests** (24 new — all integration in `tests/integration/test_purchase_orders_routes.py`): `TestPOPdfRoleEnforcement` (6) — anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403, Manager / Office / Admin 200 + content-type starts with `application/pdf` + bytes start with `%PDF`; `TestPOPdfStatusGuard` (5) — unknown 404, cancelled 400, draft / sent / received all 200; `TestPOPdfContent` (9) — content-type + disposition (`inline; filename="po-{id}.pdf"`) + magic bytes + PO id appears in stream + supplier name appears + archived-supplier suffix `\(archived\)` (PDF parens are escaped) + SKU + qty (12.0000) + 2.5000 unit cost + Total: 30.00000000 (Decimal scale-multiplication: scale 4 + scale 4 = scale 8) + em-dash total for unpriced lines is `\227` octal-escape in the PDF stream; `TestPOPdfLink` (4) — draft / sent / received render the link with the right href; cancelled hides it. **E2E walk extension** (in-place in `test_reorder_e2e.py`): after the PO2b edit-save leg, the manager grabs `po-pdf-link.href` and fetches it via `mgr_page.request.get(...)` (rides the same session cookie + avoids Chromium's PDF-viewer load-event flakiness from `target="_blank"` clicks). Asserts response 200, `Content-Type: application/pdf`, `%PDF` byte magic, supplier name + SKU in the byte stream. After the cancel leg, asserts `po-pdf-link` count=0. 978 unit+integration (was 954, +24). 11 e2e passing (unchanged). ruff + mypy clean (20 source files, +1 — `app/pdf.py`). |
| 26 | PO2b — Edit + cancel a draft PO (DoD #6 partial) | `4714c26` | Closes the editable half of MISSION §3's "Expected unit cost on a PO line is editable by the user creating the PO" requirement. New routes `POST /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}` and `POST /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}/cancel`, both **Manager + Office** (Workshop + pending 403; anon 401), both gated on `status == draft` (anything else 400 with the message naming the current status). The detail page (`purchase_order_detail.html`) is now bimodal: when `po.status == draft` it renders an *edit form* — line cells become inputs (`po-edit-qty-input` / `po-edit-cost-input`) plus top-level `expected_date` / `notes` inputs plus a separate cancel form; when not draft, the existing read-only cells render plus a `po-readonly-banner` paragraph naming the status. The existing PO2 test-ids on read-only cells (`po-line-qty-ordered` / `po-line-expected-unit-cost` / `po-line-cost-empty`) are preserved on the non-draft branch only; PO2's `test_lines_render_in_sku_order_with_correct_cells` and `test_null_expected_cost_shows_empty_marker` were flipped to `POStatus.SENT` to keep exercising that path. **`update_purchase_order` is `async def`** because it `await request.form()` — the per-line `qty_ordered_<line_id>` / `expected_unit_cost_<line_id>` keys aren't known at function-signature time. Same precedent as `app/items.py`'s `create_item` / `update_item` for custom-field reads. **Validation order** (all 400 *before* any DB mutation — atomic): (1) PO exists (404); (2) `_require_draft(po)` rejects non-drafts; (3) parse top-level `expected_date` (blank → None; ISO else 400) and `notes` (strip; blank → None; ≤ 2000 chars else 400); (4) iterate the existing PO lines, parsing each `qty_ordered_<line_id>` (positive Decimal else 400) and `expected_unit_cost_<line_id>` (blank → None; non-negative Decimal else 400). Missing line keys 400; extra form keys whose `line_id` suffix isn't on the PO are silently ignored. **Sparse diff is computed in memory before any mutation** so a no-op submit can be detected and the audit row skipped entirely. Top-level fields enter `before` / `after` only when changed; per-line entries enter `before["lines"]` / `after["lines"]` only for lines where at least one field changed (each entry carries `{line_id}` plus only the changed field(s)). No-op submit writes no audit row but still 303s with a flash ("no changes"). **Decimal equality across scale boundaries**: Python's Decimal compares numerically equal across scales (`Decimal("10") == Decimal("10.0000")` → True), so the diff logic correctly identifies a re-submitted same-value as a no-op. Stringification for audit uses `str(decimal)` which preserves the *parsed* scale. **`_require_draft` shared by both edit and cancel** so the status-guard rule is consistent. **Cancel is non-destructive on lines**: `PurchaseOrder.status` flips, lines stay attached. No-undo path. Idempotency rejection: cancelling an already-cancelled PO 400s, pinned by `test_cancel_already_cancelled_po_is_400`. **Audit shape — `purchase_order.updated`**: sparse `before` / `after` containing only the changed top-level fields, plus a `lines` list of `{line_id, <changed_field>, ...}` entries when any lines changed. Decimals via `str(decimal)`; null costs serialise as `None`. **Audit shape — `purchase_order.cancelled`**: `before={"status": "draft"}`, `after={"status": "cancelled"}`. **Templates / test-ids**: `po-edit-form` (top-level form), `po-edit-expected-date-input`, `po-edit-notes-input`, per-line `po-edit-qty-input` (with `data-line-id`), `po-edit-cost-input` (with `data-line-id`), `po-edit-submit`, `po-cancel-form`, `po-cancel-submit`, `po-readonly-banner` (only on non-draft renders). The expected_date / notes `<dl>` rows moved into the read-only branch (drafts show inputs instead). `is_draft` added to the detail-page context. **Tests** (43 new = 43 integration in `tests/integration/test_purchase_orders_routes.py` + 2 existing PO2 tests flipped to `POStatus.SENT` + e2e walk in `test_reorder_e2e.py` extended in-place): `TestPOEditRoleEnforcement` (9) — anon POST update / cancel 401; pending POST update / cancel 403; Workshop POST update / cancel 403; Manager POST update + cancel 303; Office POST update 303. `TestPOEditStatusGuard` (6) — edit `sent` / `received` 400; cancel `sent` / already-cancelled 400; edit / cancel unknown 404. `TestPOEditValidation` (11) — bad `expected_date` 400; `notes` over 2000 chars 400; qty blank / zero / negative / non-numeric (4); cost negative / non-numeric (2); missing line qty key 400; missing line cost key 400; failed validation writes no audit + leaves the line value unchanged. `TestPOEditHappyPath` (8) — change qty writes audit with `{line_id, qty_ordered: "10.0000"} → {line_id, qty_ordered: "42"}` shape (no top-level keys when only lines changed); change cost from null → value; clear cost to null; change top-level notes; change top-level expected_date; multi-line edit emits only the changed line in the audit list; no-op submit writes no audit row; flash on save includes "saved". `TestPOCancelHappyPath` (4) — status flips to `cancelled`; audit row carries the correct before/after + entity_id; redirects to detail; lines + supplier preserved. `TestPODetailRenderDelta` (5) — draft renders `po-edit-form` + `po-cancel-submit`; sent renders `po-readonly-banner` + no edit form; received renders read-only; cancelled renders read-only; draft input pre-fills with line's qty (10 → "10.0000"). E2E walk extension (in-place): after the existing dashboard / PO-create leg lands the manager on the draft PO detail page, the manager fills `po-edit-qty-input=120` + `po-edit-cost-input=1.50` + `po-edit-notes-input="rush order"`, clicks `po-edit-submit`, asserts the redirect re-renders the form with `po-edit-qty-input=120.0000` + `po-edit-cost-input=1.5000` + `po-edit-notes-input="rush order"`. Then navigates to PO list via `nav-pos`, clicks `po-row-detail-link`, lands on the detail page, clicks `po-cancel-submit`, asserts the redirect lands on the same detail URL with `po-status-badge=cancelled` + `po-readonly-banner` visible + `po-edit-form` count=0. 954 unit+integration (was 911, +43). 11 e2e (unchanged file count; reorder walk extended in-place). ruff + mypy clean (19 source files, unchanged — PO2b extends `app/purchase_orders.py` in-place). |
| 25 | PO2 — Draft PO from a supplier group on the reorder dashboard (DoD #6 partial) | `032a51d` | First write surface in the PO/reorder track. New module `app/purchase_orders.py` with three routes: `POST /admin/reorder/draft-po` (Manager + Office; Workshop + pending 403) creates a draft PO from a supplier group; `GET /admin/purchase-orders` lists POs (newest-first, status-filterable); `GET /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}` is the read-only detail view. New tables `purchase_orders` + `purchase_order_lines` (migration `0011_create_purchase_orders.py`); new `POStatus` enum (draft / sent / partially_received / received / cancelled — only `draft` is written in PO2). New templates `purchase_order_detail.html` + `purchase_orders_list.html`. New `nav-pos` link in `base.html` for admin/manager/office. The `reorder_dashboard.html` gains a "Draft PO" form button per **active** supplier group; archived-supplier groups and the "(no supplier)" group render distinct blocked-notes (`reorder-draft-po-blocked-archived` / `reorder-draft-po-blocked-no-supplier`) with copy explaining what to fix before drafting. **Validation on create** (all 400 *before* any DB write — atomic): `supplier_id` parses as int (blank/non-int reject); references an **active** supplier (unknown/archived reject — defence in depth: the dashboard already hides the button on archived-supplier groups, but a stale POST is still rejected); the supplier must have at least one active item below threshold (else 400 — typically a stale POST after a concurrent stock-in). The query mirrors PO1's WHERE clause: `Item.archived_at IS NULL AND Item.supplier_id == :sid AND Item.current_qty <= Item.reorder_threshold` ordered by SKU. **Default `qty_ordered` per line**: `item.reorder_qty` when > 0 (manager intent); else the deficit `threshold - current_qty` when > 0 (deficit signal); else `Decimal("1")` (at-threshold-zero-reorder cohort — order at least one to keep the line meaningful, operator can edit later). **Default `expected_unit_cost` per line**: most recent `CostLayer.unit_cost` for the item (`ORDER BY received_at DESC, id DESC`), regardless of `qty_remaining` (a fully-consumed layer is still the most recent unit-cost signal); `None` when no layers exist yet. **Audit shape** — `purchase_order.created`: `entity_type="purchase_order"`, `entity_id=po.id`, `before=None`, `after={supplier_id, status="draft", expected_date=None, notes=None, lines=[{item_id, qty_ordered (str), expected_unit_cost (str|None)}, ...]}`. Decimals stringified with column-quantised scale 4 (e.g. `"50.0000"`) — same wart / convention as M2 / M3 / M4 / M5. **Out of scope (deferred to PO2b)**: edit qty / expected_unit_cost / notes / expected_date on a draft PO; cancel a draft. PO2b will add `POST /admin/purchase-orders/{po_id}` + `purchase_order.updated` audit + the line-level inputs in the detail template. **Schema details**: `purchase_order_lines.po_id` is `ON DELETE CASCADE` (forward-looking parity for a future "destroy a never-sent draft" UX); `qty_received` defaults to `Decimal("0")` with `server_default '0'`; `expected_unit_cost` is nullable; `status` has both Python `default=POStatus.DRAFT` and `server_default='draft'` (belt-and-braces matching `current_qty` / `reorder_threshold` on Items). **No FK on `stock_movements.po_id`** — column is currently always NULL (no PO5 yet); adding the FK is PO5's job. **Templates / test-ids**: detail page — `po-detail-heading` / `po-status-badge` (with `data-status`) / `po-supplier-name` / `po-created-by` / `po-created-at` / `po-expected-date` / `po-notes` / `po-lines-table` / `po-line-row` (with `data-line-id` + `data-item-id`) / `po-line-sku` / `po-line-name` / `po-line-qty-ordered` / `po-line-qty-received` / `po-line-expected-unit-cost` (with `po-line-cost-empty` `<span>` when null) / `po-back-to-list` / `po-back-to-reorder`. List page — `po-list-heading` / `po-status-filter-form` / `po-status-filter` (auto-submitting `<select>`) / `po-list-empty` / `po-list-table` / per-row `po-row` (with `data-po-id`) / `po-row-supplier` / `po-row-status` / `po-row-line-count` / `po-row-created-at` / `po-row-detail-link`. **`nav-reorder` aria-current tightened** from `startswith("/admin/reorder")` to `path == "/admin/reorder" or startswith("/admin/reorder/")` so the future GET endpoints under `/admin/reorder/*` (and the POST target `/admin/reorder/draft-po`) don't accidentally light up the same nav state — pinned by `test_reorder_aria_current_does_not_match_pos_path`. **Tests** (47 new = 38 in new `tests/integration/test_purchase_orders_routes.py` + 6 in `tests/integration/test_layout.py` for `nav-pos` + 3 in `tests/integration/test_reorder_routes.py` for the draft-PO button / blocked-notes; e2e walk extended in-place): role enforcement (10) — anon GET-list / GET-detail / POST-create all 401; pending POST 403; Workshop POST + GET-list + GET-detail all 403; Manager / Office / Admin all 200 GET; POST validation (7) — blank / non-int / unknown / archived supplier; supplier with zero low-stock items 400; supplier with only above-threshold items 400; failed validation writes no PO + no lines + no audit; POST happy path (7) — single-line PO carries supplier/status/created_by/qty_ordered/qty_received/expected_unit_cost + redirect target; multi-line ordered by SKU (insert C-3/A-1/B-2 → asserted as A-1/B-2/C-3); above-threshold excluded; archived items excluded; other suppliers' items excluded; audit row carries full lines snapshot; flash includes supplier name + line count; default qty_ordered (3) — reorder_qty > 0 → that value; reorder_qty=0 + deficit > 0 → deficit; both zero → fallback 1; default expected_unit_cost (3) — no layers → null; single layer → its unit_cost; multi-layer → newest wins (oldest 2.00 + newest 3.00 → 3.00); GET list (4) — empty state; populated newest-first; `?status_filter=draft` excludes sent; `?status_filter=sent` excludes drafts; GET detail (4) — unknown 404; populated heading + supplier + status badge; lines in SKU order with right cells; null expected_unit_cost shows the empty marker; layout / nav (6) — manager / office / admin nav-pos visible; workshop nav-pos hidden; aria-current on `/admin/purchase-orders`; nav-reorder aria-current does NOT match PO list page; reorder dashboard (3) — active-supplier group renders form + button + hidden supplier_id + CSRF; archived-supplier group renders blocked-archived note (no button within section); no-supplier group renders blocked-no-supplier note. E2E walk extension: after the existing dashboard load showing `RD-LOW`, the manager clicks `reorder-draft-po-button`, lands on the PO detail page (asserts heading + 1 line + supplier="Reorder Bullion Co" + status="draft" + line sku=RD-LOW + qty_ordered=100.0000 + `po-line-cost-empty` visible), navigates via `nav-pos` to PO list (asserts 1 row, supplier name, line_count=1), navigates back via `nav-reorder` (asserts RD-LOW still listed — PO doesn't change current_qty), continues with existing stock-in → empty-dashboard cleanup. 911 unit+integration (was 864, +47). 11 e2e (unchanged file count; reorder walk extended in-place). ruff + mypy clean (19 source files, +1 — `app/purchase_orders.py`). |
| 24 | PO1 — Reorder dashboard (items below threshold, grouped by supplier) (DoD #6 partial) | `dc871cb` | First read-only surface in the PO/reorder track. New module `app/reorder.py` (single `GET /admin/reorder` route for **Manager + Office**, Workshop + pending blocked); new template `reorder_dashboard.html`; new `nav-reorder` link in `base.html` for admin/manager/office. **Query**: `select(Item, Supplier).outerjoin(Supplier, Item.supplier_id == Supplier.id).where(Item.archived_at.is_(None)).where(Item.current_qty <= Item.reorder_threshold).order_by(Item.sku)` — single round-trip. Bucketing happens in Python: rows group by `supplier_id` (or `None` for the "(no supplier)" synthetic bucket), named-supplier groups sort alphabetically (by un-suffixed name so archived suppliers don't slide to the bottom), no-supplier bucket renders last. Within each group rows preserve the SQL-side SKU order. **Per-row view shape**: `{item, current_qty, threshold, reorder_qty, deficit}` where `deficit = reorder_threshold - current_qty` (always ≥ 0). **Edge cases pinned by tests**: trigger is `<=` not `<` (item at exactly threshold is included, deficit=0); zero threshold + zero qty is included (deficit=0; the user can choose to set a real threshold or order some); zero threshold + positive qty is excluded; archived items hidden at SQL level; archived suppliers still bucket their items but the label is suffixed `" (archived)"` and the section carries `data-supplier-archived="true"`; clearing an item's supplier drops it into the "(no supplier)" bucket the next request. **No mutations, no audit, no movement type** — PO1 is purely a read; PO2 lands the first write surface. **Templates / test-ids**: `reorder-heading`, whole-page empty `reorder-empty` ("Nothing to reorder right now — every active item is above its threshold"), per-supplier `reorder-group` (with `data-supplier-id="<id|none>"`), `reorder-supplier-name` heading, `reorder-table`, per-row `reorder-row` (`data-item-id`), per-cell `reorder-item-sku` / `reorder-item-name` / `reorder-current-qty` / `reorder-threshold` / `reorder-reorder-qty` / `reorder-deficit`, row links `reorder-detail-link` (→ `/admin/items/{id}/detail`) and `reorder-stock-in-link` (→ `/admin/items/{id}/in`). The links reuse the existing `/admin/items/...` URLs — no new routes. `base.html` extends the role-gated nav block: `nav-reorder` shown for `("admin", "manager", "office")`; matches the route's `require_role(MANAGER, OFFICE)`. **Tests** (28 new = 23 in `tests/integration/test_reorder_routes.py` + 5 in `tests/integration/test_layout.py` + 1 e2e walk in new `tests/e2e/test_reorder_e2e.py`): role enforcement (6) — anon 401, pending 403, Workshop 403, Office / Manager / Admin 200; empty state (2) — no items + only above-threshold items both render the empty block and no groups/rows; single-supplier rendering (3) — one group + one row, all cells correct (qty=3, threshold=10, reorder_qty=100, deficit=7), row links to detail + stock-in; no-supplier bucket (2) — `supplier_id IS NULL` renders `data-supplier-id="none"`; coexists with named-supplier groups; multiple suppliers (2) — groups alphabetical (Alpha → Zebra), rows within a group SKU-ordered (A-1 → B-2 → C-3); threshold edge (2) — `qty == threshold` included with deficit=0, `qty > threshold` excluded; archived item filter (2) — archived items hidden, un-archive restores; archived supplier surfacing (2) — "(archived)" suffix + `data-supplier-archived="true"`, clearing item.supplier_id drops it into "(no supplier)"; zero threshold (2) — included when qty=0, excluded when qty>0; layout / nav (5) — manager/office/admin nav-reorder visible, workshop nav-reorder hidden, aria-current on `/admin/reorder`. E2E walk: a fresh manager (promoted from pending by admin) creates supplier "Reorder Bullion Co" + category "Reorder E2E Cat" + two items (`RD-LOW`, `RD-OK`) both with threshold=10 and that supplier; stocks `RD-OK` in by 50 to push it above threshold; visits `/admin/reorder` via `nav-reorder`; asserts the supplier-name heading + only `RD-LOW` row visible (not `RD-OK`); asserts the row's `reorder-current-qty=0.0000` / `reorder-threshold=10.0000` / `reorder-deficit=10.0000`; clicks the row's `reorder-stock-in-link`, lands on `/admin/items/{low_id}/in`; records 25 units; returns to `/admin/reorder` via `nav-reorder` and asserts `reorder-empty` is visible + `reorder-row` count is 0. Cleanup archives both items + the supplier + the category. 864 unit+integration (was 836, +28). 11 e2e (was 10, +1 — new walk file). ruff + mypy clean (18 source files, +1 — `app/reorder.py`). |
| 23 | M5 — Transfer between locations (no cost change) (DoD #3 partial, DoD #5 partial) | `33aa662` | Closes out the cost-engine call-site matrix: TRANSFER is the **only** `MovementType` that bypasses the engine, by design (no qty change → no cost change). New routes `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/transfer` + `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/transfer` mounted on the existing `app/movements.py` router for **Manager + Office + Workshop** (same matrix as M2 / M3 / M4 / M6). The route flips `item.location_id` from the current value to the user-picked target, records a `StockMovement(type=TRANSFER, total_cost=None)`, writes a `stock_movement.transfer` audit row, commits — never calls the cost engine, never creates a `CostLayer`, never writes a `CostLayerConsumption`, never mutates `item.current_qty`. Verified by three engine-isolation tests that seed a layer + run a transfer + assert the layer count + qty_remaining + current_qty are all unchanged. **Validation** (all 400 *before* any DB write — atomic): item exists (404), not archived (400), has a current `location_id` set (400 with "this item has no location yet — set one via the edit form before transferring"); `to_location_id` parses as int and references an *active* `Location` (archived rejects with "target location is not active"); `to_location_id` differs from current `location_id` (no-op transfers reject — would write a confusing audit row); `qty` parses as a positive Decimal (reuses `_parse_positive_decimal`). `reason` and `note` are optional, stripped, blank → None — same as M2 / M3; M4 differs because adjustments require attribution, transfers don't. **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.transfer"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before={"location_id": from_location_id}`, `after={"item_id", "qty", "from_location_id", "to_location_id", "reason", "note"}`. The `before` carries only the changed field on the *Item* (location_id), keeping the diff narrow and consistent with `item.updated`. The `after` carries the route inputs plus the resolved from/to ids so the row is self-contained for reporting (a future "by-location movement report" can read this row without joining back to movement + item). `from_location_id` appears in both `before` and `after` — that's deliberate, not a bug. **No `total_cost`, no `unit_cost`, no `source`, no `received_at`** — transfer rows don't carry any of those, consistent with the contract. **Form**: `qty` defaults to `current_qty` (most transfers are wholesale). For v1's single-location-per-item data model, the qty is informational on the movement row only — the location pointer flips wholesale regardless. Documented in the form's `<em>recorded for audit; transfers don't change current quantity or valuation</em>` hint. Partial-qty transfers across multiple "stock-by-location" rows is a v2 multi-location feature; out of scope for v1 per MISSION §3 "between locations (if multi-location is enabled)". **No in-form 400 carve-out** (unlike M3 / M4): every M5 validation error is a "user typed something wrong" case (parse error, out-of-options, same-as-current), not a data-dependent runtime error like M3's insufficient-stock. The bare-400 page is the right posture, matching every other validation 400 in the app outside M3 / M4's data-dependent stock-shortage carve-out. **M6 timeline integration**: TRANSFER rows render with no direction sign — `_attach_directions_and_breakdown`'s defensive `else` branch (already in place from M6) leaves `direction = ""` for movements that don't go through the engine. The `data-direction=""` attribute lets future styling distinguish them. The `total_cost` cell shows "—" via the existing `{% if m.total_cost is not none %}` template logic. **No layer-breakdown sub-row** appears for transfers (since the engine never wrote a consumption row for them). M6 needs zero template changes for M5. From/to location names are *not* surfaced on the timeline (they live in the audit row only) — flagged as a self-critique item for a future enrichment slice. **Templates**: new `stock_transfer_form.html` mirroring `stock_out_form.html`'s shape (header + form + recent-movements). Test-ids: `stock-transfer-form`, `stock-transfer-from-location` (read-only display of the current location label), `stock-transfer-to-location-input`, `stock-transfer-qty-input`, `stock-transfer-reason-input`, `stock-transfer-note-input`, `stock-transfer-submit`. `items_form.html` gains a fourth `data-testid="stock-transfer-link"` "Transfer →" link in the same `{% if not item.archived_at %}` block as in / out / adjust. `item_detail.html` likewise gets a `data-testid="stock-transfer-link"` action link in the same block. **Tests** (46 new = 46 integration + 1 e2e walk extension): role enforcement (10) — anon GET / POST 401, pending 403, every active role 200 GET / 303 POST; form rendering (10) — inputs + CSRF + current-location label + to-location options exclude the current AND archived rows + qty pre-fills with current_qty + recent-movements empty + 404 / 400 / item-without-location-400; validation matrix (13) — blank to_location 400, non-int 400, unknown 400, archived target 400, same-as-current 400, qty 4 cases, archived item POST 400, item-without-location POST 400, no-audit-on-failure, no-flip-on-failure across two paths (same-location reject + bad-target reject); happy path (5) — creates `StockMovement(type=TRANSFER)` with the right fields incl. total_cost=None, flips item.location_id, audit row with the right before/after shape, whitespace strip on reason, blank reason/note → None, flash includes "Transfer recorded" + both location names + item name; engine isolation (3) — no `CostLayer` / `CostLayerConsumption` / `current_qty` mutation; transfer link visibility (2) — visible for active item, hidden for archived; detail-page rendering (2) — TRANSFER row with `data-direction=""` + "—" total cost + no layer-breakdown follow-up; detail-link visibility (2) — visible / hidden the same way. E2E walk in-place extension: manager-setup step now creates two locations (`Movements From Bench`, `Movements To Storage`) and pins the item to the first; after the existing M6 detail-page leg (current_qty=75), workshop deep-links to `/transfer`, asserts the from-location shows "Movements From Bench", picks "Movements To Storage" by label, fills qty=75 + reason="end of shift", submits, asserts the flash + movement-row + flipped from-location label after the redirect (now showing "Movements To Storage"); re-visits detail and asserts 5 timeline rows + 2 layer-breakdown blocks (TRANSFER doesn't add one) + current_qty unchanged at 75.0000. Cleanup-step extension: manager archives both locations after the item + category. 836 unit+integration (was 790, +46). 10 e2e (unchanged file count; movements walk gained one new step). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files, unchanged — M5 extends `app/movements.py` in-place). |
| 22 | I1c — Workshop read-only items list + read-only item form (DoD #3 partial, DoD #9 partial) | `8874f33` | Closes the UX gap flagged by M2 / M3 / M4 / M6 self-critiques: Workshop has had deep-link access to `/in`, `/out`, `/adjust`, `/detail` for four iterations but no UI path to *find* an item without a deep-link or QR scan. After I1c, Workshop browses `/admin/items` (read-only) and clicks into a per-item read-only view that surfaces every metadata field (category, unit, tracking mode, supplier, location, QR code, notes, custom field values) — a superset of what M6's detail page shows. Implementation is small and boring: extend `require_role` on `GET /admin/items` and `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/edit` to include `Role.WORKSHOP`; add a `_can_save_item(user) -> bool` predicate (Manager + Office + Admin); thread two new template flags (`can_edit_item` on the list, `can_save` on the form); add a single `{% set ro = ... %}` line in `items_form.html` that drives every input's `disabled` attribute and hides the submit button. The POST routes (create / update / archive / unarchive) keep their existing `require_role(MANAGER)` / `require_role(MANAGER, OFFICE)` shape — Workshop hits them with 403, server-enforced, with the disabled form inputs as defense-in-depth. **Routes**: `list_items` and `edit_item_form` add `Role.WORKSHOP` to the allowed list. `edit_item_form` branches the title between "Edit {name}" (saveable) and "View {name}" (read-only) based on `_can_save_item`. `new_item_form` sets `can_save=True` always (Workshop can't reach it, but the flag has to be set for the template not to crash). `GET /admin/items/new` still 403s for Workshop. **Templates**: `items_list.html` per-row Edit link conditionally renders as `<a data-testid="view-item">View</a>` when `not can_edit_item` — same URL either way (Workshop's POST path already 403s); the label is just honest. The existing `can_create` / `can_archive` flags already evaluated to False for Workshop because Workshop role isn't in `(MANAGER, ADMIN)`, so the New CTA + per-row archive/unarchive forms hide automatically. `items_form.html` gets a single `{% set ro = (can_save is defined) and (not can_save) %}` at the top that drives every input's `disabled` attribute (sku, name, category, unit, tracking_mode, requires_checkout, reorder_threshold, reorder_qty, supplier, location, qr_code, notes — plus every custom-field input across all seven types). The submit button is wrapped in `{% if not ro %}` so it disappears entirely. A `<p data-testid="item-form-readonly-note"><em>View only — your role cannot edit items.</em></p>` block renders above the form when `ro`. The "Stock in / Stock out / Adjust / Details / Manage units" action links remain visible (they're gated on `not item.archived_at`, not on role — Workshop has access to all of them). `base.html` extends the `nav-items` visibility from `("admin", "manager", "office")` to `("admin", "manager", "office", "workshop")` — the `aria-current` on `/admin/items*` paths still works. **Tests** (16 new = 6 in `TestRoleEnforcement` + 10 in new `TestWorkshopReadOnlyView` + 1 layout flip + 1 e2e leg-set in the existing movements walk): `TestRoleEnforcement` flips `test_workshop_get_list_is_403` → `test_workshop_get_list_is_200`; adds `test_workshop_get_edit_form_is_200`, `test_workshop_get_new_form_is_403`, `test_workshop_update_is_403` (POST returns 403 + sku/name unchanged + no audit row), `test_workshop_archive_is_403`, `test_workshop_unarchive_is_403`. New `TestWorkshopReadOnlyView` class (10 tests): list shows `view-item` not `edit-item` link for Workshop; list hides `new-item` CTA for Workshop; list hides `archive-item`/`unarchive-item` forms for Workshop; list shows `edit-item` link for Office (sanity-check the link-label split); form inputs all carry `disabled` attribute (sampled across 10 test-ids: sku/name/category/unit/tracking-mode/requires-checkout/supplier/location/qr/notes — checks the `<input>`/`<select>`/`<textarea>` tag for the literal `disabled` substring); form has no `item-submit` for Workshop; form renders the read-only note for Workshop; form title is "View {name}" for Workshop; form still shows `stock-in-link`/`stock-out-link`/`stock-adjust-link`/`detail-link` for Workshop; form disables custom-field inputs for Workshop; form for Manager keeps the submit + omits the read-only note (sanity). `test_layout.py` flips `test_workshop_nav_excludes_items_link` → `test_workshop_nav_includes_items_link`. E2E walk in-place extension: in `test_movements_e2e.py`'s existing single walk, after the Workshop user signs in and asserts the welcome heading + `nav-items` is now visible, Workshop navigates via the nav link, asserts the New CTA is hidden, sees the manager-created `MV-E2E-001` row, asserts `view-item` is the per-row link (not `edit-item`) and `archive-item` is hidden, clicks `view-item`, asserts the form renders with the read-only note + no submit + the `stock-in-link` still visible. Walk file's docstring + step-4 comment updated for the new posture (the I3a-era "Workshop still 403s on items list" line is gone). 790 unit+integration (was 774, +16). 10 e2e (unchanged file count; movements walk gained one new leg at step 6 between sign-in and the deep-link to /in). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files, unchanged — I1c extends `app/items.py` in-place; new helper `_can_save_item` adds 8 lines). |
| 21 | M6 — Item detail page: open layers + paginated movement timeline + per-row layer breakdown (DoD #5 partial, DoD #3 partial) | `f3d05f9` | Read-only consolidation of the cost-engine outputs into one page. Until M6 the only "view" surfaces for movements were the per-form recent-movements lists in M2 / M3 / M4 (each capped at LIMIT 10). M6 introduces `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/detail` on `app/movements.py` for **Manager + Office + Workshop** that surfaces: (1) item header (name + sku + archived flag), (2) summary line (current_qty + open_value + reorder_threshold), (3) action links (in / out / adjust + edit when `_can_edit_item` + manage units when unique-tracked), (4) open-cost-layer table (FIFO order; columns received_at / qty_received / qty_remaining / unit_cost / source / source_movement_id), (5) paginated movement timeline (`?page=N&page_size=20`) with per-row sign + total_cost + actor + reason, plus a follow-up `<tr>` carrying the **layer breakdown** for OUT and negative-adjustment rows (one `<li>` per consumption — qty_consumed × unit_cost_at_consumption + the layer's received_at). No mutations, no audit, no form. **Engine ownership maintained**: route never touches `item.current_qty`, `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `movement.total_cost`. **Helpers added next to the existing `_recent_movements`:** `_PAGE_SIZE = 20`; `_open_layers(db, item_id)` (FIFO-ordered open layers as view-shaped dicts); `_count_movements(db, item_id)` (int total); `_movements_page(db, item_id, page, page_size)` (newest-first page as view-shaped dicts; same outer-join-on-User pattern as `_recent_movements`); `_attach_directions_and_breakdown(db, movements)` (mutates the page in place to add `direction` and `breakdown` keys); `_paginate(total, page, page_size)` (clamps out-of-range to `[1, page_count]` — silent friendlier UX vs 400 on stale links); `_can_edit_item(user)` (Manager + Office + Admin — drives edit-link visibility, hides for Workshop). **Direction derivation**: a movement gets `+` if there's a row in `cost_layers` with `source_movement_id == movement.id` (IN or POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT), `-` if there are rows in `cost_layer_consumptions` with `movement_id == movement.id` (OUT or negative-adjustment). For TRANSFER (M5, future) — neither — the defensive `else` branch leaves direction `""`. Two batched queries per page (one for the layer-side ids, one for the consumption rows + their parent layers' received_at). **Archived-item posture**: the detail page renders for archived items (it's a read of history) but suppresses the in / out / adjust action links — same `{% if not item.archived_at %}` posture as `items_form.html`. The `data-testid="item-detail-archived"` marker tells the user. Different from M2 / M3 / M4 form routes which 400 archived (write paths reject; read paths allow). **Templates**: new `item_detail.html` with seven test-id surfaces — `item-detail-heading` / `item-detail-sku` / `item-current-qty` / `item-open-value` / `item-detail-threshold` / `item-detail-archived`;
