`cost-layers-table` with per-row `cost-layer-row` (and per-cell `cost-layer-qty-remaining` / `cost-layer-unit-cost` / `cost-layer-source`) plus empty state `cost-layers-empty`; `movements-timeline` with per-row `timeline-row` (carrying `data-direction` plus `timeline-qty` showing the signed qty and `timeline-total-cost`); per-row `layer-breakdown` (a follow-up `<tr>` containing a `<ul>` of `layer-breakdown-row` items) appearing only for non-empty breakdowns; pagination footer `pagination` with either `pagination-single-page` (when `page_count <= 1`) or `pagination-info` + conditional `pagination-prev` / `pagination-next` links carrying `?page=…` URLs. `items_list.html` gains a per-row `data-testid="detail-link"` "Details" link in the Actions cell; `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="detail-link"` "Details →" link in the same `{% if item %}` block as the in / out / adjust links — visible on every saved item including archived ones (detail read works for archived). **Tests** (34 new = 34 integration + 1 e2e leg-set in the existing movements walk): role enforcement (6) — anon 401, pending 403, Workshop / Office / Manager / Admin all 200; rendering (5) — unknown 404, archived item still renders + shows archived flag + suppresses in/out/adjust links, header content, action-link visibility for active item, workshop sees in/out/adjust but not the edit-item-link; cost layers (5) — empty state, single layer, multi-layer mixed sources, fully-consumed layer excluded, FIFO ordering; movements timeline (6) — empty state, IN row direction `+` + `total_cost = 14.00`, OUT row direction `-` + `total_cost = 6.0000` (column-quantised scale 4), adjustment-increase `+`, adjustment-decrease `-`, ordering newest-first; layer breakdown (5) — IN no breakdown, OUT 1 breakdown row, negative-adjustment 1 breakdown row, positive-adjustment no breakdown, multi-layer OUT 2 breakdown rows; pagination (5) — empty state replaces pagination, single-page footer for ≤ page_size, 2-page navigation (page 1 has 20 rows + next, page 2 has 1 row + prev), `?page=99` clamps to last, `?page=0` clamps to first; detail-link visibility (2) — items list + edit form. E2E walk extended in-place: after the M4 leg-2 (decrease, current_qty=75), workshop deep-links to `/detail`. Asserts: heading contains "Casting alloy", current_qty=75.0000, open_value contains "197.5" (= 55 × 2.50 + 20 × 3.00), 2 cost-layer rows, 4 timeline rows, 2 layer-breakdown blocks (one for OUT, one for adjust-decrease — IN and adjust-increase don't get one), pagination-single-page visible, in-link visible, edit-link absent (workshop role). 774 unit+integration (was 740, +34). 10 e2e (unchanged file count; movements walk gained one new step). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files, unchanged — M6 extends `app/movements.py` in-place). |
| 20 | M4 — Adjustment movements (positive creates layer; negative consumes FIFO; reason required) (DoD #5 partial, DoD #3 partial) | `5a94cd7` | Pairs the receipt path (`record_receipt` with `source=POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT`) with the consume path (`consume_fifo`) under one route surface. New routes `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/adjust` + `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/adjust` mounted on the existing `app/movements.py` router (no new module — every helper overlaps with M2 / M3). Same role surface as M2 / M3: **Manager + Office + Workshop**. POST handler **dispatches on direction**: `increase` calls `record_receipt(..., source=CostLayerSource.POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT, received_at=now())` (creates a layer + bumps current_qty + sets total_cost); `decrease` calls `consume_fifo(...)` (writes consumption rows + decrements current_qty + sets total_cost from the layer-weighted sum). Either path writes a single `stock_movement.adjustment` audit row, commits, 303s back with a flash. **The decrease path inherits M3's atomic-on-raise insufficient-stock contract verbatim** — `InsufficientStockError` caught, `db.rollback()` drops the orphan movement, item reloaded, form re-rendered at HTTP 400 with `<p data-testid="stock-adjust-error" role="alert">Not enough stock: requested {N}, only {M} available.</p>` block above the form + the user's qty / direction / unit_cost / reason / note all preserved. Same carve-out justification as M3 — data-dependent failure where re-typing is more friction than re-typing a typo. **Two new helpers** added next to the existing parsers: `_parse_required_reason(raw) -> str` (strip; blank → 400 — distinct from M2 / M3 where reason is optional, because MISSION §3 requires variance attribution); `_parse_direction(raw) -> str` (must be `"increase"` or `"decrease"` — anything else 400). New `_render_stock_adjust_form` factors GET-and-error rendering symmetric with M3's `_render_stock_out_form`. **Engine wiring**: route never touches `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `cost_layer_consumptions.*`, `movement.total_cost`, or `item.current_qty` — those are M1's invariants and the engine is the single owner. **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.adjustment"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after`. **Increase**: `{item_id, qty, direction="increase", unit_cost, total_cost, source="positive_adjustment", reason, note, received_at}`. **Decrease**: `{item_id, qty, direction, total_cost, reason, note}` — **no `unit_cost` / `source` / `received_at`** (varies per layer; matches M3's stock-out audit shape). Decimals stringified via `str(decimal)`; the increase audit's `total_cost` carries the input scale (e.g. `"12.50"` from `5 × 2.50`) because `record_receipt` computes the product in memory before the column round-trip; decrease's `total_cost` carries column-quantised scale 4 (e.g. `"8.0000"`) because the engine reads `layer.unit_cost` back from `Numeric(14,4)` storage — same wart as M3's stock-out audit. **`unit_cost` field is rendered for both directions** but only parsed + required for increase. For decrease, garbage in the field is accepted and ignored — the form's `<em>required for increases; ignored for decreases</em>` hint is the user-facing cue. Conservative; doesn't need HTMX swap (still no concrete second use case for HTMX). **Templates**: new `stock_adjust_form.html` mirroring `stock_out_form.html` with the addition of a `direction` `<select>` and the always-rendered `unit_cost` input + reason marked HTML-required. Test-ids: `stock-adjust-form`, `stock-adjust-qty-input`, `stock-adjust-direction-input`, `stock-adjust-unit-cost-input`, `stock-adjust-reason-input`, `stock-adjust-note-input`, `stock-adjust-submit`, `stock-adjust-error`. `items_form.html` gains a third `data-testid="stock-adjust-link"` "Adjust →" link in the same `{% if not item.archived_at %}` block as in / out. **Tests** (45 new = 45 integration + 2 e2e legs in the existing movements walk): role enforcement (10) — anon GET / POST 401, pending 403, every active role 200 GET / 303 POST; form rendering (5) — inputs + CSRF + both directions + reason marked required + current_qty / open_value / recent-movements + 404 / 400; validation matrix (16) — qty 4 cases, direction 2 cases, reason 2 cases (incl. whitespace-only), unit_cost on increase 3 cases, zero-cost-on-increase allowed, unit_cost-ignored-on-decrease, unknown 404 + archived 400 on POST, no-audit-on-failed-validation; increase happy path (4) — creates `StockMovement(type=ADJUSTMENT)` + `CostLayer(source=POSITIVE_ADJUSTMENT)` + bumps current_qty, total_cost = qty × unit_cost, audit content (direction=increase, unit_cost, source, received_at present), whitespace strip, flash with `+qty`; decrease happy path (4) — creates movement + consumption rows + decrements current_qty, total_cost = layer-weighted sum, audit content (direction=decrease, no unit_cost / source / received_at), blank-note-becomes-None, flash with `-qty`; insufficient stock on decrease (3) — atomic rollback (no movement / consumption / qty mutation; layer.qty_remaining unchanged), in-form error block + preserved inputs (incl. direction value), no audit on insufficient stock; multi-layer FIFO on decrease (1) — two layers (4 @ 2.00 + 6 @ 3.00, distinct received_at), decrease 7 → consumes 4 from old + 3 from new; total_cost = 4×2 + 3×3 = 17.00; consumption rows ordered correctly; current_qty = 3; adjust link on edit form (2) — visible for active item; hidden for archived. E2E walk extended in-place: after the existing OUT + insufficient-stock legs (current_qty=70 at that point), workshop deep-links to `/adjust`. Leg 1 (increase): qty=20, unit_cost=3.00, reason="found extra" → flash with `+20`, current_qty=90, recent-movements row with reason + 20 + 60. Leg 2 (decrease): qty=15, reason="damaged batch" → flash with `-15`, current_qty=75, recent-movements row. 740 unit+integration (was 695, +45). 10 e2e (unchanged file count; movements walk gained two new legs). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files, unchanged — M4 extends `app/movements.py` in-place). |
| 19 | M3 — Manual stock-out form (qty → consumes layers FIFO → records consumptions + "out" movement) (DoD #3 partial) | `1bf2eeb` | Mirror of M2 wired through `consume_fifo` instead of `record_receipt`. New routes `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/out` + `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/out` mounted on the existing `app/movements.py` router (no new module — helpers all overlap with M2). Same role surface as M2: **Manager + Office + Workshop**. New helper `_render_stock_out_form` factors the GET-and-error rendering paths so the insufficient-stock 400 path doesn't fork. **Insufficient-stock path** is the new shape choice in M3: the engine raises `InsufficientStockError(item_id, requested, available)` *before* any mutation (atomic-on-raise tested in M1); the route catches the error, calls `db.rollback()` to drop the orphan `StockMovement` we added before the engine call, reloads the item via `_get_item_or_404`, and re-renders the form via `_render_stock_out_form` with HTTP 400 + the user's qty / reason / note preserved + an `<p data-testid="stock-out-error" role="alert">Not enough stock: requested {N}, only {M} available.</p>` block above the form. **One carve-out**, not a new pattern: this is the only validation 400 in the route that re-renders; blank / zero / negative / non-numeric qty / archived item / unknown item all still 400 to FastAPI's bare exception page (matches the existing seven-form convention). The carve-out is justified because insufficient-stock is *data-dependent* (changes minute-by-minute as other actors consume) where re-typing the qty is more friction than re-typing a typo. **Engine wiring**: route never touches `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, `cost_layer_consumptions.*`, `movement.total_cost`, or `item.current_qty` — `consume_fifo` is the single owner per M1. **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.out"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after={item_id, qty, total_cost, reason, note}`. **No `unit_cost`** (varies per layer — the layer-weighted sum is captured in `total_cost`). **No `source`** (consumes don't carry one). The layer-level breakdown lives in `cost_layer_consumptions WHERE movement_id = ?` (also append-only-by-engine per M1) — duplicating it in the audit row would re-encode information already durably stored. Decimals stringified via `str(decimal)`; note that `total_cost` carries the column-quantised scale 4 (e.g. `"8.0000"` not `"8"`) because the engine reads `layer.unit_cost` back from `Numeric(14,4)` storage. **Templates**: new `stock_out_form.html` mirroring `stock_in_form.html` but without the unit_cost input, with an "Open value" summary line (`data-testid="item-open-value"`), and an `{% if error %}` error block. `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="stock-out-link"` "Stock out →" link alongside the existing "Stock in →" link, on the same conditions (item not archived). **Tests** (37 new = 37 integration + 1 e2e walk extension): role enforcement matrix (10); form rendering (7) — including a check that `name="unit_cost"` is *not* in the rendered body; validation matrix (7); happy path (5) — single-layer consume verifies movement / consumption / qty_remaining / current_qty + audit shape + flash + decimal-precision-on-`total_cost`; insufficient-stock (4) — including atomic-on-raise (no mutations after rollback), no-layers-at-all → 400, no audit on insufficient stock, exact-balance-consume succeeds; multi-layer FIFO (2) — two-layer span verifies oldest-first ordering + layer-weighted total_cost (4×2 + 3×3 = 17) + recent-movements list reflects both IN and OUT; stock-out link (2). E2E walk extended in-place: workshop deep-links to `/admin/items/{id}/out`, asserts current_qty=100 + open_value contains "250", fills qty=30 / reason="Production run", submits; verifies flash + current_qty=70 + OUT movement-row showing "Production run" / 30 / 75. Second leg: fills qty=1000, submits, asserts the error block + preserved inputs + current_qty unchanged at 70.0000. 695 unit+integration (was 658, +37). 10 e2e (unchanged file count; movements walk gained two legs). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files, unchanged — M3 extends `app/movements.py` in-place). |
| 18 | M2 — Manual stock-in form (qty + unit cost → creates layer + "in" movement) (DoD #3 partial) | `2bed0f4` | First slice that wires M1's cost engine into a route. New `app/movements.py` (mounted under `/admin/items`) with two routes for **Manager + Office + Workshop** — Workshop's first positive-write surface in the app. `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/in` renders a form (qty + unit_cost + reason + note) plus a recent-movements table (last 10, newest first); `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/in` validates, builds a `StockMovement(type=IN)`, flushes, calls `record_receipt(..., source=CostLayerSource.MANUAL_IN)`, writes a `stock_movement.in` audit row, commits, and 303s back with a flash. **Engine ownership maintained**: route never touches `item.current_qty`, `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, or `movement.total_cost` — those are M1's invariants. Caller pattern is exactly what M1 documented: `db.add(movement); db.flush(); record_receipt(...); record_audit(...); db.commit()`. **Validation** (all 400, *before* any DB write): `qty` positive Decimal (blank/zero/negative/non-numeric all reject); `unit_cost` non-negative Decimal (zero allowed for gifted/sample stock); `reason`/`note` stripped, blank → None. Item must exist (404) and be unarchived (400). **`received_at`** defaults to `now()`; the engine supports backdating but no UI surface for it in M2 (deferred to PO5/M4). **Audit shape**: `action="stock_movement.in"`, `entity_type="stock_movement"`, `entity_id=movement.id`, `before=None`, `after={item_id, qty, unit_cost, total_cost, source, reason, note, received_at}`. Decimals stringified via `str(decimal)` to preserve precision through JSON (same convention as I2's custom-field audit). **Templates**: new `stock_in_form.html` (page-shaped, not HTMX — HTMX swap deferred until a second use case appears); recent-movements table with `data-testid="movements-table"` + per-row `data-testid="movement-row"`; empty-state `data-testid="movements-empty"`. `items_form.html` gains a `data-testid="stock-in-link"` visible on the edit form when the item is not archived. **Workshop UX gap (deliberate)**: Workshop can't reach the items list (still 403) so they reach the form by deep-link or QR scan (SC1+). The "Stock in →" link sits on the edit form, which Workshop can't see — but that's fine for M2 because Workshop's primary path will be a scan, not a list-click. Documented in self-critique. **Tests** (39 new = 38 integration + 1 e2e): role enforcement matrix (anonymous 401, pending 403, every active role 200/303); form rendering (inputs/CSRF/current_qty/recent-movements empty + populated); validation matrix (qty 4 cases, unit_cost 3 cases, zero-cost-allowed, unknown 404, archived 400, no audit on failure); happy path (movement + layer fields, current_qty increment, audit shape, flash, decimal precision); multi-receipt (accumulation, ordering, sibling-isolation); stock-in link (visible on active edit form, hidden on archived); e2e walk (workshop deep-links, posts a receipt, sees the qty bump + flash + recent-movements row). 658 unit+integration (was 620, +38). 10 e2e (was 9, +1). ruff + mypy clean (17 source files; +1 — `app/movements.py`). |
| 17 | M1 — `cost_layers` + `cost_layer_consumptions` + `stock_movements` + FIFO consumption engine (DoD #5, #6 prerequisite) | `2cd2c6c` | Pure-logic slice — no routes, no UI, no e2e. New `app/cost_engine.py` exposes `record_receipt`, `consume_fifo`, `open_value`, and `InsufficientStockError`. The engine is the **single owner** of `item.current_qty`, `cost_layers.qty_remaining`, and `stock_movements.total_cost` going forward — every M2-M6 / PO5 / ST-series route must call into it rather than mutate those columns directly. New ORM models in `app/models.py`: `StockMovement` (with `MovementType` enum: in/out/adjustment/transfer), `CostLayer` (with `CostLayerSource` enum: po_receipt/manual_in/positive_adjustment), `CostLayerConsumption`. Migration `0010_create_cost_layers_and_movements.py` adds the three tables in dependency order (movements first, then layers, then consumptions — consumptions FK into both layers and movements). **Schema deviation from MISSION §6**: `stock_movements.po_id` and `stock_take_id` are plain `Integer` columns with **no FK constraint** in M1, because `purchase_orders` and `stock_takes` don't exist yet. The FK constraint is a one-line addition in PO2 / ST1's migrations. Documented on the model. **No `archived_at` on any of the three new tables**: they're append-only by mission (§3 "Cost layer history is part of the audit trail and cannot be edited. Corrections are made via new movements"). The engine *does* mutate `cost_layers.qty_remaining` on consumption — that's the one exception, called out in the model docstring (running balance vs frozen received qty). **No DB-level immutability triggers** on the new tables (the audit-log triggers stay scoped to `audit_log` only): the engine is the only writer, and the route layer (M2+) won't expose UPDATE/DELETE paths. If a future "data correction" backdoor is ever added, that's the time to add triggers. **Engine contract**: caller creates the `StockMovement` row, flushes for id, then calls into the engine. Engine takes the movement as input (rather than constructing one) so the route layer owns actor / reason / type / note / po_id wiring. Engine sets `movement.total_cost` as a side effect. Engine raises `ValueError` for input violations (qty <= 0, unit_cost < 0, unflushed movement) and `InsufficientStockError(item_id, requested, available)` for over-consume. **Atomicity** of `consume_fifo`: the available-qty pre-check happens before any mutation, so a raise leaves the DB exactly as it was — verified by `test_over_consumption_is_atomic`. **FIFO order**: `(received_at ASC, id ASC)` — backdated receipts can land ahead of existing layers (e.g. invoice arrives late), and ties broken deterministically by id (verified by `test_ties_broken_by_id_when_received_at_identical` using `freezegun` to force the timestamp tie). The composite `ix_cost_layers_item_received` covers the ORDER BY. **`current_qty` ownership**: chosen "engine owns it" over "route owns it". The alternative invites every M2/M3/M4 route to redo the same arithmetic and risk drift. The engine is the only place that can compute `total_cost` correctly (FIFO sum), so co-locating `current_qty` mutation makes the inventory-level invariant atomic with the cost-level one. The I3 self-critique entry "current_qty not updated when units are created or archived" is now fixable — the M2 route for unique-tracked stock-in will go through the engine and bump `current_qty`. The historical wart (existing unique items show 0 until a movement fires) is still present; a one-shot backfill is M2's call. **No audit-log writes inside the engine**: that's the route's responsibility. The engine is below the audit layer. **No route-level integration in M1**: M2-M6 each add a route that wraps the engine. **Tests**: 44 new (15 in `tests/unit/test_cost_models.py` covering defaults + enum round-trips + FK NOT NULLs + qty_remaining-vs-qty_received independence + plain-Integer po_id/stock_take_id; 29 in `tests/unit/test_cost_engine.py` covering: receipt 9 (correct fields, current_qty increment, total_cost set, received_at default + override, qty/unit_cost validation matrix incl. zero-cost-allowed, unflushed-movement-rejected); single-layer consume 2 (partial + exact); multi-layer consume 3 (FIFO across two layers, exhausted-layer skipped, id-tiebreak with frozen time); errors 6 (over-consume raises with carried-fields, atomic-on-raise, no-layers-at-all, zero qty, negative qty, unflushed movement); isolation 1 (sibling items don't cross-consume); open_value 6 (empty, single, multi-source-sum, exhausted-excluded, partial-included, isolation); combined 2 (full receipt→consume→receipt→consume cycle + positive→negative adjustment cycle). 620 unit+integration (was 576, +44). 9 e2e unchanged. ruff + mypy clean (16 source files; +1 — `app/cost_engine.py`). Migration applies upgrade + downgrade cleanly against a fresh SQLite DB. |
| 16 | I3a — Admin items e2e walk → tick DoD #2 (DoD #2) | `b6a1295` | Tests-only slice, zero app code change. Adds `tests/e2e/test_items_e2e.py::test_admin_creates_qty_then_unique_item_end_to_end` (a second test function, not an extension of the manager walk — keeping the walks independent so a failure points at the right role path; per the S5 self-critique that recommended new functions over more steps once the manager walk hit ~290 lines). The new walk: signs the seed admin in via `_dev_login` (bootstrap promotion is one-shot per session DB but idempotent thereafter, same defensive pattern the manager walk uses); admin creates a category named `Admin Items E2E Cat` (distinct from the manager walk's `Items E2E Cat` to avoid a row clash on the shared session DB); clicks the `nav-items` link to land on the empty items list (the manager walk's cleanup leaves it empty); opens the new-item form *without* a `?node_id=` deep-link (exercises the unfiltered new-item path the manager walk skips, since the manager walk needs the `?node_id=` to render its required custom-field block); fills core fields and picks `Admin Items E2E Cat` from the category dropdown; submits as `AD-E2E-001` qty-tracked with unit `ea`; verifies flash + row + category-cell text; re-opens the item, flips `tracking_mode` to `unique`, follows `manage-units`, creates `AD-SN-001`; cleanup archives the unit, the item, and the category. Why a *separate* walk and not one extending the manager walk: the manager walk is already 290 lines / 12 narrative steps and the S5 critique flagged the readability cost. A second short walk (~110 lines) keeps each function focused on one role's path and makes a future failure attribution trivial. Why no custom-field defs on the admin's category: that's the manager walk's job (I2 already covers required text + optional select); doubling it on the admin path adds nothing the integration suite doesn't already cover (28 tests in `TestItemCustomFieldsCreate`/`Edit`). 9 e2e passing (was 8, +1). 576 unit+integration unchanged. ruff + mypy clean (15 source files, no app code change). DoD #2 ticked: backed by ~100 integration tests across the items + item-units + field-defs routers AND two end-to-end walks (manager + admin). |
| 15 | I3 — Items: unique-tracked per-unit rows (`item_units` table + serial / label + status) (DoD #2) | `3e76b07` | New `app/item_units.py` router (mounted alongside `app/items.py` at `/admin/items`) with seven URL patterns: `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/units?[show=…]` (parent-scoped list with active/archived tabs + `qty-tracked` note + `item-archived` note), `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/units/new` (Manager-only form, 400 on qty-tracked or archived-item), `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/units` (create), `GET /admin/items/units/{unit_id}/edit` (flat-by-id edit form), `POST /admin/items/units/{unit_id}` (update with sparse diff), `POST /admin/items/units/{unit_id}/{archive,unarchive}` (Manager-only, idempotent — no audit row on no-op). Migration `0009_create_item_units.py` adds the `item_units` table per MISSION §6: `id, item_id (FK RESTRICT, NOT NULL), serial_or_label (String 128), status (String 16, SAEnum-backed), location_id (FK RESTRICT nullable), archived_at, created_at, updated_at`. Four indexes: `uq_item_units_item_serial` UNIQUE on `(item_id, serial_or_label)` (per-item — different items can share a label; archive doesn't free the serial — Supplier/Location/SKU convention), plus `ix_*` per-FK + per-archived. New `ItemUnitStatus` `StrEnum` with two values: `AVAILABLE` (the normal in-the-workshop state) and `LOST` (presumed missing but not yet retired). **Deviation from MISSION §6**: §6's sketch lists `status` on the table but doesn't enumerate values; "checked out" is intentionally NOT a status here — it's derived from the (future) `checkouts` table, so the enum stays small + orthogonal. Documented in the model docstring. **Validation** (all 400 on failure): `serial_or_label` non-empty after strip; per-item unique across active+archived; item must exist (404) and have `tracking_mode == UNIQUE` (else 400 with "this item is qty-tracked; switch its tracking mode to 'unique' to manage individual units"); item must not be archived on create (existing units still editable — cleanup vs new structure, S4 posture); `status` parses to enum (defaults to `available` when blank); `location_id` blank → None, otherwise must be active OR match the unit's existing assignment if archived (preservation contract from I1b, threaded via `current_location_id` kwarg on `_resolve_optional_location`). **`current_qty` is intentionally NOT updated by this slice** — for unique-tracked items, the count would be `COUNT(active+available units)`, but plumbing it today would bake in a contract that the M1+ stock-movements engine will want to own. Acceptable wart: `item.current_qty` shows 0 for unique items until M1 lands; flagged in self-critique. **Audit vocabulary**: `item_unit.{created,updated,archived,unarchived}`. Created `after_json` carries `{item_id, serial_or_label, status, location_id}`. Updated uses the sparse `_diff` pattern from items.py (only changed fields appear in `before/after`). **Templates**: new `item_units_list.html` (per-parent active/archived tabs with `qty-tracked` and `item-archived` notes that hide the New CTA conditionally; columns: serial / status / location / created date), new `item_unit_form.html` (serial input + status `<select>` + location `<select>` with the I1b-style "(archived)" suffix on the assigned-but-archived row); `items_form.html` gains a "Manage units" link visible only when editing a saved unique-tracked item (test-id `manage-units`). 67 new tests: `tests/unit/test_item_units.py` (9 — model defaults + status enum round-trip + location FK round-trip + per-item serial uniqueness across active+archived + two items can share a serial + sibling units coexist on the same item + item_id/serial_or_label NOT NULL); `tests/integration/test_item_units_routes.py` (57 across 5 classes — `TestUnitsRoleEnforcement` 12 incl. `test_admin_create_is_303` for DoD #2; `TestUnitsList` 9 incl. qty-tracked-note + archived-item-note + location-name + ordering-by-serial; `TestUnitCreate` 18 incl. happy + lost-status + location + whitespace + audit row + blank-serial + dup-serial-spans-archived + two-items-same-serial-ok + qty-tracked-400 + archived-item-400 + bad-status-400 + unknown/archived-location-400 + no-audit-on-failure; `TestUnitEdit` 9 incl. happy + sparse-audit + no-op-no-audit + dup-serial + archived-location-preserved + switch-to-different-archived-location-400 + clear-location + form-lists-archived-location-with-suffix; `TestUnitArchive` 7 incl. archive idempotent + unarchive idempotent + 404s + archive-under-archived-item-still-works); plus 1 new `test_admin_can_create_item` in `tests/integration/test_items_routes.py::TestRoleEnforcement` (DoD #2 explicit). E2E walk extended in-place: after the unarchive step, manager flips the item's tracking mode to `unique`, follows the `Manage units` link from the edit form, creates `SN-001` and `SN-002`, archives `SN-001`, switches to the archived tab to confirm. 576 unit+integration + 8 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (15 source files; `app/item_units.py` added). |
| 14 | I2 — Item custom field values rendered + validated from the leaf node's schema (DoD #2) | `eba6b60` | New `ItemFieldValue` model + migration `0008_create_item_field_values.py` (table per MISSION §6: `item_id` + `field_def_id` FKs RESTRICT, sparse `value_text` / `value_number` / `value_decimal` / `value_date` / `value_bool` / `value_json` columns + timestamps; three indexes — `uq_item_field_values_item_field_def` UNIQUE on `(item_id, field_def_id)` so the "one row per (item, field def)" invariant is a storage guarantee, plus `ix_*` per-FK). `app/items.py` gains nine helpers (`_get_active_field_defs`, `_VALUE_COLUMN` mapping, `_parse_custom_field` per-type coercion, `_is_set` predicate, `_collect_custom_fields`, `_audit_friendly`, `_persist_custom_field_values`, `_load_custom_field_value_rows`, `_diff_and_apply_custom_fields`, `_form_for_custom_fields`) and converts `create_item` + `update_item` to `async def` so they can `await request.form()` to pick up dynamic `cf_*` keys (the typed `Form()` params for core fields still work — FastAPI caches the form). Per-type behaviour: text → `value_text` blank→None; number → `value_number` blank→None, non-int→400; decimal → `value_decimal` blank→None, non-numeric→400; date → `value_date` blank→None, non-ISO→400; boolean → `value_bool` always definite (checkbox present→True, absent→False); select → `value_text` with the chosen option as-is, blank→None, out-of-options→400; multiselect → `value_json` list, empty→None, partial-out-of-options→400. **Required-flag enforcement:** non-blank for everything except boolean, where required=True means "must be checked" (an unchecked required boolean is meaningless otherwise — documented in the route docstring). **Audit shape:** create's `after_json["custom_fields"]` is a `key→audit-friendly` dict (decimals stringified, dates ISO-formatted, lists pass through); update's `before/after["custom_fields"]` is sparse — only changed keys appear, with `None` for newly-set / cleared values. **Archived field defs:** the form silently omits them (only active defs are iterated, both in render and parse), so existing values for archived defs are preserved on the item but invisible — matches MISSION §3 "Deleting a field hides it from new entry but preserves the value in audit history". **Atomicity:** required-flag and type-coercion 400s short-circuit before any item write — tests verify the existing item state is untouched after a 400 on edit. **Office** can edit custom field values (editing custom fields is part of editing the item, which Office can do per I1b). **Templates:** `items_form.html` gains a `{% if field_defs %}<fieldset data-testid="item-custom-fields">` block after the core fields with a per-type input dispatch — text → `<input type="text">`; number → `<input type="number">`; decimal → text+`inputmode="decimal"`; date → `<input type="date">`; boolean → `<input type="checkbox">`; select → `<select>` with blank "— pick one —" option; multiselect → `<select multiple>`. Inputs name `cf_<key>` and prefill from `form.custom[key]`. Required marker is the same `<span aria-hidden="true">*</span>` pattern as core fields. Test-ids: `item-custom-fields` (fieldset), `item-cf-row` per row (with `data-cf-key`), `item-cf-<key>-input` per input. 38 new tests (10 unit `test_item_field_values.py` covering model defaults + per-type round-trip + `(item_id, field_def_id)` uniqueness + sibling rows on different items + sibling rows of different types on the same item; 18 integration in `TestItemCustomFieldsCreate` covering form rendering of active+omission of archived+section-omitted-when-no-defs, persistence per type, blank-optional-no-row, missing-required-text 400, required-boolean-unchecked 400, bad-number/date 400, select-not-in-options 400, multiselect-partial-invalid 400, archived-def submission silently ignored; 10 in `TestItemCustomFieldsEdit` covering edit-form-prefill, archived-defs-not-rendered-on-edit, set-value writes row + sparse audit before=None, change-value diffs only that key, clear-value deletes row + sparse audit after=None, no-change writes no audit, required-blank 400 + existing row preserved, archived-def value not touched on edit, Office can edit custom field values). E2E walk extended in-place: after creating "Items E2E Cat", manager creates a required text "Alloy" + an optional select "Karat" with options 9/14/18; new-item form is opened with `?node_id=<cat_id>` to render the custom-field block; both fields filled at creation; after redirect, the edit form is reopened and the custom-field values are asserted to round-trip via `to_have_value`. 509 unit+integration + 8 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (14 source files unchanged). |
| 13 | I1b — Office role on items + archived-FK preservation on item edit (DoD #2, #9) | `f1aac23` | Splits the items-route role guards: list (`GET /admin/items`) and edit (`GET /admin/items/{id}/edit`, `POST /admin/items/{id}`) now accept **Manager + Office**, while create (`GET/POST /admin/items[/new]`) and archive/unarchive (`POST /admin/items/{id}/{archive,unarchive}`) stay Manager-only. New `_can_edit_thresholds(user)` predicate (returns True for Manager + Admin); used in `update_item` to override Office-submitted `reorder_threshold` / `reorder_qty` with the row's existing values *before* `_normalise` runs (so Office tampering can't even fail validation, can't make the audit diff include those fields, can't move the row), and in `edit_item_form` to drive the template's `can_edit_thresholds` flag (Manager + Admin see editable inputs; Office sees a read-only `<span>` showing the existing value with "(Manager-only)" suffix, mirroring the `current_qty` pattern). `items_list.html` gains `can_create` / `can_archive` flags from the route — the New Item CTA and per-row Archive/Unarchive buttons hide for Office; the Edit link still shows. `base.html` extends the Items nav link to Office (separate `{% if %}` block from Suppliers/Locations/Taxonomy so a future "Workshop read-only" addition won't reshape the existing block). **Archived-FK preservation** — fixes I1a's silent-data-loss wart. The three resolvers `_resolve_optional_supplier`, `_resolve_optional_location`, `_resolve_leaf_node` now take a `current_id` kwarg; the archived-FK rejection contract changes from "reject any archived FK" to "reject *switches to* archived FKs; accept the existing archived FK when unchanged". `_normalise` threads `current_node_id` / `current_supplier_id` / `current_location_id` through to those resolvers; `update_item` passes the existing item's FK ids. Blank submissions on optional FKs still clear the link (explicit user action; explicit clear is not silent data loss). The form-side helpers `_supplier_options` / `_location_options` change return shape from `list[Supplier|Location]` → `list[dict[str, Any]]` (`id` + `label`), and append the assigned-but-archived row with "(archived)" suffix if it's not already in the active list. `_leaf_options` likewise appends the archived leaf as a plain selectable option (not a disabled group label) — for sub-cat leaves, the label is `"Parent / Sub (archived)"`. Archived rows that aren't the current assignment are *not* listed (dropdown doesn't become a graveyard). 23 new integration tests: 4 in `TestRoleEnforcement` (`test_office_get_list_is_200`, `test_office_get_new_form_is_403`, `test_office_create_is_403`, `test_office_archive_is_403`, `test_office_unarchive_is_403`); 7 in new `TestOfficeEdit` class (form GET, threshold-input-hidden, threshold-input-shown-for-Manager-sanity, non-threshold-edit-succeeds, threshold-change-silently-ignored, threshold-mixed-with-real-change-only-real-in-audit, list hides New/Archive buttons); 12 in new `TestArchivedFKPreservation` class (form lists archived supplier / location / leaf / sub-cat-leaf with "(archived)" suffix, POST keeps unchanged archived supplier / location / leaf, POST rejects switch to different archived supplier / leaf, blank submission on archived FK clears it explicitly with audit diff, archived non-assigned rows hidden from dropdown, new-item form lists no archived FKs). 1 existing layout test flipped: `test_office_nav_excludes_items_link` → `test_office_nav_includes_items_link`. 471 unit+integration + 8 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (14 source files unchanged). No new e2e walk for Office editing — would re-walk the role-promotion flow already covered, marginal value over integration coverage. |
| 12 | I1a — Item core CRUD against a chosen leaf node (Manager-owned) | `07cf0a7` | New `app/items.py` router (Manager-owned, mounted at `/admin/items`). Seven URL patterns: `GET /admin/items[?show=…&node_id=…]` (list with active/archived tabs + optional node filter; columns: SKU, Name, Category-as-`Parent / Leaf`, Unit, Tracking, Qty, Threshold), `GET /admin/items/new[?node_id=…]` (form, pre-selects category from query string), `POST /admin/items` (create), `GET /admin/items/{item_id}/edit` + `POST /admin/items/{item_id}` (edit/update by id, flat URL), `POST /admin/items/{item_id}/archive` + `/unarchive` (idempotent — no audit row on no-op). Migration `0007_create_items.py` adds the `items` table per MISSION §6: `id, sku (String 64), name (String 255), taxonomy_node_id (FK RESTRICT, NOT NULL), unit (String 32), tracking_mode (String 16, SAEnum-backed), requires_checkout (bool default False), current_qty / reorder_threshold / reorder_qty (Numeric(14,4) default 0), supplier_id (FK RESTRICT nullable), location_id (FK RESTRICT nullable), qr_code (String 128 nullable), notes (String 2000 nullable), archived_at, created_at, updated_at`. Six indexes: four FK/archive ix's, `uq_items_sku` unique across active+archived (same convention as Supplier names), `uq_items_qr_code` partial unique WHERE `qr_code IS NOT NULL` (so multiple items with no printed label are fine). All indexes also declared in `Item.__table_args__` so unit-test `Base.metadata.create_all` picks them up. New `TrackingMode` `StrEnum` (`qty`, `unique`) stored with `values_callable` per the Role/UserStatus convention. **Leaf invariant** (reused from S5, duplicated as `_is_leaf` private helper to keep the module dependency graph one-way; one-line predicate, two callers): `_resolve_leaf_node` 400s on missing/archived/non-leaf node ids — both top-level-with-active-children and the leaf rule. Sub-cats are always leaves; tested. **Validations**: sku/name/unit non-empty after strip; sku unique across active+archived; tracking_mode parses to enum; reorder_threshold/reorder_qty parse as non-negative `Decimal` (blank → 0; negative → 400; non-numeric → 400); supplier_id/location_id blank → None, otherwise must reference a non-archived row (blank-on-edit-of-existing wart documented in self-critique → I1b). **`current_qty` is read-only**: not part of `_FIELDS`, never written from the form; create pins to 0; edit preserves the existing value. The first stock-in movement (M1+) will own this column. **Audit vocabulary**: `item.{created,updated,archived,unarchived}`. Created `after_json` carries every stored field incl. `current_qty`. Updates use the sparse `_diff` pattern. **Templates**: new `items_list.html` (active/archived tabs + columns + per-row archive/unarchive forms with CSRF) and `items_form.html` (12 fields incl. category `<select>` with disabled "group label" options for non-leaf top-levels + active leaves only, supplier/location `<select>` with blank "— none —" + active rows only, tracking-mode `<select>`, `requires_checkout` checkbox, decimal inputs as `inputmode="decimal"`, read-only `current_qty` display visible only on edit). `base.html` updated: new "Items" nav link visible to Manager + Admin (data-testid `nav-items`, `aria-current` on `/admin/items*`). 64 new tests (`tests/unit/test_items.py` 9: model defaults + decimal round-trip + unique-tracking-mode + sku-uniqueness-spans-archived + qr-uniqueness-when-set + qr-NULL-coexists + qr-uniqueness-spans-archived + taxonomy_node_id required; `tests/integration/test_items_routes.py` 50 across 4 classes — `TestRoleEnforcement` 7 (anon/workshop/office/manager/admin/workshop-create-403/pending-403), `TestItemList` 6 (active default, archived filter, ordering by SKU, node_id filter, new CTA, category label rendering), `TestItemCreate` 22 (full validation matrix incl. happy + optional fields + whitespace + audit row contents + every 400 case + dup-sku-spans-archived + missing/unknown/archived/non-leaf/sub-cat-leaf node + invalid tracking mode + negative/non-numeric threshold + archived FK rejections + qr dup + qr-blank-no-collision + no-audit-on-failure), `TestItemEdit` 9 (form GET, 404, happy, sparse diff, no-op no audit, move-to-leaf, reject-non-leaf, dup-sku, current_qty preserved), `TestItemArchive` 4 (archive idempotent + audit-once, unarchive idempotent + audit-once, archive 404, unarchive 404); 5 new layout tests for `nav-items` per role + aria-current) + new `tests/e2e/test_items_e2e.py` (1 walk: pending manager promoted → creates "Items E2E Cat" → lands on empty Items list → creates RM-E2E-001 with reorder threshold/qty → archive → archived tab → unarchive → cleanup: archives item *and* category so taxonomy_e2e starts with empty active list). 448 unit+integration + 8 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (14 source files; `app/items.py` added). |
| 11 | S5 — Taxonomy: custom field defs per leaf node (Manager-owned) | `48db5e8` | New `app/field_defs.py` router (manager-owned, mounted at `/admin/taxonomy` alongside `app/taxonomy.py`). Seven URL patterns: `GET /admin/taxonomy/{node_id}/fields[?show=…]` (list with active/archived tabs + parent context), `GET /admin/taxonomy/{node_id}/fields/new` (form, 400 on archived/non-leaf node), `POST /admin/taxonomy/{node_id}/fields` (create), `GET /admin/taxonomy/fields/{field_id}/edit` + `POST /admin/taxonomy/fields/{field_id}` (edit/update by field id, flat URL — same shape rationale as S4 sub-cat edit), `POST /admin/taxonomy/fields/{field_id}/archive` + `/unarchive` (the unarchive re-checks the leaf invariant — symmetric with the create-time check). Migration `0006_create_taxonomy_field_defs.py` adds the `taxonomy_field_defs` table per MISSION §6 column list: `id, node_id (FK RESTRICT, NOT NULL), name, key, type, options_json (JSON nullable), required (bool), sort_order, archived_at, created_at, updated_at`. Two unique indexes scope per-node *across active + archived rows* — `uq_taxonomy_field_defs_node_name` on `(node_id, name)` and `uq_taxonomy_field_defs_node_key` on `(node_id, key)` — both also declared in the model `__table_args__` so `Base.metadata.create_all` picks them up for unit tests. New `FieldType` `StrEnum` with seven values (`text, number, decimal, date, boolean, select, multiselect`), stored as `SAEnum(..., values_callable=lambda cls: [e.value for e in cls])` to match the `Role`/`UserStatus` convention (the migration's column is `String(16)` which is what SAEnum boils down to with `native_enum=False`). **Leaf invariant**: a top-level node is a leaf iff it has no *active* children; a sub-category is always a leaf. Field-def creation + unarchive both 400 if the target isn't a leaf or is an archived node. **Cross-cutting guard**: `app/taxonomy.py` now imports `has_active_field_defs` and 400s `GET/POST /admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children/new\|''` when the parent has active field defs (forces the "archive defs first, then add sub-cats" workflow; archived defs do NOT block sub-cat creation — they're history, not schema). **Key auto-derivation** in `_derive_key`: `re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", name.lower()).strip("_")`. Empty after derive → 400. The audit diff records both `name` and `key` when a rename shifts the slug; casing-only renames keep the slug intact and only `name` appears in the diff. **Options validation**: `_parse_options` parses textarea blobs line-by-line (strip + drop empty + reject duplicates). Required for select/multiselect (400 if empty); rejected for other types (400 if any). **Required flag**: HTML checkbox with explicit `value="true"`; absent in form data ⇒ False. **sort_order**: same convention as nodes — blank-on-create steps by 10, blank-on-edit keeps existing. **Audit vocabulary**: `taxonomy_field_def.{created,updated,archived,unarchived}`. **Templates**: new `taxonomy_field_defs_list.html` (active/archived tabs, parent breadcrumb, "non-leaf" + "archived node" notes that hide the CTA when applicable, columns: order/name/type/required/options) and `taxonomy_field_def_form.html` (name + type select + options textarea + required checkbox + sort_order). `taxonomy_list.html` updated to render a per-row "Fields" link only when `n.id in leaf_ids` (server-computed in `list_taxonomy`); `taxonomy_children_list.html` renders the "Fields" link unconditionally on every sub-cat row. 76 new tests (`tests/unit/test_field_defs.py` 10: model defaults + options round-trip + per-node uniqueness for both name + key, both spanning archived; `tests/integration/test_field_defs_routes.py` 66 across 6 classes — `TestFieldDefRoleEnforcement` 7, `TestFieldDefList` 8 (incl. non-leaf-note + archived-node-note), `TestFieldDefCreate` 22 (full validation matrix incl. key auto-derive, key collision via casing rename, options-required-for-select/multiselect, options-rejected-for-non-select, options-with-duplicates, required-checkbox-true/unchecked, blank-sort-step-by-10, leaf invariant, audit content), `TestFieldDefEdit` 9 (rename re-derives slug, casing-only rename keeps key, type+options change, required toggle, blank-sort keeps existing, audit-diff-only-changed, name+key change recorded together, no-op no audit, sibling clash 400), `TestFieldDefArchive` 9 (idempotent archive + audit, idempotent unarchive, unknown 404, leaf-re-check on unarchive, archived-node block on unarchive), `TestSubCategoryCreateGuardedByActiveFieldDefs` 3) + e2e extension (5 new steps after the existing sub-cat walk: create "Karat" select with options 9/14/18 + required, archive, archived tab, unarchive). 384 unit+integration + 7 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (13 source files; `app/field_defs.py` added). |
| 10 | S4 — Taxonomy: sub-categories under a category (Manager-owned) | `cb19de3` | Sub-category routes added to `app/taxonomy.py` alongside the existing top-level routes. URL shapes: `GET /admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children[?show=…]` (per-parent list with active/archived tabs), `GET /admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children/new` (form, scoped by parent), `POST /admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children` (create), and four flat `/sub/{node_id}` routes for edit/update/archive/unarchive. The flat URL on edit/archive/unarchive avoids a "URL parent vs DB parent" mismatch (the handler verifies `parent_id IS NOT NULL` and 404s otherwise; URL parent doesn't have to match the actual parent). New helpers in `app/taxonomy.py`: `_check_child_name_unique(parent_id, name, exclude_id?)` (sibling-scoped 400 friendlier than a DB IntegrityError 500), `_next_child_sort_order(parent_id)` (mirror of `_next_top_sort_order`), `_get_top_level_parent` (loads parent, 404 if missing, 400 if the candidate is itself a sub-cat — the depth-limit guard), `_get_sub_category` (loads node, 404 if missing or top-level). Archived-parent guard: `GET …/children/new` and `POST …/children` both 400 when the parent is archived ("no new structure under archived"); existing sub-cats remain editable + archivable + unarchivable underneath an archived parent (cleanup, not new structure) — explicitly tested. Audit vocabulary unchanged: `taxonomy_node.{created,updated,archived,unarchived}` reused; the `created` row's `after.parent_id` carries the actual parent id for sub-cats (was hard-coded `None` for top-level, kept that way). New template `taxonomy_children_list.html`. `taxonomy_form.html` reused with new optional `parent` and `back_url` context vars: heading and CTA text adapt accordingly, and an "&larr; Back" link points at the right list. Top-level `taxonomy_list.html` gains a "Manage" link per row that drills into `/admin/taxonomy/{n.id}/children`. Route-pattern collisions: none — `/admin/taxonomy/{node_id}/{archive,unarchive,edit}` uses different literals than `/admin/taxonomy/{parent_id}/children[/new]`, and `/admin/taxonomy/sub/{node_id}/…` uses a literal `sub` segment that doesn't match any existing pattern. 46 new integration tests (`TestSubCategoryRoleEnforcement` 5; `TestSubCategoryList` 6; `TestSubCategoryCreate` 16 — happy, whitespace-strip, blank-sort steps-by-10, blank-sort zero on first, empty-name, duplicate-sibling, duplicate-archived-sibling, same-name-different-parent, same-name-as-top-level, unknown-parent 404, depth-violation 400, archived-parent 400, audit-row-with-parent_id, archived-parent-form-GET 400, list-show-archived; `TestSubCategoryEdit` 9; `TestSubCategoryArchive` 10) + e2e extension (after the existing top-level walk, manager clicks "Manage" → drills into Raw Materials' sub-cats → creates "Silver" → archives → tab to archived → unarchive). 308 unit+integration + 7 e2e passing. ruff + mypy clean (12 source files). |
| 9 | S3 — Taxonomy: top-level categories CRUD (Manager-owned) | `981a8aa` | New Manager-only `/admin/taxonomy` server-rendered CRUD: list (active/archived tabs, ordered by `sort_order` then name), new, create, edit, update (with field-level audit diff), archive, unarchive. Migration `0005_create_taxonomy_nodes.py` adds the `taxonomy_nodes` table with self-referencing nullable `parent_id` (ON DELETE RESTRICT — soft-delete only), `sort_order` (int default 0), and two partial unique indexes: `uq_taxonomy_top_name` on `(name)` where `parent_id IS NULL` and `uq_taxonomy_child_name` on `(parent_id, name)` where `parent_id IS NOT NULL`. Both indexes span archived rows (matches Supplier/Location convention: archiving doesn't free the name). The same partial indexes are declared in `TaxonomyNode.__table_args__` so `Base.metadata.create_all` (used by unit tests) creates them too. New `app/taxonomy.py` router with `_FIELDS=("name","sort_order")`, a `_check_top_name_unique` helper that filters by `parent_id IS NULL`, a `_next_top_sort_order` that returns `max(existing) + 10` (or 0 if empty), and a `_normalise` that parses `sort_order` to int (400 on non-integer). Edit-form blank `sort_order` means "leave alone" — never silently resets to 0. Every route guards `node.parent_id is not None` and 404s — sub-cat ids cannot be edited/archived/unarchived through the top-level routes, leaving room for S4 to introduce sub-cat-aware variants. New "Taxonomy" nav link (data-testid `nav-taxonomy`) for Manager + Admin with `aria-current="page"` on `/admin/taxonomy*` paths. 58 new tests (10 unit `_taxonomy` covering top-level uniqueness, sub-cat sibling-scoped uniqueness across archived rows, name required, sort_order default; 38 integration `taxonomy_routes` covering role enforcement, list filters incl. excludes-sub-cats and orders-by-sort-order, create happy + whitespace strip + blank-sort-order steps-by-10 + duplicate name + invalid sort_order + parent_id-form-field-ignored + audit + no-audit-on-failure, edit happy + same-name + clash + empty + blank-sort-keeps-existing + diff-only-changed + no-op-no-audit + 404-on-sub-cat, archive/unarchive idempotency + audit + 404 + 404-on-sub-cat; 5 layout tests for nav-taxonomy visibility per role + aria-current) + 1 new e2e: pending → admin promotes → manager creates "Raw Materials" → archives → tab to archived → unarchive. 262 unit+integration + 7 e2e passing. mypy strict-clean (12 source files). |
| 8 | S2.5 — Extract shared `app.template_env` (drop `init_templates` plumbing) | `139d4f8` | Refactor only — no behaviour change. New `app/template_env.py` exposes a single module-level `templates: Jinja2Templates` pre-loaded with `csrf_context_processor` and a moved-and-renamed `flash_context_processor` (was `_flash_context_processor` in `app/main.py`). `app/main.py` now imports `templates` from there and no longer constructs a `Jinja2Templates` itself; the `suppliers_module.init_templates(templates)` and `locations_module.init_templates(templates)` shim calls are deleted. `app/suppliers.py` and `app/locations.py` drop their per-router `_templates` global, `init_templates()`, and `_t()` helper, and now import `templates` directly. Six rendering call-sites change from `_t().TemplateResponse(...)` to `templates.TemplateResponse(...)`. Net diff: −51 lines across the three routers and `main.py`, +25 lines in the new module. New unit suite `tests/unit/test_template_env.py` (6 tests) pins: `templates` is a `Jinja2Templates`, the search-path includes `app/templates/`, both context processors are registered on the instance, and `flash_context_processor` (a) pops the session entry, (b) returns `{"flash": None}` when there's no flash, (c) returns `{"flash": None}` when there's no session in scope (anonymous paths). All existing tests pass unchanged: 204 unit+integration (was 198, +6), 6 e2e (unchanged). mypy still strict-clean (now 11 source files including `app/template_env.py`). |
| 6 | S1 — Suppliers CRUD (Manager-owned) | `bac73b0` | New Manager-only `/admin/suppliers` server-rendered CRUD: list (active/archived tabs), new, create, edit, update (with field-level audit diff), archive, unarchive. Migration `0003_create_suppliers.py` adds the `suppliers` table (`name` unique). `Supplier` ORM model in `app/models.py`. New `app/suppliers.py` router mounted under the shared `Jinja2Templates` (with CSRF + new flash context processor) via a small `init_templates()` shim. `base.html` gains a **flash region** (`role="status"`, one-shot; `_flash_context_processor` pops `request.session["flash"]`) and a **Suppliers nav link** visible to Manager + Admin (with `aria-current="page"`). 48 new tests (10 unit `_suppliers` + 32 integration `suppliers_routes` + 6 layout for nav-suppliers/flash) + 1 new e2e: pending → admin promotes → manager creates "Acme Wax Co" → archives → tab to archived → unarchive. Idempotent archive/unarchive (no audit row on no-op). Validation: empty/whitespace name = 400, duplicate name = 400 (active and archived names share the namespace by design). 152 unit+integration + 5 e2e passing. |
| 5 | F4 — Base layout, role-aware nav, CSRF, `require_active_user` | `03f51a1` | New `app/csrf.py` with `CSRFMiddleware` (raw ASGI, double-submit cookie, header **or** form-field token, exempt set = `{/auth/google/callback, /auth/_dev-login}`) + `csrf_context_processor` registered on `Jinja2Templates`. New `require_active_user` dependency in `app/auth.py` applied to `/auth/me` (so a disabled user with a still-valid cookie immediately gets 403). Rebuilt `app/templates/base.html`: skip link, focus styles, header with email/status/role + CSRF-protected sign-out form, role-aware primary nav with `aria-current="page"`, HTMX 1.9.12 via CDN with SRI hash. `admin_users.html` forms render the hidden CSRF input. 25 new tests (8 unit `_csrf` + 11 integration `csrf` + 11 integration `layout` + 4 `require_active_user` unit + 2 disabled/pending `/auth/me` integration). Existing admin/audit/auth POST tests threaded with `_csrf(client)` helper. 104 unit/integration + 4 e2e passing. |
| 3 | F2.1 — Admin user-management UI: assign role, activate/disable users | `e80feb6` | `/admin/users` is now an HTML page with per-user role + status forms (POST → 303 redirect, no HTMX yet). New routes `POST /admin/users/{id}/role` + `/status` with server-side guards: admin can't demote themselves, can't disable themselves, can't activate a user with no role. Pending users sort to the top of the list. e2e covers the full round-trip: pending user signs in → admin promotes them → user signs back in and sees the welcome page. 43 unit/integration + 4 e2e passing. |
| 2 | F2 — Google SSO login + pending-state user model + role enum | `b46ee57` | `users` table (Alembic mig), `Role`/`UserStatus`, Authlib OAuth, signed sessions, `require_role`, role-gated `/admin/users`, anon/pending/welcome index, dev/test-only login backdoor for Playwright. Prod-config validator now requires non-default `SECRET_KEY` + Google creds. 27 unit/integration + 3 e2e passing. |
| 1 | F1 — Project skeleton and verification harness | `884cd46` | FastAPI app + `/health`, SQLAlchemy + Alembic wired, pytest + Playwright harness, `make check` green. |

## Self-critique notes (rolling)

*(Carry forward weaknesses noted during iteration self-critiques but not yet addressed. Clear an entry when it gets fixed in a later slice. Don't let this get longer than ~10 items — if it does, something has gone off the rails.)*

**Self-critique notes (rolling) — OAUTH3:**

- **DoD #1 + DoD #10 ticked.** OAUTH3 closes (f) — the final gap — by building a local OAuth stub and running a Playwright walk through the real `/auth/google/login` + `/auth/google/callback` routes in a browser.
- **MISSION §4 check.** Performance: stub endpoints are trivial (no DB, no template rendering); main-path unchanged. Reliability: prod code paths unaffected (stub gated by two flags). Security: stub only mounted when both `APP_ENV=test` AND `OAUTH_STUB_MODE=1`; stub token path CSRF-exempt only when those flags active; STUB_USER contains no real credentials. Usability and accessibility: no UI change. Maintainability: stub is self-contained in one file; auth.py branch is clearly commented.
- **MISSION §9 check.** No tech-stack swap. No JS framework. No password storage. No out-of-scope feature. No audit-log edit. Tests added. DoD declared done on ticked items only. All hard rules respected.
- **Stub always returns a fixed identity (`oauthstub@uc.example`).** Multiple-identity e2e tests (e.g. two different Google accounts signing in) require a parameterised stub. Acceptable for v1 — the existing integration tests cover `upsert_user_from_userinfo` for multiple identities; the Playwright walk is primarily about the OAuth route scaffolding, not the identity matrix.
- **Playwright browser version mismatch (1194 vs 1217).** The previous loop iteration created symlinks to make playwright 1.59 find the installed 1194 binaries. This is an infra drift concern; a future CI job should install matching browsers. Mitigated for now by the symlink approach which produced 18 passing e2e tests.
- **DoD #11 + DoD #12 remain.** DoD #11 (Postgres CI) needs GitHub Actions infra not yet wired. DoD #12 needs P4 (deploy target + Dockerfile). Next slice is P4.
- **Iter-76 count anchor was accurate.** Re-anchored via `git checkout 5136ba4 -- tests/ && uv run pytest --collect-only 2>&1 | tail -1` → `2511 tests collected`. Matches what iter-76's "Current state" claimed (2511 passed + 3 skipped). No stale count drift this iteration.
- **Cross-checked every load-bearing claim against source before writing tests**: `app/auth.py:29-36` (oauth.register only fires when both client_id + client_secret are set — gates the 503 path); `app/auth.py:184-195` (google_login: `oauth.create_client("google")` → 503 if None; calls `authorize_redirect(request, redirect_uri)`); `app/auth.py:191` (`redirect_uri = f"{settings.app_base_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/google/callback"`); `app/auth.py:199-233` (google_callback: same 503 guard; `authorize_access_token` raises `OAuthError` on protocol failure, caught at `:210-214` and re-raised as 400 with `f"OAuth error: {exc.error}"`; `userinfo = token.get("userinfo")` then fallback `userinfo = await google.userinfo(token=token)`; `not userinfo or "sub" not in userinfo or "email" not in userinfo` → 400 with `"OAuth response missing user identity"`); `app/config.py::Settings.app_base_url` (used by the redirect_uri test).
- **Repo health**: ruff clean (no auto-fixes needed); mypy clean (29 source files unchanged — OAUTH1 is tests-only, mypy strict only on `app/`); pytest 2519 passed + 3 skipped (was 2511 + 3 at iter-76 HEAD `5136ba4`; **+8 OAUTH1 tests**); Playwright e2e — `test_auth_e2e.py` + `test_admin_users_e2e.py` re-run (3/3 passing); the rest of the e2e suite (16/16 baseline) unaffected by tests-only addition to `tests/integration/`.
